[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#overview)

Overview
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean’s Admin Audit Logs provide comprehensive visibility into administrative actions performed within your Glean instance. These logs serve as a critical tool for troubleshooting configuration issues, maintaining security compliance, and tracking changes made by both internal admins and Glean support staff.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#troubleshoot-configuration-issues)

Troubleshoot Configuration Issues
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Admin audit logs are essential for diagnosing and resolving configuration problems in your Glean deployment:

Change History Tracking
-----------------------

Review chronological changes to identify when and what modifications were made to your configuration

Root Cause Analysis
-------------------

Quickly identify the source of issues by correlating problems with recent configuration changes

Compliance Reporting
--------------------

Generate reports for security audits, compliance reviews, and regulatory requirements

External Analysis
-----------------

Import data into business intelligence tools or custom analytics platforms

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#audit-log-features)

Audit Log Features
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#coverage)

Coverage

Data Source Configuration

*   Connector setup and modifications
*   Failures during connector setup
*   Connector creation and deletion
*   Crawl schedule changes

System Configuration

*   Feature enablement and disablement
*   Global configuration changes
*   Branding and customization changes

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#advanced-search-and-filtering)

Advanced Search and Filtering

Efficiently locate specific audit entries using powerful search capabilities:

*   **Time Range Filtering**: Narrow down logs to specific dates or time periods
*   **User-Based Filtering**: View actions by specific admins
*   **Action Type Filtering**: Focus on particular types of changes (e.g., data source changes, global configuration changes)
*   **Keyword Search**: Find logs containing specific configuration names or values

Use a combination of filters to quickly identify patterns or investigate specific incidents. For example, filter by “Past week” + “Admin” + “Failures” to review recent failures to setup triggered by an admin.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#export-and-reporting)

Export and Reporting

Export audit log data for external analysis, compliance reporting, and long-term record keeping:

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Access Export Feature

Navigate to [Admin Console](https://app.glean.com/admin/)
 > Settings > Audit Logs

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Apply Filters

Use the filtering options to select the specific logs you want to export

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Export to CSV

Click the “Export to CSV” button to download your filtered audit log data

CSV exports include all available log fields including timestamps, user identities, action descriptions, affected resources, and change details.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#audit-log-retention)

Audit Log Retention
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#default-retention-period)

Default Retention Period

Glean maintains audit logs for a standard retention period to balance storage efficiency with administrative needs. The default retention period of **30 days** ensures you have sufficient historical data for typical troubleshooting and compliance requirements.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#custom-retention-periods)

Custom Retention Periods

If your organization requires a longer audit log retention period for compliance, security, or operational reasons, contact your Glean representative to discuss custom retention options.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#when-to-consider-extended-retention)

When to Consider Extended Retention

*   **Compliance Requirements**: Regulatory or industry standards requiring longer data retention
*   **Security Investigations**: Need for extended historical analysis capabilities
*   **Organizational Policies**: Internal governance requiring longer audit trails
*   **Change Management**: Extended rollback and analysis capabilities

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#how-to-request-retention-changes)

How to Request Retention Changes

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Assess Requirements

Determine your specific retention needs based on compliance, security, and operational requirements

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Contact Glean

Reach out to your Glean Account Manager or [file a ticket](https://support.glean.com/hc/en-us)
 with Glean Support

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Provide Details

Share your retention requirements, justification, and any compliance standards that apply

4

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#)

Implementation

Glean will work with you to implement the appropriate retention period for your needs

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#security-considerations)

Security Considerations
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Restrict audit log access to appropriate admins only
*   Regularly export logs for backup and compliance purposes
*   Monitor for suspicious administrative activity patterns
*   Maintain separate secure storage for exported audit data

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/audit-logs/admin-audit-logs#accessing-audit-logs)

Accessing Audit Logs
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Navigate to [Admin Console](https://app.glean.com/admin/)
 > Settings > [Audit Logs](https://app.glean.com/admin/setup/auditlogs)
 to view, search, and export your audit log data.

Access to audit logs requires appropriate administrative privileges. Contact your Glean admin if you need access to review audit log data.

* * *

For questions about audit log retention, custom compliance requirements, or advanced audit capabilities, contact your Glean Account Manager or [file a ticket](https://support.glean.com/hc/en-us)
 with Glean Support.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

⌘I

# Company Branding - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Customization

Company Branding

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Workspace Name](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#workspace-name)
    
*   [Branding Settings](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#branding-settings)
    
*   [Logo](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#logo)
    
*   [Primary Brand Color](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#primary-brand-color)
    
*   [Navigation Color](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#navigation-color)
    

![banner](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/branding/branding.20240204234730169.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=e91c336fb5ac437992919696991a82f3) You can personalize the Glean UI to match your company’s branding by adjusting:

*   The company name displayed throughout the UI
*   Logo
*   Color theme

You must be assigned the **Admin** or **Super Admin** roles to be able to customize the workspace appearance.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#workspace-name)

Workspace Name
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Workspace name** appears throughout the Glean UI and in placeholder text. For example, “_Search for anything at company\_name_”. This will be set to the name of your company by default. If you require it to be changed, you can do so by navigating to  [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance](https://app.glean.com/admin/appearance), and changing the value of the **Workspace name** field.

We recommend setting this to a shorthand version of your company name, for example: “MCN” instead of “My Company Name LLC”

![The Workspace name is used throughout the Glean UI and should be set to a shorthand version of your company name](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/branding/branding.20240204235617932.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=9832d9dff036d8c0e3990e3d571f2755)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#branding-settings)

Branding Settings
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The branding menu includes all of the customization settings that impact the look and feel of the Glean UI. These settings will apply to all users at a company. To customize the branding and color palette of the Glean interface, navigate to  [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance](https://app.glean.com/admin/appearance), then click on the **Manage branding** button.

![The branding menu allows the customization of the logo and color palette used throughout the Glean UI](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/branding/branding.20240205000129907.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=18c180266c5fe02c8f05d92fba9480d4)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#logo)

Logo

The logo will replace the Glean logo in the upper left-hand corner of the navigation sidebar. It must meet the following criteria:

*   Square aspect ratio
*   Transparent background
*   SVG format

If used, custom logos **must** be uploaded for both light mode and dark mode.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#primary-brand-color)

Primary Brand Color

The primary brand color is the clearest way to bring your brand into a user’s entire Glean experience. It is used for links, monocolored icons, and a wide variety of other visual components throughout Glean.

You are not able to explicitly set the colors used for the Dark theme. These are derived automatically from the primary color set for the Light theme.If you have a specific use case that requires this to be overridden, please contact Glean support.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding#navigation-color)

Navigation Color

This is the color of the left-hand navigation menu, which appears on all Glean pages except for the new tab page.

Glean will display an error if the color combinations selected are too low in contrast for sufficient accessibility.

You are not able to explicitly set the colors used for the Dark theme. These are derived automatically from the primary color set for the Light theme.If you have a specific use case that requires this to be overridden, please contact Glean support.

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Navigation

Customization

Home Page

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)

On this page

*   [Cards](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#cards)
    
*   [Quick Actions](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#quick-actions)
    
*   [Default Wallpaper](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#default-wallpaper)
    
*   [Mentions card](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#mentions-card)
    
*   [Behavior and limits:](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#behavior-and-limits)
    
*   [Supported sources and triggers:](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#supported-sources-and-triggers)
    
*   [Permissions and visibility](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#permissions-and-visibility)
    
*   [Configuration](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#configuration)
    

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/home-page/home-page.20240205094051016.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=b6c36d692f7972bf18629878f83c8440) The home page settings menu allows admins to define the look and feel of the Glean home page for employees at their company. This is the primary place where your employees will interact with Glean across their workflows.

You must be assigned the **Admin** or **Super Admin** roles to be able to customize the workspace appearance.

You can personalize the Home page for all of your employees by adjusting:

*   **Cards:** Cards are the sections that are visible on the Glean home page for each of your users. These can include suggested documents, collections, and people celebrations.

*   **Quick Actions:** Quick actions are the items that are displayed under the **New** button in the top-right corner of the Home and New Tab pages; providing shortcuts for your employees to create new content across all of your apps.
*   **Default wallpapers:** You can set the default wallpaper that is used for both light and dark themes when users first open Glean.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#cards)

Cards
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance > Manage home page > Cards](https://app.glean.com/admin/preview/cards) The _Cards_ menu is where you can customize the widgets (known as _Cards_) that are visible on the Glean home page of your employees. ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/home-page/home-page.20240205095102825.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=8f7fddaaaa7cebf904342e4656038661) There are a total of 6 cards that can be toggled:

*   **Suggested**: Recently and frequently accessed content across all apps.
*   **Recents**: Recently accessed content, with the most recent on top.
*   **Mentions**: Notifies users about relevant conversations, updates, and tasks across integrated platforms like Jira and Google Drive whenever they are tagged, assigned, or referenced.
*   **Trending**: Top content viewed within a teammate’s department.
*   **People Celebrations**: See who’s new and celebrate teammates’ work anniversaries.
*   **Collection**: A list of resources organized by topic, eg: HR Policies, New Hire Resources, etc.
    *   You must first add a collection under [Knowledge > Collections](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/collections)
        .
    *   You can add multiple collections to the home page.
    *   The collections card supports different visibility settings. For example, you might make a “New Hire” collection visible for employees with a tenure under 90 days.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#quick-actions)

Quick Actions
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance > Manage home page > Quick actions](https://app.glean.com/admin/preview/quickActions) Customize the actions that are displayed under the **New** button in the top-right corner of the Home page. ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/home-page/home-page.20240205100220838.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=16905aba2d2063430e9b74398ba5ab78) The actions available are dependent on the datasources that you have connected to Glean. If you would like to add additional links to this menu other than what is available to be selected, please contact Glean support.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#default-wallpaper)

Default Wallpaper
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance > Manage home page > Background](https://app.glean.com/admin/preview/themes) ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/customization/home-page/home-page.20240205094630375.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=71a1fd0671970ac3c0e63d36dd9a92ee) The _Themes_ menu is where you can customize the default background image that is used for all users at your company when they first access the Glean. Users who have set their background from Glean’s library will not have their choices affected by any change in the default background. The images must be in SVG format, or be at least 2400 x 1600px. Larger images will be scaled down, with the initial aspect ratios retained. Different images can be set for light mode and dark mode.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#mentions-card)

Mentions card
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Mentions card aims to streamline the process of staying updated on relevant discussions and tasks, ensuring that you do not miss out on important mentions across your work platforms. When there are new mentions, this card/tab is prioritized on **Home** and if there are no new mentions, the Suggested card is shown instead.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#behavior-and-limits)

Behavior and limits:

*   Mentions remain visible for up to 10 days (240 hours).
*   Up to 50 mentions are retrieved and the card shows up to 5 by default, with an option to view more.
*   Mentions are chronological and is **consolidated** to reduce noise.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#supported-sources-and-triggers)

Supported sources and triggers:

*   Jira integration
    *   You will receive notifications in your Mentions Feed when another user tags you in a comment or the body of a Jira issue, even if the issue is not assigned to you.
    *   Notifications are also sent when someone CC’s you in an issue, assigns an issue to you, or comments on an issue that is assigned to you.
    *   The feature covers instances when someone changes or updates an issue assigned to you. However, it does not include follow-up comments on a Jira comment you are tagged in unless you are tagged again.
*   Google Drive integration
    *   In Google Drive, you are notified when someone assigns a comment to you, @mentions you in a comment, or shares a document with you. Similar to Jira, if you are @mentioned in the body of a document, it is not currently within the scope of what triggers a mention notification.
*   Other supported apps
    *   Mentions can also surface from select connected apps. Current coverage includes Confluence, Google Drive, GitHub, and Jira.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#permissions-and-visibility)

Permissions and visibility

Only items where the user has at least read access will appear.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page#configuration)

Configuration

You can enable or disable the Mentions card by default for your domain or you can manage it as a self‑serve card in **Home page** settings.

# Admin Guide: Glean DNS-based Go Links - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Features

Admin Guide: Glean DNS-based Go Links

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Glean Sign In](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#glean-sign-in)
    
*   [Options Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#options-overview)
    
*   [Use go.glean.com](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#use-go-glean-com)
    
*   [Use Custom Domain](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#use-custom-domain)
    
*   [HTTP-only Solution](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#http-only-solution)
    
*   [HTTPS Solution](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#https-solution)
    
*   [Browser Support](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#browser-support)
    

In addition to using Glean’s Chrome extension to enable Go Links, customers can also use our DNS-based Go Links solution. This enables the use of Go Links on any browser or mobile device (via a fully qualified domain name) without the installation of the Chrome Extension.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#glean-sign-in)

Glean Sign In
===================================================================================================================================

Glean is protected by SSO authentication. If a user has not already signed in to Glean in a given browser, they will be required to do so before accessing the first Go Link they navigate to.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#options-overview)

Options Overview
=========================================================================================================================================

Glean customers can use either **go.glean.com** directly or a custom domain for hosting their Go Links. Here is a task list to enable each solution.

| Domain Name | Example URLs | Tasks |
| --- | --- | --- |
| go.glean.com | `https://go.glean.com/link` | Configure search domain to “glean.com” |
| Custom Domain | `go/link`  <br>`http://go/link`  <br>`https://<custom domain>/link` | Configure search domain to the custom domain  <br>Configure DNS records  <br>Optional: configure Go Links redirections |

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#use-go-glean-com)

Use go.glean.com
=========================================================================================================================================

Glean customers can access their Go Links from any device using our fully qualified domain name (FQDN) **go.glean.com** without any further configuration. When a user types a URL following the format go.glean.com/\[link\] in any browser that they have used to log in to Glean, they will be redirected to the desired target. Further on, Glean customers can configure [search domain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Search_domain)
 on their users’ devices to “**glean.com**”, which allows their users to use the short URLs “go/\[link\]”. In an enterprise environment, search domain can be configured in multiple ways:

*   Configured by the [DHCP Domain Search Option](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3397)
     (consult your vendors’ documentation for how to set this up in your environment)
*   Configured by a desktop management framework like [JAMF](https://www.jamf.com/)
    

Taking JAMF as an example, a JAMF administrator will create or add the below to /Library/Preferences/SystemConfiguration/preferences.plist on Mac:

Copy

    <key>DNS</key>
      <dict>
        <key>SearchDomains</key>
        <array>
          <string>glean.com</string>
        </array>
      </dict>
    

Alternatively, a JAMF administrator can use [a script](https://community.jamf.com/t5/jamf-pro/best-way-to-set-search-domain/td-p/73297)
 that executes /usr/sbin/networksetup to set up search domains.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#use-custom-domain)

Use Custom Domain
===========================================================================================================================================

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#http-only-solution)

HTTP-only Solution
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When preferred, Glean customers can also configure their own domain name for accessing Glean Go Links. You can follow the below simple steps for setting it up:

*   Create your custom domain (e.g. `go.<company>.com`) and add a DNS CNAME record pointing to **go.glean.com**.
*   Add your custom domain to the search domains list as mentioned in the [above section](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f8FzFv2P-GjE096WYr7C93ySOBMtDKjxxxfNn5tyODw/edit#bookmark=kix.u2jijk9rcqhm)
    .

This setup allows Glean customers to use the short URL “go/\[link\]” via HTTP protocol to access Glean Go Links.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#https-solution)

HTTPS Solution
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When using HTTPS protocol is required, you will need to follow the below steps:

*   Set up a load balancer that redirects both HTTP and HTTPS requests from `http(s)://<custom domain>/links` to `https://app.glean.com/go/links`.
*   Create `<custom domain>` DNS name and add a DNS A record with the load balancer IP address.
*   Provision an SSL certificate with `<custom domain>`.
*   Add your custom domain to the search domains list as mentioned in the [above section](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f8FzFv2P-GjE096WYr7C93ySOBMtDKjxxxfNn5tyODw/edit#bookmark=kix.u2jijk9rcqhm)
    .

You can use either a Cloud-based load balancer or any other redirection server to set up the redirection as mentioned above. As a quick example, here is a Terraform configuration snippet on how such redirection can be set up by using a [GCP Load Balancer](https://cloud.google.com/load-balancing/docs/https/setting-up-url-rewrite)
. The full example Terraform configuration will be provided when asked.

Copy

    resource "google_compute_url_map" "go-links" {
      name    = "go-links"
      project = var.project
      default_url_redirect {
        host_redirect          = "app.glean.com"
        https_redirect         = true
        prefix_redirect        = "/go"
        redirect_response_code = "FOUND"
        strip_query            = false
      }
    }
    

We can deploy the load balancer inside the same GCP project used by the Glean application, if you would like.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/admin-guide--glean-dns-based-go-links#browser-support)

Browser Support
=======================================================================================================================================

After the search domain is configured correctly, a short domain name “go” will be expanded to an FQDN (app.glean.com/go/\[link\]) and resolved to an IP address. However, different browsers vary in their support for short links in their address bars. Here is a brief overview of which URLs are supported by the below browsers:

*   FQDN URLs are supported by all browsers.
*   Explicit HTTP URLs ([http://go/\[link](http://go/%5Blink)\
    \]) are supported by all browsers.
*   To use short links without the http(s) prefix on Chrome or Edge, users must enter an explicit HTTP URL ([http://go/\[link](http://go/%5Blink)\
    \]) the first time they access a Go Link, and then can omit the preceding http:// thereafter.
*   On Safari, a user needs to hold the ctrl key after entering a short URL so that the browser parses “go” as a domain name and does not use the Go Link as a web search.
*   Short links without the http(s) prefix are not supported properly by the Firefox browser.

| Address Bar URL | Chrome | Edge | Safari | Firefox |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `https://go.glean.com/linkhttps://<custom domain>/link` | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| `http://go/link` | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| `go/link` | ✅   | ✅   | ✅ (by holding the ctrl key when first entering the Go Link) | becomes a search query |

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[iOS & Android](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/mobile)
[Glean Language Support](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/glean-language-support)

⌘I

Datasource Failure Alerts

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#overview)
    
*   [Datasources Covered](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#datasources-covered)

*   [What happens if, after following the documented instructions, I continue to receive the alert daily?](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#what-happens-if-after-following-the-documented-instructions-i-continue-to-receive-the-alert-daily)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#overview)

Overview
===========================================================================================================

After a datasource is set up, it can fail for a variety of reasons, such as:

*   Credentials expire and need to refreshed on Glean
*   Required plug-in installation failed
*   Domains are no longer valid

Glean is enabling daily Datasource Failure alerts for Slack, O365, Google Drive, Github, so that admins can immediately take action. You can edit who receives these alerts via the alerts page on the admin workspace.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#datasources-covered)

Datasources Covered
=================================================================================================================================

*   Slack (Regular and Enterprise Grid)
*   O365 (OneDrive, Sharepoint)
*   Google Drive
*   GitHub
*   Microsoft Teams
*   Jira
*   Workday

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#what-do-these-alerts-look-like)

What do these alerts look like?
========================================================================================================================================================

The alerts include the name of the datasource that is failing (as specified in your Glean admin workspace), the time stamp where the error occurred, the specific error that triggered the alert, [documentation to fix the error](https://support.glean.com/hc/en-us/sections/21066299704475-Glean-Error-Codes)
, and a link to the datasource in your Glean workspace. ![Datasource Failure Alerts](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=0f6db68488169c2a7392add00f5a69d6)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#is-this-alert-required-if-so-why)

Is this alert required? If so, why?
==============================================================================================================================================================

**Yes, datasource failure alerts are required**. Datasource setup and successful continuous indexing is an essential part of Glean working well for your users. Delays in fixing data sources can result in your corpus in Glean becoming stale and user queries returning old documents. In particular, this is risk with stale permissions, as users may have access to content on Glean that they don’t have access to in their native datasource. If you have further issues when fixing your datasource setup, Glean’s support team can assist you with errors you encounter. If there are data sources that you no longer wish to connect to Glean, you can delete them on Glean’s Admin Workspace.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#why-are-you-sending-alerts-to-those-four-data-sources)

Why are you sending alerts to those four data sources?
======================================================================================================================================================================================================

Slack, O365, Google Drive, and Github are 4 of the most frequently used data sources by Glean customers. We plan to add additional datasource to the alerts in the future.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#how-often-are-these-alerts-sent)

How often are these alerts sent?
==========================================================================================================================================================

Alerts are sent every day to the admins until the datasource setup issue is resolved or the admin has requested that Glean Support deletes the datasource.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#who-are-alerts-sent-to)

Who are alerts sent to?
========================================================================================================================================

Currently, alerts are sent to all admins specified in the Alerts page on workspace. In the future, we will provide the ability to pick recipients for each alert.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#how-do-i-change-who-these-alerts-are-sent-to)

How do I change who these alerts are sent to?
====================================================================================================================================================================================

Recipients for alerts can be changed via the alerts page on Workspace. Simply add or remove the name of admins on this page. ![Alerts Page](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=5636cd99c3ec1975af252f99c52a9d70)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#i-want-to-receive-some-alerts-and-not-others-how-do-i-specify-this)

I want to receive some alerts and not others. How do I specify this?
=================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

On the alerts page, you can edit the audiences that receive the Datasource Failure Alerts, along with other alerts. Click the three-dot button next to the alert audience images to specify which admins should receive each alert. ![Alert Audience Selection](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=ce07e144bbaee2bdf0436555a5a9afba)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/datasource-failure-alerts#what-happens-if-after-following-the-documented-instructions-i-continue-to-receive-the-alert-daily)

What happens if, after following the documented instructions, I continue to receive the alert daily?
================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

If the alert continues after reconnecting, please contact [Glean Technical Support](https://support.glean.com/)
 for further assistance. Please provide the alert notification body contents in the support ticket. If you do not have access to the Glean Support portal, please contact your account CSM to provision access for you.

On this page

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#overview)
    
*   [Configuration](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#configuration)
    
*   [Alert Settings](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#alert-settings)
    
*   [Alert Delivery](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#alert-delivery)
    
*   [Supported Alert Types](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#supported-alert-types)
    
*   [Mandatory Alerts](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#mandatory-alerts)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#overview)

Overview
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Admin alerts notify you of critical events in your Glean instance that could affect performance. These notifications enable rapid response to potential issues, helping you maintain system health and resolve problems efficiently.

All alerts are delivered via email and are enabled by default.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#configuration)

Configuration
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#alert-settings)

Alert Settings

Navigate to [Admin console](https://app.glean.com/admin/)
 > Settings > [Alerts](https://app.glean.com/admin/setup/alerts)
 to manage your alert configuration.

Configuration Options
---------------------

*   Admins can customize recipient lists for each alert type
*   At least one recipient must be specified as alerts are mandatory
*   Alert settings are managed on a per-alert basis

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#alert-delivery)

Alert Delivery

*   Frequency: One email notification per day for each alert until the issue is resolved
*   Sender Address: [noreply@glean.com](mailto:noreply@glean.com)
    
*   Preview: Admins can preview email alert content before enabling

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#supported-alert-types)

Supported Alert Types
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean continuously monitors various performance metrics for your instance. Admin alerts represent a subset of these monitoring capabilities and will expand over time.

*   Note: ‘Failing Credentials’ alerts only flag the following datasources at this time: Slack2, Slack Enterpise Grid,Google Drive, Github, O365 Onedrive, O365 Sharepoint, Jira, Microsoft Teams, and Workday

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts#mandatory-alerts)

Mandatory Alerts

Failing Credentials
-------------------

Triggers when data source credentials fail, potentially affecting search result integrity

*   Initial alert on first detection
*   Daily follow-up alerts after 7 days

Slack Integration Errors
------------------------

Alerts when the Glean application in Slack encounters setup issues

*   Indicates problems with Slack message response capabilities
*   Affects some or all Slack functionality

LLM Provider Errors
-------------------

The Glean Assistant calls your LLM provider’s endpoint (Azure, OpenAI, Google Vertex AI, or Amazon Bedrock). You are receiving the alert because the endpoint has been returning errors. Glean does not have administrative access to your LLM provider, so we need your help to resolve these issues.

All three alerts are mandatory and cannot be disabled to ensure system reliability and optimal performance.

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Alerts

LLM Provider Alerts

On this page

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#overview)
    
*   [What does this mean?](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#what-does-this-mean)
    
*   [How to resolve the errors](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#how-to-resolve-the-errors)
    
*   [Status Code 401/403](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#status-code-401%2F403)
    
*   [Status Code 429](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#status-code-429)
    
*   [Status Code 500](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#status-code-500)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#overview)

Overview
=====================================================================================================

If you have received an email stating that “Endpoint XYZ is running into errors” with a distribution of HTTP status codes, this guide will help you understand what it means and how to address the issue.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#what-does-this-mean)

What does this mean?
============================================================================================================================

The Glean Assistant calls the endpoint mentioned in the email to make requests to your LLM provider (Azure, OpenAI, Google Vertex AI, or Amazon Bedrock). You are receiving the alert because the endpoint has been returning errors. Glean does not have administrative access to your LLM provider, so we need your help to resolve these issues.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#how-to-resolve-the-errors)

How to resolve the errors
=======================================================================================================================================

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#status-code-401/403)

Status Code 401/403
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These status codes usually indicate an authentication issue with the endpoint. Please check that your account with your LLM provider is still active and the [Admin > Setup > Assistant > LLM Provider](https://app.glean.com/admin/setup/gleanassistant)
 settings are correct.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#status-code-429)

Status Code 429
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This status code indicates that the endpoint might be underprovisioned for your traffic. Please refer to [Glean Assistant LLM Providers](https://docs.glean.com/administration/assistant/configuration/supported-llms)
 to check if you are appropriately provisioned. Contact a Glean representative if you believe you are provisioned correctly but are still receiving 429 errors.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/llm-provider-alerts#status-code-500)

Status Code 500
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This status code indicates a server error on the LLM provider’s side. Check if there was any outage or service disruption from the provider:

*   [OpenAI Status](https://status.openai.com/)
    
*   [Azure Status](https://azure.status.microsoft/en-us/status)
    
*   [Google Cloud Status](https://status.cloud.google.com/)
    
*   [AWS Health Dashboard](https://health.aws.amazon.com/health/status)
    

Once the errors are resolved, you will stop receiving the alert emails and no further action is required.

Looking for the original version of this page? You can find the archived version [here](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/archive/help-glean/uncategorized/llm-provider-error-debugging-guide)
.

# Maintenance Window Policy - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#content-area)

Management

Maintenance Window Policy

On this page

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#overview)
    
*   [Maintenance Impact](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#maintenance-impact)
    
*   [Maintenance Settings](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#maintenance-settings)
    
*   [Maintenance Notifications](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#maintenance-notifications)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#overview)

Overview
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a managed SaaS service, Glean automatically updates our services to ensure that the underlying infrastructure is reliable, performant, secure, and up-to-date. Most of these updates are performed while your Glean services are up and running. However, certain software updates like Elasticsearch require a brief service interruption to be completed. These updates are called maintenance. One example is Elasticsearch updates which require the GKE nodes to be restarted, hence they incur a brief downtime.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#maintenance-impact)

Maintenance Impact
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

During a maintenance event, Glean’s Query Endpoint service loses connectivity to the Elasticsearch GKE cluster for up to 90 minutes for large indexes. Customers having small indexes should expect smaller downtime, around 15 minutes on average. Customer users are expected to see a “Temporarily down for maintenance“ page when they use the service during the maintenance window. Glean schedules maintenance no more than once a week. When there is no maintenance event for a week, customers may use the service as normal but there is no availability guarantee during the maintenance window.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#maintenance-settings)

Maintenance Settings
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The default maintenance window is **Saturday 12:00 pm - 01:30 pm UTC**. If there is a need to move your maintenance window to a different time slot, please contact the Glean Technical Services team to customize your maintenance settings.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/maintenance-windows#maintenance-notifications)

Maintenance Notifications
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As Glean is a SaaS service and we use a fixed maintenance schedule, there is no notification about upcoming maintenance sent to our customers currently. We are also working on removing the maintenance window in the near future, which will improve our service’s availability further.

Browser Extension

About the Browser Extension

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205145841087.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=34446b8325ae71174f365055523c1b88) By default, a user must access Glean by explicitly typing `https://app.glean.com` into their browser, or by clicking the Glean tile in your company’s SSO App Library. Both of these options have significant friction to them, and on their own, lead to poor adoption of Glean. The best way to drive Glean adoption at your company is by deploying the browser extension to their corporate devices.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#supported-browsers)

Supported Browsers
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Glean Browser Extension is available for:

[Microsoft Edge\
--------------](https://microsoftedge.microsoft.com/addons/detail/glean/lkhkfgdfpoijiiinpjgcgacdkgjeieak)
[Google Chrome\
-------------](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/glean/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)
[Mozilla Firefox\
---------------](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/glean/)
[Safari\
------](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/glean-for-safari/id6444195239?mt=12)
[Brave\
-----](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/glean/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#extension-features)

Extension Features
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Glean Browser Extension provides a better user experience and creates habits for higher adoption and usage, allowing all of your employees to get the most out of Glean.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#new-tab-page)

New Tab Page

Extension users have an option to use Glean as their new tab page. The Glean homepage will open every time users open a new tab and provides easy access to:

*   Search
*   Knowledge management tools
*   Announcements and resources
*   Intelligent content feed
*   Calendar

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205151219988.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=8b9f92a0eec5e255731b4ced29707184) Open Glean search or Assistant in a sidebar on **any** web page. This allows for a quick search without breaking flow. To open the Glean sidebar without breaking flow:

*   MacOS: **CMD+J**
*   Windows: **CTRL+J**
*   Some company apps that have been connected to Glean, like Google Drive, will have a sticky tab on the right that can also be used to open the Glean sidebar.

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205151231123.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=7685d5cfa9d01215bee66196e6772bd9)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#native-search-replacement-embedded-search)

Native Search Replacement (Embedded Search)

Glean can make searches within other apps better! Clicking or focusing the native search box on certain company-connected apps (like Jira, Confluence, and Google Drive) opens Glean search instantly in a modal dialog where typing continues to get Glean’s more relevant results. The following apps are currently supported:

*   Box
*   Confluence
*   Google Drive
*   Jira
*   Simpplr
*   OneDrive & SharePoint (partial support)

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205151249272.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=84acd37c748c1cf1b59730240d7a7180)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#go-links)

Go Links

Go Links are short, memorable links that redirect to important URLs within your company. For example, you could create `go/401k` for your company’s 401k portal, `go/it-help` for your IT help desk, or `go/sprint-planning` for your Jira project. The Glean browser extension enables the use of these Go Links directly from your browser. Simply type your Go Link in the browser URL bar (e.g., `go/onboarding`), and you will be automatically redirected to the corresponding URL. ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205151257224.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=26736abb269cff8cd3155a1512c6f1b8)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#browser-history-search-results)

Browser History Search Results

For specific workplace apps in which Glean cannot build out a native connector (typically due to a limited API from the app vendor), the Glean extension can be used in place to allow employees to see results and suggestions from these apps; pulled from their browser history. Glean will be able to search across the titles of the pages your employees have visited, but because these are history-based results, users will only be able to view content that they have seen before in these workplace apps. Results from a user’s browser history are completely secure and private to the individual user: the company will not be able to see results from a user’s browser history. ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205153053214.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=47fea425f5ee31a8254c87339739392e)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#url-bar-search)

URL Bar Search

Type `Gl`+`tab` in any Chromium browser to search Glean directly from the URL bar. ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/about.20240205151327997.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=aae1f230089e291f9a7a3502a68a2481)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about#enhanced-telemetry-&-activity-signals)

Enhanced Telemetry & Activity Signals

When using Glean, the extension reports user events back to your Glean tenant so that Glean can provide:

*   **Enhanced search result personalization**: Glean leverages activity data reported by the extension to learn the relevance of a document and its groupings within an application. This allows Glean to deliver a higher degree of personalization than without the browser extension.
*   **Content suggestions**: Activity data obtained from the extension helps Glean learn and proactively suggest content that a user might be interested in at any point in time.
*   **Insights**: The enhanced activity data provides additional analytics available to admins in the Insights dashboard. Glean also uses data captured by the extension (like dwell time) to help improve search rankings.

**What information is sent by the browser extension?**

*   Each event consists of the page URL, title, referring page URL, visit timestamp, dwell time, and the one-way hash encrypted user ID.
*   Events are only reported for the limited set of URLs that belong to workplace apps that are configured to be connected to Glean (including both API connected and browser history enabled). They’re not sent for all domains to which the extension has host permissions.
*   Care is taken to also prevent this activity reporting for personal instances of an app that is also used in the workplace (e.g. Gmail and Google Drive with an account other than the one logged into Glean are excluded).

On this page

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/glean-language-support#capabilities)

Capabilities
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Search** – Results are a subset of all documents written in the same language as the query.
*   **Chat** – Responses match the language of the query, and typically are based on documents written in the same language of the query.
    *   Note that this means Glean can only answer questions based off documents in the same language – for example, an English query that needs knowledge from a Spanish document is not GA.
    *   We do have initial support for the above (asking a question in language X that requires knowledge from language Y) for 2-lingual corpora as early access (🟦).
*   **Summarization** – Summaries are provided in the user interface language, regardless of the source document’s language.

**WARNING:** Because our LLM engines are multilingual, it may appear upon casual testing that Assistant can understand languages not enumerate below – however, this is very different from our end-to-end, core technology actually functioning, so please do not use that to infer that Glean supports that language!

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/glean-language-support#support-matrix)

Support matrix
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

✅ Generally available  
🟦 Early access and welcoming design partners to help battle-test it!

|     | Keyword Search | Semantic Search | Assistant | UI  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| English | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| German | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| Japanese | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |
| French | ✅   | 🟦  | 🟦  | ✅   |
| Spanish | ✅   | 🟦  | 🟦  | ✅   |
| Dutch | ✅   |     | 🟦  | ✅   |

| Chinese (Simplified) | 🟦  |     | 🟦  | ✅   |
| Chinese (Traditional) | 🟦  |     | 🟦  | ✅   |
| Korean | 🟦  |     | 🟦  | ✅   |
| Portuguese | 🟦  |     | 🟦  | ✅   |
| Turkish | 🟦  |     | 🟦  |     |
| Greek | ✅   |     |     | ✅   |

| Croatian | 🟦  |     |     | ✅   |
| Czech | 🟦  |     |     | ✅   |
| Slovak | 🟦  |     |     | ✅   |

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/glean-language-support#glossary)

Glossary

**Keyword Search** – The syntax/grammatical structure of the language is understood by the search stack. Search is functional.  
**Language detection** – The language of the query is understood.  
**Segmentation** – The boundary between words is understood.  
**Stemming** – Concepts such as plurals and verb tenses are understood.  
**Stop words** – Common words such as articles (e.g. a, the) and prepositions (e.g. of, from, in) are ignored. **Semantic Search** – The semantics of the language as used in the particular enterprise context is understood. Search is stronger.  
**Frequency-based term weights** – System understands the relative frequency of all terms (not just stop words) and weighs them appropriately when constructing a result set.  
**Domain-Adapted Vector Search** – a fine-tuned embedding model is used within the larger hybrid search system  
**Acronyms** – Corpus specific acronyms are automatically mined.  
**Synonyms** – Corpus specific synonyms are automatically mined. **Assistant** – Glean Chat has been optimized for the language and in-context learning examples have been provided in the language. Note that as Assistant is reliant on Search through RAG, quality is dependent on how much of the first 2 columns is complete for a given language: keyword Search is a strict requirement, and Semantic Search will improve upon quality. **User Interface** – All end-user facing product surfaces are localized into the given language / region. Note that external help documentation and admin workspace setup are not yet localized.

On this page

*   [CMD+J / CTRL+J Keyboard Shortcut Stops Working](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/troubleshooting#cmd%2Bj-%2F-ctrl%2Bj-keyboard-shortcut-stops-working)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/troubleshooting#cmd+j-/-ctrl+j-keyboard-shortcut-stops-working)

CMD+J / CTRL+J Keyboard Shortcut Stops Working
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sometimes, the Glean shortcut (command + J) suddenly stops working. It could happen due to updates from the Chrome side or maybe due to another extension. To fix this problem, you need to reset the browser shortcut for Glean. To reset the shortcut:

*   Go to _chrome://extensions/shortcuts_ ![Glean Shortcut Settings](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/glean-shortcutcmd-j-is-not-working-in-chrome-browser-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=3fe25b85a430fc8efeb50ed65040b062)
*   Edit ‘Activate the extension’ under the Glean app.

![Glean Extension Shortcut](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/glean-shortcutcmd-j-is-not-working-in-chrome-browser-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=3740a26a65328f9ec7bb44bea9ca6fed)

*   Add cmd + J (if already added, please edit it and enter again).
*   Refresh the page and try.

Navigation

Notifications

Admin Notifications Dashboard

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Notifications](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#notifications)
    
*   [Notification components](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#notification-components)
    
*   [Filter notifications by severity](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#filter-notifications-by-severity)
    
*   [How Dashboard notifications relate to Alerts](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#how-dashboard-notifications-relate-to-alerts)
    

The Admin Console Dashboard includes a Notifications panel that surfaces issues and system messages that require admin attention. This panel provides a centralized view for connector health problems and high-priority alerts. Admins can identify broken or at-risk components immediately upon opening the console. The **Notifications** panel empowers admins by providing immediate clarity and control over data source health. This feature directly addresses the following challenges:

*   **Verified readiness**: Admins now receive clear indicators when a new data source is ready for testing in search and chat, eliminating guesswork after initial connection.
*   **Proactive health monitoring**: Instead of silent failures, Glean surfaces real-time status updates and prescriptive troubleshooting steps. This allows admins to resolve issues independently and quickly.

This streamlined communication reduces reliance on manual support and ensures your Glean deployment remains healthy and high-performing.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#notifications)

Notifications
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Notifications** panel appears below the page title and description. Each row represents a single notification. Glean groups these in a scrollable list ordered by recency.

![Dashboard notification panel](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/gr-uYChBumkih2DV/images/administration/management/alerts/dashboard-notification.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=gr-uYChBumkih2DV&q=85&s=3111297e9826813cc21c9661d9dd124d)

**Figure 1:** Dashboard view with credential alerts.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#notification-components)

Notification components
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each notification card provides a concise and actionable summary:

*   **Title**: Summarizes the issue, such as `Failing credentials: Slack` or `Failing credentials: Freshdesk`.
*   **Description**: Explains the error and its impact, such as degraded search results due to access failures.
*   **Recommended action**: Instructs the admin to review failed checks and follow the linked documentation.
*   **Status and recency**: Indicates how long ago the issue occurred and includes an unread indicator blue color dot for new items.
*   **Fix issues**: Provides a **Fix now** button that navigates directly to the affected data source or configuration page.
*   **Overflow menu**: Allows the admin to **Mark as unread** or **Delete** the notification.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#filter-notifications-by-severity)

Filter notifications by severity
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Notification type** menu allows you to filter the list by severity. You can toggle the following categories independently:

*   **Critical**: Focuses on alerts that directly affect data freshness, search completeness, or core system health.
*   **Warning**: Surfaces urgent issues that do not yet break core functionality.
*   **Info**: Displays informational updates or low-priority system messages.

![Notification type](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/vWXCMfCYhBUC5jJN/images/administration/management/alerts/notification-type.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=vWXCMfCYhBUC5jJN&q=85&s=e576d1e8e6ace3c468562c4c5568d633)

**Figure 2:** Severity filter options.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/notifications/admin-notifications-dashboard#how-dashboard-notifications-relate-to-alerts)

How Dashboard notifications relate to Alerts
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Dashboard notifications are powered by the same health checks and alerting framework that drives email‑based admin alerts and the **Alerts** settings page. Conceptually:

*   The **Dashboard Notifications** panel is the in‑product view: a live list of active issues that impact search quality, data freshness, or connector health.
*   The **Alerts** page (Settings → Alerts) is where admins configure recipients and behavior for email‑based alerts.
*   **Email alerts** remain the push channel; Dashboard notifications are the always‑available, pull‑based view when an admin is already in the console.

If a connector credentials check fails, admins can expect:

*   A notification card on the Dashboard that summarizes the failing data source and links to the relevant troubleshooting article.
*   An email alert (depending on alert configuration) sent to the recipients defined under **Alerts**.

Admin notifications are designed to be the first place admins look when they want to understand the current health of their deployment and quickly drill into the highest‑impact issues. For more information on alerts, see [About Admin Alerts](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/alerts/admin-alerts)
.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[About the Browser Extension](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/about)

# Managing agent access - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Managing Agents

Managing agent access

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Examples of possible configurations](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#examples-of-possible-configurations)
    
*   [Modify default member Agent permissions](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#modify-default-member-agent-permissions)
    
*   [](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#)
    
*   [Add Agent roles to an individual user](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#add-agent-roles-to-an-individual-user)
    
*   [Agent roles and permissions reference](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#agent-roles-and-permissions-reference)
    

Glean implements a comprehensive role and permission scheme for agents by default. However, you may wish to modify these permissions or make exceptions for specific users.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#examples-of-possible-configurations)

Examples of possible configurations

These controls will provide you with a range of options for how broadly you want to enable creation and sharing of agents in your deployment.

| Desired outcome | Configuration |
| --- | --- |
| Let a small set of users create and share agents | Assign the **Agent creator** role to a small set of users Set the default member permissions: **Can create new agents: Off Can share and publish agents: On** |
| Let all users create agents and tightly control which agents are shared to the library | Assign the **Agent moderator** role to a small set of users Set the default member permissions: **Can create new agents: On Can share and publish agents: Off** Agent creators will need to ask an agent moderator to share their agent |
| Let all users create and share agents | Set the default member permissions: **Can create new agents: On Can share and publish agents: On** |

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#modify-default-member-agent-permissions)

Modify default member Agent permissions

By default, Glean enables agent creation for Default Members, but restricts them from sharing. You can change this behavior in the **Default Member permissions** console. **Prerequisites**

*   You must be an Admin.

1.  Navigate to the **Admin Console** **\> Teammates**
2.  Select the **Default Member permissions** button
3.  Enable or disable the desired agent options:
    *   Can create new Agents
    *   Can share and publish Agents

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#add-agent-roles-to-an-individual-user)

Add Agent roles to an individual user

When necessary, you can assign specific Agent roles to individual users. **Prerequisites**

*   You must be an Admin.

1.  Navigate to the **Admin Console** **\> Teammates**
2.  Select the context menu for the user you wish to modify, followed by **Edit roles**
3.  Under **Additional roles**, select the checkboxes for the agent roles you wish to add:
    *   Agent Creator
    *   Agent Moderator
    *   Departmental Agent Moderator (requires selecting at least one department)
4.  Select the **Save** button

If your organization uses [group-based permissions](https://docs.glean.com/administration/identity/roles/group-based-permissions)
, you can also assign Agent Creator and Agent Moderator roles to identity provider groups. Members of those groups will automatically inherit the corresponding agent permissions when they sign in.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-access#agent-roles-and-permissions-reference)

Agent roles and permissions reference

The following table describes the roles that can interact with Agents and their default permissions:

| Role | Permissions |
| --- | --- |
| Default Member | The ability for members to create or share agents will be governed by the Default member permissions. |
| Agent Creator | Allowed to create, edit, and delete their own agents. Capable of viewing anyone’s agent configurations, though editing requires proper permissions. Cannot broadly share/publish if the “Can share and publish agents” default is Off. |
| Action Creator | Create, edit, and delete their own actions. Actions must be enabled and governed by admins before use in agents. |
| Agent Moderator | Create, edit, delete, view, feature, share and publish anyones’s agent, regardless of the creator. They gate which agents reach the library when “share/publish” is restricted. This role includes oversight capabilities to ensure compliance and effective use of agents across the organization. |
| Departmental Agent Moderator | Create, edit, delete, view, share and publish agents created by members of their assigned departments. They have all agent sharing permissions except for the ability to share agents to the entire company. |
| Admin | Holds all permissions including the ability to assign Agent Creator or Agent Moderator roles to any users. Can manage overall agent settings and permissions for the entire organization. |

YesNo

Browser Extension

Deploying the Extension to Managed Devices
