On this page

*   [Managed Devices](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#managed-devices)
    
*   [Post-Install experience](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#post-install-experience)
    
*   [Email Template](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#email-template)
    
*   [Unmanaged Devices](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#unmanaged-devices)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#managed-devices)

Managed Devices
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To facilitate adoption and usage, a company-managed rollout of the Glean Chrome and/or Edge extensions can be pushed to all employees. This means all employees will automatically have Glean’s extension installed on company-managed computers.

*   Users will not be able to disable/uninstall the extension if it is installed in this manner.
*   The user’s New Tab Page will be switched to Glean. Users can disable this in their Glean settings in the Glean UI.

*   Managed Chrome
    
*   Managed Edge
    

1.  Ensure you have or work with someone who has Google Workspace Admin privileges.
2.  Go to [https://admin.google.com/ac/chrome/apps/user](https://admin.google.com/ac/chrome/apps/user)
     ![Admin Console](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=750f190e530805bdbe0ff611a0a0ab70)
3.  Use the left bar to choose the organizational unit you would like to deploy the extension to. Even if this is being rolled out company-wide, it’s good to select a company-wide organizational unit so that the extension will only be pushed on the Chrome profile associated with employees’ work account.
4.  Hover over the + button in the bottom right corner and select “Add from Chrome Web Store”: ![Add Extension](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=d09fd5664c39d0bb39007037b18e19b4)
5.  Search for “Glean” and select the option corresponding to the Glean work extension (the first one pictured here): ![Select Glean Extension](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=25c5ba266d9e1e15568c86608ae205c0)
6.  Hit “Select” on the top right: ![Confirm Selection](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-5.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=4b4fe75b74ce82d059bd55f75693faea)
7.  The extension should appear as below. Click on it to open a right nav bar. ![Extension Details](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-6.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=27a4dfff15df481b9d6a8a9e6375d09a)
8.  In “Policy for extensions” enter: `{ "BaseUrl": { "Value": "https://<YOUR_COMPANY_INSTANCE>-be.glean.com" }}`

Please ask a Glean employee to confirm your company instance. An example is shown below.![Policy Configuration](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-7.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=18e4a5cac5ef00a74c461d1a49609648)

9.  Hit “Save” at the top after entering the “Policy for extensions”: ![Save Policy](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-8.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=1a06dba781d2a1d7c75ca04905d8f349)
10.  Hover over Installation Policy for the extension and choose “Force Install” or “Force Install + pin to browser toolbar”: ![Installation Policy](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-9.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=0fa3117794121ca73f10a981e7ca1abc)
11.  Hit “Save” at the top: ![Final Save](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-10.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=3d2285a6fb3f0e09e026353c162a66e6)

You’re all set!There may be a delay on the Google/Chrome side for when the extension is pushed. Expect all employees to have the extension present within 24-48 hours.

You can use the [ExtensionInstallForceList](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/DeployEdge/microsoft-edge-policies#extensioninstallforcelist)
 policy to install the Glean extension silently.

1.  In the Group Policy Editor, go to **Administrative Templates** > **Microsoft Edge** > **Extensions** > and then select **Control which extensions are installed silently**.
2.  Select **Enabled**.
3.  Click **Show**.
4.  Enter the following ID to install the Glean extension from the Chrome web store:
    
    Copy
    
        cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij;https://clients2.google.com/service/update2/crx
        
    

!!! success You’re all set!The extension is installed silently without user interaction. The user also won’t be able to uninstall or disable the extension. This setting overwrites any blocklist policy that is enabled.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#post-install-experience)

Post-Install experience
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Upon installation of the Glean extension via a managed push, all new users will see the below page to prompt them to log into Glean. Your company name, as set in the Glean Admin settings, will be automatically populated in the popup to the user. ![Onboarding Page](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW/images/administration/management/apps/browser/managed-chrome-extension-rollout-11.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=RMY0t4dKmgK2y8cW&q=85&s=fdfcc097444d6151b38e2b20df48b9c9)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#email-template)

Email Template
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Refer to below email template below for a company-wide communication to inform of a managed Chrome/Edge install.

> **Subject: New browser extension available for all employees**

> _Title: Key updates to your browser_

> In \[company name\]‘s continued effort to support employee well-being and productivity, we are rolling out a new company-wide search tool, [Glean](https://www.glean.com/)
> .

> Using Glean, you can search across all work applications we use at \[company name\]. Whether the document or answer you are looking for lives in \[GDrive, Slack, Email, Jira \*customize with your company’s tools\] or with another colleague, Glean will help you search for and discover what you need to get your work done. All without remembering where all your documents are hosted or messaging multiple people.

> Starting \[launch date\], Glean will be installed on your Chrome browser. Note that this launch will replace your current default new tab page with Glean so that you can easily [access search across all company apps](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/search-from-wherever-you-work)
> .

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/features/browser/deployment#unmanaged-devices)

Unmanaged Devices
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Glean Browser Extension can be directly installed by users manually at the following links:

[Microsoft Edge\
--------------](https://microsoftedge.microsoft.com/addons/detail/glean/lkhkfgdfpoijiiinpjgcgacdkgjeieak)
[Google Chrome\
-------------](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/glean/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)
[Mozilla Firefox\
---------------](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/glean/)
[Safari\
------](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/glean-for-safari/id6444195239?mt=12)
[Brave\
-----](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/glean/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)

Looking for the original version of this page? You can find the archived version [here](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/managing-your-glean-deployment/managed-chrome-extension-rollout)
.

If you are an admin or agent moderator, by default you can see agents that have been created by all users in the agent library, including agents that are not shared broadly. You can access an additional “Shared with” filter that allows you to display only agents that are shared with the entire company or shared with a specific department. The “Shared with” filter is not shown to normal users.

# Manage Featured Agents in the Agent Library - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Managing Agents

Manage Featured Agents in the Agent Library

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [What users see](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#what-users-see)
    
*   [Who can curate Featured agents](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#who-can-curate-featured-agents)
    
*   [Configure Featured agents](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#configure-featured-agents)
    
*   [Tips](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#tips)
    

Featured agents allow Admins and Agent Moderators to curate a single “Featured” category in the Agent Library, promoting high‑impact agents for quick discovery. The Featured section also appears in the chat for users who have access to at least one featured agent.

Changes respect existing permissions; featuring an agent never grants new access.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#what-users-see)

What users see
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

End users see a Featured section in the Agent Library and in chat, but only if they already have access to at least one agent that’s been featured. Featuring does not override an agent’s sharing/permissions. If no eligible agents are featured for a user, the section won’t appear.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#who-can-curate-featured-agents)

Who can curate Featured agents
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Admins and Agent Moderators can create and maintain the Featured category from the Agent Library, including adding and reordering agents. These roles are managed in the Admin Console under agent access and roles.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#configure-featured-agents)

Configure Featured agents
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Prerequisites**You must be an Admin or Agent Moderator.

1.  **Create and manage the Featured category:** Go to the Agent Library. You’ll see controls to create/manage the Featured category and add agents to it.
2.  Add eligible agents. Reorder your featured agents by dragging and dropping to control display order.
3.  Save your changes. Allow up to ~15 minutes for updates to propagate to end users due to client caching.

Changes to Featured agents (e.g., adding/removing or reordering agents) can take up to ~15 minutes to appear for users due to client‑side caching.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/featured-agents#tips)

Tips
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Pin your most broadly useful agents (e.g., onboarding, daily workflows) to maximize visibility.
*   Keep the list concise and periodically review usage to ensure the most helpful agents are featured.

YesNo

# Glean Assistant Auto-Routing to Agents - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Managing Agents

Glean Assistant Auto-Routing to Agents

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#overview)
    
*   [Key Benefits](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#key-benefits)
    
*   [Typical Use Cases](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#typical-use-cases)
    
*   [How It Works](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#how-it-works)
    
*   [Step-by-Step Setup](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#step-by-step-setup)
    
*   [Example Routing Conditions](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#example-routing-conditions)
    
*   [HR Agent](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#hr-agent)
    
*   [IT Help Desk Agent](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#it-help-desk-agent)
    
*   [Best Practices](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#best-practices)
    
*   [Limitations & Known Issues](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#limitations-%26-known-issues)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#overview)

Overview
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean’s Assistant auto-routing to Agents feature enables organizations to deliver faster, more accurate, and specialized responses by automatically directing user questions to custom-built agents. Instead of relying solely on the Glean Assistant’s general knowledge, you can configure the system to route, for example, HR or IT questions to dedicated agents tailored to your company’s policies, workflows, and data sources. This ensures users get authoritative answers and can trigger automated actions without needing to know which agent to use or how to find it.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#key-benefits)

Key Benefits
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Faster, more accurate answers**: Users get responses from the most relevant expert agent, improving quality and speed.
*   **Seamless user experience**: Employees continue to use Glean Assistant as their single entry point. No need to search for the right agent.
*   **Centralized control**: Admins can define and update routing rules to match evolving business needs.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#typical-use-cases)

Typical Use Cases
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **HR**: Route all questions about benefits, PTO, or company policies to an HR agent that references official documentation and can initiate HR workflows.
*   **IT Help Desk**: Direct IT support requests to an IT agent that can troubleshoot, file tickets, or grant access.
*   **Departmental Experts**: Route specialized queries (e.g., “Who should I contact about X?”) to expert finder agents.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#how-it-works)

How It Works
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   When a user submits a question to Glean Assistant, the system evaluates the request against the routing conditions you’ve defined for each agent. If a match is found, the Assistant automatically routes the request to the appropriate agent. The user sees a message such as “Using Agent: \[Agent Name\]” at the top of the response, making the handoff transparent.
*   If multiple agents’ conditions match, the first agent in your configured list is selected. You can reorder agents to set priority.
*   If no agent matches, the Assistant responds as usual.
*   If an agent was mistakenly invoked or the user prefers not to use the agent, the user can “X” out of the agent at the top of the response and have the Assistant regenerate the answer using its general knowledge.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#step-by-step-setup)

Step-by-Step Setup
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Prerequisites**

*   Glean Agents must be enabled and launched for your deployment
*   Admin access to the Glean Admin Console is required to configure routing

1.  **Go to the Admin Console**: Navigate to [**Admin Console → Platform → Agents**](https://app.glean.com/admin/setup/agents)
    .
2.  **Open Assistant Auto-Routing**: Expand the “Assistant auto-routing” section and click **Configure**.
3.  **Add Agents and Routing Conditions**: You can specify up to 15 conversational agents.
    *   For each agent, provide a routing condition - a natural language description (max 500 characters) of the types of requests that should be routed to this agent.
    *   Add example user requests to improve routing accuracy.
4.  **Set Priority Order**: Drag and drop agents to change their order.
5.  **Save Changes**: Click **Save changes** to activate your configuration.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#example-routing-conditions)

Example Routing Conditions
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#hr-agent)

HR Agent

_Route to agent if the user asks about HR policy, benefits, leave (PTO, sick, parental/maternity/paternity), holidays, payroll/tax docs (W‑2, paystubs), employment verification, or reports a sensitive concern (harassment, discrimination). Do not route if it’s a login, MFA, SSO, or access issue to an HR/benefits system._ Example requests:

*   “How do I request a PTO?”
*   “What holidays do we get off?”
*   “How do I enroll in benefits?”
*   “What is the process for maternity leave?”
*   “How do I update my direct deposit?”
*   “Where can I find my W‑2?”
*   “I need an employment verification letter.”
*   “How do I report harassment?”

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#it-help-desk-agent)

IT Help Desk Agent

_Route to agent if the user needs help with accounts or access (password reset, MFA, SSO/Okta), devices/hardware (laptop, mouse, keyboard, monitor, dock), software installs/licenses, network/VPN/Wi‑Fi, email/calendar/chat tools, printers, or provisioning/access to applications (including the HR/benefits portal)._ Example requests:

*   “How do I reset my password?”
*   “How do I request a new laptop?”
*   “My mouse isn’t working.”
*   “I can’t log in to the benefits portal — my MFA code is invalid.”
*   “Outlook isn’t showing company holidays.”
*   “Please grant me access to Zoom Pro and Figma.”
*   “VPN keeps disconnecting when I’m on hotel Wi‑Fi.”

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#best-practices)

Best Practices
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Be specific in routing conditions**: Use clear, concise language and provide several example requests for each agent.
*   **Review and update regularly**: As your organization’s needs change, revisit your routing rules and agent list.
*   **Test with real queries**: Try common user questions to ensure they’re routed as expected.
*   **Limit to 15 agents**: Prioritize the most impactful use cases for routing.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/agent-routing#limitations-&-known-issues)

Limitations & Known Issues
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Routing is not 100% accurate: Quality depends on the clarity of your routing instructions and examples.
*   Only conversational agents viewable by everyone can be routed to.
*   If an agent is removed or made private, it will be excluded from routing.
*   Currently, only agents with chat-message triggers are supported for routing. Expansion to input-form types is planned.
*   Routing applies to all users in your deployment; limiting to a subset (e.g., pilot group) is not currently supported.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

# How an App is translated to an Agent - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Managing Agents

How an App is translated to an Agent

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)

[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Building blocks of an AI Application:](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#building-blocks-of-an-ai-application)
    
*   [Additional Migration Details:](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#additional-migration-details)
    
*   [Example: Sample Agent (migrated from an App)](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#example-sample-agent-migrated-from-an-app)
    

When migrating from Apps to Agents, each App component maps directly to its Agent equivalent to ensure seamless functionality.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#building-blocks-of-an-ai-application)

Building blocks of an AI Application:

1.  Name
    *   App Display Name → Agent Name
    *   Direct one-to-one mapping with no changes
2.  Icon
    *   App Icon → Agent Icon
    *   Visual representation remains identical
3.  Description
    *   App Description → Agent Description
4.  Instructions
    *   App Instructions → Instructions for the “Plan & Execute” step in the Agent (see screenshot below)
5.  Actions
    *   App Actions → Actions for the “Plan & Execute” step in the Agent
    *   All configured actions and their customization are preserved as-is.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#additional-migration-details)

Additional Migration Details:

1.  Permissions & Access:
    *   App library audience (Users, Departments) → Agent VIEWER roles
    *   App Editors → Agent EDITOR roles
    *   Creator becomes Agent Owner
2.  Configuration:
    *   Prompts → Converted to conversation starters for the agent.
3.  Integration:
    *   Slack Settings are preserved as-is and can be accessed from the “Share menu” in the Agent builder.  
        ℹ️ The migrated agent will refer to itself as `Agent: <name>` in Slack in its responses instead of `App: <name>`.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/managing-agents/how-an-app-is-translated-to-an-agent#example-sample-agent-migrated-from-an-app)

Example: Sample Agent (migrated from an App)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/apptoagentimage1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=4543c411e160f1dcd67654a3852f790a) ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS/images/apptoagentimage2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=EKmVGUrN4mIUPxpS&q=85&s=81a6fced605caccdba3019935db2332e)

FAQs

##### \[Q\] What happens to bookmarked links to my Application post-migration, such as [https://app.glean.com/chat?applicationId=example\_application](https://app.glean.com/chat?applicationId=example_application)
 ?

*   Once the migration is completed, Glean will seamlessly redirect any navigation from a link to an App to the corresponding Agent, so any previously bookmarked links for Apps will continue to work.

##### \[Q\] What about the Web SDK? I’m using an App with chat embedded on my website.

*   Currently, one can embed an AI App in their site by using the applicationId parameter such as:

Copy

    GleanWebSDK.renderChat(document.getElementById('container'), { 
      applicationId: "example_application" 
    })
    

*   Post-migration, your existing code that is using applicationId with the Web SDK will continue to work as Glean internally translates the applicationId to the corresponding migrated agentId, ensuring your embedded chat functionality remains uninterrupted.

Note:

*   The applicationId parameter will not work for new agents, and agentId should be used instead.
*   We recommend switching to agentId for Web SDK usage of migrated agents once the same is completed.

Copy

    GleanWebSDK.renderChat(document.getElementById('container'), { 
      applicationId: "example_application" // Will continue to work for agents that used to be Apps.
    })
    
    GleanWebSDK.renderChat(document.getElementById('container'), { 
      agentId: "example_application" // (Recommended) Use this for new agents or agents migrated from Apps.
    })

##### \[Q\] I’m using the App via REST API, will the app stop working when called via /rest/api/v1/chat?

*   For Apps that are migrated to an Agent, Glean will internally translate the applicationId parameter in the chat request to the corresponding agentId to ensure functionality remains uninterrupted.

Note:

*   The applicationId parameter will not work for new agents, and agentId should be used instead.
*   We recommend switching to agentId for REST API usage of migrated agents once the same is completed.

Copy

    curl 'https://<your-glean-be>.glean.com/rest/api/v1/chat' \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      -H 'Authorization: Bearer ...' \
      -d '{
            "stream": false,
            "applicationId": "example_application",
            "messages": [\
                {\
                    "author": "USER",\
                    "fragments": [\
                        {\
                            "text": "What are the holidays this year?"\
                        }\
                    ]\
                }\
            ]
        }'
    
    
    curl 'https://<your-glean-be>.glean.com/rest/api/v1/chat' \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      -H 'Authorization: Bearer ...' \
      -d '{
            "stream": false,
            "agentId": "example_application", // For new agents Or agents migrated from Apps
            "messages": [\
                {\
                    "author": "USER",\
                    "fragments": [\
                        {\
                            "text": "What are the holidays this year?"\
                        }\
                    ]\
                }\
            ]
        }'

##### \[Q\] Will my App’s conversation history be preserved?

*   Yes, all existing chat sessions and conversation history will be maintained.

##### \[Q\] What happens to my App’s Slack integration?

*   Your App’s Slack settings and channel mappings will transfer directly to the corresponding Agent. The Agent will maintain the same Slack functionality and channel associations as your original App.

##### \[Q\] Can I continue using my App during the migration process?

*   During the migration phases, your Apps will continue to function normally until it is successful and they are replaced with the corresponding agent.

##### \[Q\] What if I made changes to my App before migration?

*   Any changes made to Apps before the final migration will be preserved in the resulting Agent.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

⌘I

# Model deprecation - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Management

Model deprecation

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Assistant using a deprecated model](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#assistant-using-a-deprecated-model)
    
*   [Glean Universal Model Key](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#glean-universal-model-key)
    
*   [Customer Key](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#customer-key)
    
*   [Agents using a deprecated model](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#agents-using-a-deprecated-model)
    
*   [What needs your attention](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#what-needs-your-attention)
    

Glean periodically deprecates older models as newer, more capable versions become available. This section explains how deprecation works and what actions you may need to take.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#assistant-using-a-deprecated-model)

Assistant using a deprecated model
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#glean-universal-model-key)

Glean Universal Model Key

If you are using Glean Universal Model Key, your assistant model is managed and configured by Glean. Glean ensures your assistant continues to use the latest models available in the market.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#customer-key)

Customer Key

Glean will send you advance notifications when your configured assistant model is going to be deprecated.

| Timeline | Email notifications |
| --- | --- |
| **60 days before deprecation** | Glean will send an email notification to all the administrators regarding the upcoming deprecation of their assistant model. |
| **15 days before deprecation** | In case the admins have not yet migrated to a supported model, Glean sends a reminder email to all admins. |
| **After deprecation** | The model is no longer available for use. If the assistant model is not updated by this time, assistant requests will fail with an error and users will not be able to use the assistant until the model is updated to a supported model. |

In addition to email notifications, you will see a banner in the admin console informing you that your assistant model is being deprecated along with the deprecation date.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#agents-using-a-deprecated-model)

Agents using a deprecated model
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   If any of your agents (or any of the steps within an agent) are using a model that is scheduled for deprecation, you will receive an email notification informing about which agents or steps of the agents are using the deprecated model along with the date of deprecation.
*   Glean will also recommend suitable migration models for your agents, based on the models currently enabled within your deployment.
*   Apart from the email notifications, you will also see a banner in the admin console informing you about the agents using a deprecated model and the date of deprecation.
*   After the deprecation date, if any agents or any steps of the agents are still using the deprecated model, Glean will automatically switch these to the recommended fallback model. This ensures seemless usage of the agents by the users without any disruption.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-deprecation#what-needs-your-attention)

What needs your attention
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Admins:** If you receive an email notification about your assistant model being deprecated or see a banner in the admin console, you must update your assistant model to any newer model as soon as possible to avoid any disruption.
*   **Agent builders:** If you receive an email notification about your agents using a deprecated model or see a banner in the admin console, Glean recommends that you proactively update your agents to a newer model of your choice. If you are unable to do so before the deprecation date, Glean will automatically move affected agents to a suitable fallback model so they continue to run.

*   [Troubleshooting and cautions](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#troubleshooting-and-cautions)
    
*   [Related links](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#related-links)
    

Glean can act as an OAuth 2.1 Authorization Server (issuing access tokens) and as the Resource Server (validating them), while delegating user authentication to your configured Single Sign-On (SSO) Identity Provider (IdP) using OpenID Connect (OIDC) or Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML). **This is currently designed for MCP remote server authentication.** While the underlying infrastructure supports Client API operations, full Client API support requires additional UI components that are planned for future releases. **Glean does not replace your SSO.** When you enable the Glean OAuth Authorization Server, Glean only acts as the authorization server that issues OAuth access tokens. User authentication continues to happen through your existing SSO Identity Provider (Google Workspace, Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, etc.) using the same OIDC or SAML configuration you already have. Glean reuses your existing SSO for authentication and adds OAuth token issuance on top.

The Indexing API uses a separate authentication model with Glean-issued tokens and does not accept OAuth.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#overview)

Overview
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#when-to-choose-this-approach)

When to choose this approach

*   You are connecting MCP host applications to Glean’s remote MCP server. This is the recommended authentication path for MCP and requires enabling the Glean OAuth Authorization Server.
*   You want faster setup without coordinating with your IdP team for new integrations.
*   You want Glean-defined, fine-grained scopes for future Client API integrations (note: full Client API support with UI is coming in a future release).

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#what-glean-does-and-does-not-do)

What Glean does and does not do

*   **Does**: Act as OAuth 2.1 Authorization Server (issue access tokens); define and enforce scopes; validate tokens at the Resource Server.
*   **Does not**: Replace or modify your SSO setup. User authentication continues via your existing SSO IdP (OIDC or SAML). Glean reuses your SSO, it doesn’t reinvent it.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#where-it-applies)

Where it applies

*   **MCP Remote Servers**: Fully supported and recommended. This is the primary use case for the Glean OAuth Authorization Server. Note that host OAuth support may vary by MCP client application.
*   **Client API**: Technically supported for operations like search, chat, and agents, but full support requires additional UI components planned for future releases.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#setup-and-configuration)

Setup and Configuration
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#enabling-the-glean-oauth-authorization-server)

Enabling the Glean OAuth Authorization Server

The Glean OAuth Authorization Server is disabled by default. To enable it, follow the steps below:

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#)

Navigate to OAuth Settings

Go to the Admin Console and navigate to Settings → Third‑party access (OAuth).

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#)

Enable Glean OAuth Authorization Server

![Glean OAuth Authorization Server](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/2iiHF_lD0X4cVbMj/images/oauth-server/glean-oauth.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=2iiHF_lD0X4cVbMj&q=85&s=cedc53eeea29dcd66b7aa24b225a02ac)

Once the Glean OAuth Authorization Server is enabled, you can start using it immediately, without any additional configuration.

Admins who want to restrict which applications can connect via DCR (e.g. MCP host applications) can configure redirect URI restrictions. See [Dynamic Client Registration](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration)
 for details.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#using-oauth-with-mcp-remote-servers)

Using OAuth with MCP Remote Servers

Once enabled, MCP host applications can authenticate using the following flow:

*   The MCP host initiates the Authorization Code flow (opening a browser for IdP login and user consent)
*   The host obtains tokens through the Glean OAuth server
*   The host connects to Remote MCP endpoints using the OAuth token
*   Bearer token fallback: If an MCP host does not support OAuth, a user-scoped bearer token can be used as a fallback (less preferred than OAuth)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#technical-reference)

Technical Reference
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#supported-flows-and-endpoints)

Supported flows and endpoints

*   Authorization Code with Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE).
*   Endpoints: Authorization, Token, Introspection, and OIDC Discovery. These support standard client registration and token acquisition patterns without exposing non-public base URLs.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#scopes-and-permissions)

Scopes and permissions

Glean-defined scopes govern API capabilities. Request the minimum scopes needed for your integration. Examples include `SEARCH`, `CHAT`, `AGENTS`, `MCP`, `DOCUMENTS`, `TOOLS`, and `ENTITIES`. For MCP remote servers, the relevant scopes (particularly `MCP` and related resource scopes) are automatically managed during the OAuth flow. The Indexing API uses a separate authentication model and does not accept OAuth.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#troubleshooting-and-cautions)

Troubleshooting and cautions

*   Ensure the Glean OAuth server is enabled; otherwise token acquisition or validation will fail.
*   Redirect URIs must match exactly; the PKCE verifier must match the original challenge.
*   Do not use OAuth for the Indexing API, which requires Glean-issued tokens.
*   MCP host client authentication failures or unknown client errors: Clear the host’s stored MCP credentials and re-authenticate.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#related-links)

Related links
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Back to Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/oauth-overview)
    
*   [Dynamic Client Registration](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration)
    
*   [IdP-based OAuth](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/oauth-idp)
    
*   [Client API authentication overview](https://developers.glean.com/api-info/client/authentication/overview)
    
*   [OAuth (IdP tokens) developer guide](https://developers.glean.com/api-info/client/authentication/oauth)
    
*   [MCP Remote Server (overview/host configurator)](https://developers.glean.com/guides/mcp/)
    
*   [MCP Server setup (admin enablement context)](https://docs.glean.com/administration/platform/mcp/connecting-to-glean-mcp-server)

# Exclude Models From Glean Agents - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-exclusion#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Management

Exclude Models From Glean Agents

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Glean Universal Model Key (formerly Glean Key)](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-exclusion#glean-universal-model-key-formerly-glean-key)
    
*   [Customer Key](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-exclusion#customer-key)
    

Glean allows you to exclude models from being used in your Glean Agents. This is useful if you want to prevent certain models from being available to your agent builders to be used in their agents.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-exclusion#glean-universal-model-key-formerly-glean-key)

Glean Universal Model Key (formerly _Glean Key_)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For the Glean Universal Model Key, Glean supports exclusion on the basis of model families. This means that you can exclude all models from a particular family from being used in your agents. Steps:

1.  Go to **Admin Console**.
2.  Select **Platform > LLM**.
3.  Click **Add LLM**.
4.  Select the specific hosting provider.
5.  Select the model families you want to exclude from the **Agent model families** section.
6.  A pop-up will appear informing you about the agents that will be affected by the exclusion.
7.  Click **Exclude**.

You cannot exclude model families used in the Assistant.

Any agents (or a step within an agent) that were using a model from a previously included model family will automatically start using the default assistant model. All the excluded model families will be shown in the **Excluded from agents** section. You can click on **Include** to use these model families in your agents again. ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/zagKvzHjzM_Fw7_d/images/model-hub/image-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=zagKvzHjzM_Fw7_d&q=85&s=d5cd7ec85c0f5e573cea37f6764140b5)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/model-exclusion#customer-key)

Customer Key
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For Customer Key, you can exclude specific models from being used in your agents. Steps:

1.  Go to **Admin Console**.
2.  Select **Platform > LLM**.
3.  Click **Add LLM**.
4.  Select the specific hosting provider.
5.  Clear the checkbox next to the model you want to exclude.
6.  Validate the connection.
7.  Click **Save**.

YesNo

# Dynamic Client Registration - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Authorization Server

Dynamic Client Registration

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Restriction options](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#restriction-options)
    
*   [Configuring the Glean-managed list](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#configuring-the-glean-managed-list)
    
*   [Configuring your own static list](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#configuring-your-own-static-list)
    
*   [Redirect URI patterns](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#redirect-uri-patterns)
    
*   [Example](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#example)
    
*   [Related links](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#related-links)
    

Admin-configured dynamic client restrictions is a new feature and may not be available in your tenant yet.

The Glean OAuth Authorization Server supports Dynamic Client Registration (DCR). This is the preferred approach for [MCP host applications to connect to Glean](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/authorization#dynamic-client-registration)
. By default, any MCP host application can register with the Glean OAuth Authorization Server. Admins who prefer to restrict dynamic client registration can do so by limiting the redirect URIs that dynamically registered clients may use.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#restriction-options)

Restriction options
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You have two options for restricting DCR:

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Glean-managed list** | Use a Glean curated list of clients, with optional additional redirect URIs for other applications. This list may change over time as new clients are evaluated. |
| **Static list** | Use a list of redirect URI patterns fully maintained by you. You have complete control over which clients can register. |

Use the **Glean-managed list** if you want to automatically allow popular MCP host applications as Glean vets them. Use a **static list** if your security policy requires explicit approval of every client.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#configuring-the-glean-managed-list)

Configuring the Glean-managed list
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use this option to allow Glean-vetted clients while optionally adding your own.

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Enable the Glean OAuth Authorization Server

Follow the steps in [Enabling the Glean OAuth Authorization Server](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#enabling-the-glean-oauth-authorization-server)
.

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Open settings

Click **Manage settings** to open the settings modal.

![Manage settings button](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/manage-settings.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=9f6d458a28b62920c76219ffc13a0f49)

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Enable redirect URI restrictions

Enable the **Restrict Dynamic Clients by Redirect URI** toggle.Leave the **Use Glean-managed List** toggle enabled to automatically allow future Glean-vetted clients.

![Glean-managed list toggle enabled](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/glean-managed-on.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=282f8785c467a60b1ea029b8809731fb)

4

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Add additional redirect URIs (optional)

Add any additional redirect URI patterns for clients you want your users to connect with.

![Add redirect URI pattern](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/add-redirect-uri-pattern.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=1268a106821fbd80186ddae9cc076656)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#configuring-your-own-static-list)

Configuring your own static list
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use this option for complete control over which clients can register.

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Enable the Glean OAuth Authorization Server

Follow the steps in [Enabling the Glean OAuth Authorization Server](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server#enabling-the-glean-oauth-authorization-server)
.

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Open settings

Click **Manage settings** to open the settings modal.

![Manage settings button](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/manage-settings.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=9f6d458a28b62920c76219ffc13a0f49)

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Enable redirect URI restrictions

Enable the **Restrict Dynamic Clients by Redirect URI** toggle.

![Restrict Dynamic Clients by Redirect URI toggle](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/restrict-dcr-by-redirect.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=7876a1546598866ebe70cab4dd1d2004)

4

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Disable Glean-managed list

Disable the **Use Glean-managed List** toggle. Now the only redirect URIs allowed during client registration are those that match the patterns in the table.

![Glean-managed list toggle disabled](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/glean-managed-off.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=92e6c89954c3211a47d68d160b92cbae)

5

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Configure allowed clients

Toggle individual clients to add or remove their redirect URI patterns.

![Toggle individual clients](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/client-toggle.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=ce765f54eee554bb5ebecacfc69a9cd4)

Alternatively, remove redirect URI patterns individually.

![Delete redirect URI pattern](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/delete-redirect-uri-pattern.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=85c1639e8b55a1de3f1f07f015318257)

6

[](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#)

Add redirect URI patterns

Add redirect URI patterns for any additional clients you want your users to connect with.

![Add redirect URI pattern](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU/images/oauth-server/add-redirect-uri-pattern.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=Vse9Yg0fOP5MR9GU&q=85&s=1268a106821fbd80186ddae9cc076656)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#redirect-uri-patterns)

Redirect URI patterns
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Patterns can match redirect URIs exactly or use `*` as a wildcard to match characters within a single path segment.

The wildcard `*` does not match path separators (`/`) or traverse directory boundaries. This prevents overly broad patterns from inadvertently allowing unintended redirect URIs.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#example)

Example

For a redirect URI `https://my.example.com/oauth/callback`:

| Pattern | Matches? | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `https://my.example.com/oauth/callback` | Yes | Exact match |
| `https://my.example.com/oauth/*` | Yes | Wildcard matches `callback` |
| `https://my.example.com/*/callback` | Yes | Wildcard matches `oauth` |
| `https://my.example.com/*` | No  | Wildcard cannot match across path separators (`oauth/callback`) |

[​](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/dynamic-client-registration#related-links)

Related links
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Glean OAuth Authorization Server](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/authorization-server)
    
*   [OAuth Overview](https://docs.glean.com/administration/oauth/oauth-overview)
    
*   [MCP Authorization (specification)](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/basic/authorization#dynamic-client-registration)

Stage 2: Build

Glean Deployment Models

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Glean Hosted (SaaS)](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#glean-hosted-saas)
    
*   [Description](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#description)
    
*   [Advantages](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#advantages)
    
*   [When should you choose this?](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#when-should-you-choose-this)
    
*   [Customer-Hosted (Cloud-Prem)](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#customer-hosted-cloud-prem)
    
*   [Description](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#description-2)
    
*   [Advantages](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#advantages-2)
    
*   [When should you choose this?](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#when-should-you-choose-this-2)
    

Glean currently offers two deployment models for customers: Glean Hosted (SaaS) and Customer-Hosted (Cloud-Prem). Regardless of the deployment model chosen:

1.  Glean only uses a single-tenant infrastructure, meaning that each customer’s data is isolated from other customers’ data. Customer data does not leave this environment.
2.  Glean is responsible for infrastructure and application management, including upgrades, patching, and monitoring.
3.  Glean maintains support access to the customer environment for debugging and support purposes.

Our product is the same in both deployment models, with the only difference being where the infrastructure is hosted.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#glean-hosted-saas)

Glean Hosted (SaaS)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#description)

Description

In this scenario, Glean hosts your Glean instance within our own tenant. This is equivalent to a true SaaS service. The customer does not have any infrastructure responsibility in this model. The cloud provider that Glean uses is GCP.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#advantages)

Advantages

*   Simplified setup process - no infrastructure setup required on customer end.
*   Full auto-scaling as the corpus and users grow.
*   Rely on Glean’s security controls and guarantees to protect your data.
*   Simpler licensing and pricing model. Glean is responsible for all infrastructure costs.
*   Multiple regions supported

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#when-should-you-choose-this)

When should you choose this?

*   You do not want to host the infrastructure for Glean.
*   You want a full out-of-the-box experience.
*   You want to have a simpler pricing model.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#customer-hosted-cloud-prem)

Customer-Hosted (Cloud-Prem)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#description-2)

Description

In the Cloud-prem model, your Glean tenant is deployed by Glean **as a managed service** in isolation within your own cloud environment (i.e. GCP, AWS). **Note that this is equivalent to a hosted-SaaS model where Glean still has minimal access to operate it like as SaaS service. This is not a traditional self-hosted model.**

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#advantages-2)

Advantages

*   Full data residency guarantees - data does not leave your organizational boundaries
*   Enhanced visibility - you have full access to all raw logs stored within Glean
*   Additonal options for networking to internal resources - while Glean still operates in an isolated VPC controlled by Glean, organizations have more options for networking to internal resources.
*   Additional security controls - customers can apply VPC SC + Org policies (GCP) or SCPs (AWS) to further lock down the project.
*   Use your existing cloud spend commitments to retire the costs of hosting Glean. Glean provides lower licensing costs for customers that host Glean in their own cloud environment.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/about-self-hosted#when-should-you-choose-this-2)

When should you choose this?

*   You have strict data residency requirements where data cannot leave your organizational boundaries. Alternative geographic regions are also available in SaaS
*   You want full access to raw logs for compliance or security reasons
*   You want to leverage your existing cloud spend commitments to retire the costs of hosting Glean
*   You want additional security controls and networking options on the project

Glean does not support manually deploying/patching the Glean services, or altering any part of the Glean architecture. Glean’s architecture is not just a set of containerized services: It is a fully integrated system spanning different private networks, storage, databases, compute resources, and other integrated services.If your organization has significant restrictions on what can be deployed in your cloud environment, we recommend considering the Glean-hosted option instead.

Looking for the original version of this page? You can find the archived version [here](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/getting-started-in-glean/glean-deployment-options)
.

YesNo

On this page

![banner](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/fRXYLCZbzve_-I7o/images/get-started/start/welcome-banner.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=fRXYLCZbzve_-I7o&q=85&s=aa64f09b0eac72f18435f7291b116e28) Here’s what you need to do next to make sure Glean becomes a seamless part of your daily operations.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#checklist)

Checklist
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#)

Conduct a follow-up survey with your users

[](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#)

Expand Glean to additional users

[](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#)

Set Up Regular Check-Ins with the Glean team

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#1-conduct-a-follow-up-survey)

1\. Conduct a Follow-Up Survey
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

~4 weeks after the kickoff to the initial wave of users, you should send out a survey to collect their thoughts and experiences, and examine how the results from the initial survey have changed. This feedback is crucial for understanding what’s working and what might need tweaking before the next wave of users is brought on-board.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#survey-template)

Survey Template

Glean offers a template for the follow-up survey designed to gather all necessary user insights effectively. This template can be used as-is or can be customized to better fit the specific needs of your organization.

*   [Glean Follow-Up User Survey](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1LgXBqKJqlP8q_xpR2p0J9h7_QI8wCUcE6XGZn5VFeUI)
     (Google Forms)

You should ensure that all of your initial users have completed the survey before on-boarding the next group.

Consider posting an **Announcement** in Glean that mandates the completion of this survey for all users.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#2-deploy-additional-users)

2\. Deploy Additional Users
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If user feedback is positive, start to expand the Glean rollout to additional waves of users. If your initial rollout was only within a single department or team, you should now expand to additional business units.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/start/post-launch-tasks#3-meet-with-glean)

3\. Meet with Glean
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If not in place already, ensure that regular meetings with your Delivery Excellence Manager (DEM) or Account Manager have been scheduled. These are invaluable for troubleshooting, exploring new features, and ensuring that your Glean deployment continues to go smoothly.

Customize Branding

On this page

*   [Company Branding](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/golive/customize-branding#company-branding)
    
*   [Home Page](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/golive/customize-branding#home-page)
    

![banner](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/1v8VopG7Y8qlR0ki/images/get-started/golive/branding-banner.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=1v8VopG7Y8qlR0ki&q=85&s=939bb0ca51c08b2bd18c85cb034752a1) Administrators can control several aspects of Glean’s look and feel to ensure that it fits with the rest of the tools that employees use at work. The customization options include the following:

*   **Company Branding:** These settings control the look of Glean everywhere, and include support for custom logos and colors.
*   **Home page:** These settings control how the Glean home page appears to users across your company, and include settings for a default background image, quick actions, and available home page widgets.
*   **Feature Enablement:** These settings control which features within the Glean app are enabled/disabled for your employees. E.g. You may wish to disable the org chart from being accessed by your users.

You must be assigned the **Admin** or **Super Admin** roles to be able to customize the workspace appearance.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/golive/customize-branding#company-branding)

Company Branding
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance > Manage Branding](https://app.glean.com/admin/appearance) ![company branding](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/1v8VopG7Y8qlR0ki/images/get-started/golive/branding-customize.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=1v8VopG7Y8qlR0ki&q=85&s=64af49d0017b66910aedb7b1107bc935) You can personalize the Glean UI to match your company’s branding by adjusting:

*   The company name displayed throughout the UI
*   Logo
*   Color theme

More information: [Company Branding](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/branding)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/golive/customize-branding#home-page)

Home Page
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 [Admin Console > Settings > Appearance > Manage Home Page](https://app.glean.com/admin/appearance) ![home page](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/1v8VopG7Y8qlR0ki/images/get-started/golive/branding-homepage.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=1v8VopG7Y8qlR0ki&q=85&s=648d84f4049538bc4baeac0d8e59014c) Customize the look and feel of the Glean home page for your employees, including:

*   **Cards:** Cards are the sections that are visible on the Glean home page for each of your users. These can include suggested documents, collections, and people celebrations.
*   **Quick Actions:** Quick actions are the items that are displayed under the New button in the top-right corner of the Home and New Tab pages; providing shortcuts for your employees to create new content across all of your apps.
*   **Default wallpapers:** You can set the default wallpaper that is used for both light and dark modes when users first open Glean.

More information: [Home Page Settings](https://docs.glean.com/administration/management/customization/home-page)

# Items to Prepare - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/prepare/items-to-prepare#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Stage 1: Prepare

Items to Prepare

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

1\. Identify Phase 1 Data Sources

Every organization has a long list of applications that need to be connected and integrated with Glean.To speed up your time to value, you should identify 4-6 “mission critical” apps to be integrated first. For example, SharePoint, Outlook, Google Drive, Jira, Confluence, Zendesk, etc.**These should be the core applications that have the largest user footprints within your organization.** At a bare minimum you should ensure you have in scope at least:

*   One document repository app (SharePoint, OneDrive, Google Drive, Box, etc)
*   One communications app (Teams, Slack, etc)

Any apps outside of this core scope can be added at any time at a later stage.

**Why can’t every app be connected upfront?**Before Glean can be used, a full fetch of data from core apps needs to be completed so that your search algorithm can be created. The algorithm cannot be created before this is complete.If the focus is on connecting **every** application in the organization upfront, you will significantly delay the point at which our training workflows can be started, and in turn, the point at which users can start using the service.

2\. Obtain App Owner & Security Approvals

One of the biggest delays encountered by customers when setting up Glean is the internal approvals needed (both from an application owner and security perspective) to connect each app to Glean.We recommend initiating the approval process as early as possible to avoid any unnecessary delays. Start by:

*   Identifying the key stakeholders within your organization who are responsible for application ownership and security.
*   Schedule meetings or consultations with these stakeholders to discuss the integration of Glean and address any concerns they may have.
*   Provide them with detailed documentation about Glean’s security protocols, compliance certifications, and data handling practices to facilitate a smoother approval process.
    
    Some of this information may require an NDA. Please contact your Glean account team for more information.
    
*   Prepare a clear and concise summary of the benefits and functionalities that Glean will bring to your organization, which can help in gaining their support.

3\. (Self Hosted instances) Prepare Your Cloud Environment

If you are opting for a self-hosted deployment of Glean, it is crucial to prepare your cloud environment in advance by following the preparation guide of the cloud provider you are choosing. To do this, please work with your internal administrator of the cloud provider.This will involve:

1.  Picking one of the supported regions of the cloud provider.
2.  Correctly setting billing information for the environment.
3.  Enabling all the required services
4.  Adjusting the quotas of the cloud provider
5.  Providing Glean with details of the environment including:
    *   (GCP) The project ID, name, number, and the selected region
    *   (AWS) The account ID, name, and the selected region

For detailed documentation on the requirements for creating the environment, please refer to the docs for [GCP](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/gcp/deploy-gcp)
 or [AWS](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/build/aws/deploy-aws)
.

4\. Identify the user group

If not launching to the entire company at once, you should identify the intial group of users who will test Glean, and provide feedback on the system. Consider the following when selecting users to pilot Glean:

*   Diverse Representation: Choose users from different departments and roles to ensure diverse feedback and comprehensive testing.
*   Tech-Savvy Users: Select users who are comfortable with technology and can quickly adapt to new tools.
*   Key Stakeholders: Include key stakeholders who can champion the adoption of Glean within their teams.

Once identified, provide these users with the necessary training and resources to get started with Glean (we can help!). Their feedback will be invaluable in fine-tuning the system before a broader rollout.

5\. Ready Your SSO and People/Directory Data

*   **SSO Integration:** Ensure that your SSO provider is ready for integration with Glean. Glean supports a variety of SSO providers, including Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace. You should have the necessary permissions to configure SSO settings in your IdP. Glean requires SSO integration to be complete before end-users can access Glean.
*   **People/Directory Data:** Ensure that your people data is up-to-date and accurate in your people data source. Glean uses this data to provide personalized search results and recommendations to users. The important fields are First Name, Last Name, Email, Title, Team, Department, Location, and Manager Email. People data is a required item.

In most organizations, people data is already synced to the SSO provider you are using and both the SSO and People data can be configured by the same person.

6\. Timeline Planning

A well-structured timeline ensures smooth deployment and sets appropriate expectations.

*   **Project Plan:** Develop of a detailed project plan that outlines all the key milestones, tasks, and deadlines associated with the deployment.
*   **Resource Allocation:** Ensure that you have allocated the necessary resources, including personnel, to assist with deployment of Glean.
*   **Risk Management:** Identify potential risks and develop mitigation strategies to address any issues that may arise during the deployment process.
*   **Communication Plan:** Establish a communication plan to keep all stakeholders informed about the progress of the deployment. You should also ensure that mechanisms to collect and aggregate user feedback have been identified.
