[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Features and Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#features-and-limitations)
    
*   [Supported Objects](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#supported-objects)
    
*   [Key Features](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#key-features)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#limitations)
    
*   [Requirements](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#requirements)
    
*   [How to Set up the Connector](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#how-to-set-up-the-connector)
    
*   [Step 1: Obtain the API Token from Shortcut](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#step-1-obtain-the-api-token-from-shortcut)
    
*   [Step 2: Set up a Webhook in Shortcut](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#step-2-set-up-a-webhook-in-shortcut)
    
*   [Step 3: Configure the Connector in Glean](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#step-3-configure-the-connector-in-glean)
    
*   [Permissions & Security](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#permissions-%26-security)
    

The Shortcut connector enables secure, unified search of Shortcut stories and epics from within Glean’s enterprise search platform. It is designed to work with **Shortcut (formerly Clubhouse)** to centralize project management knowledge alongside content from all your other applications. This integration allows users to quickly find relevant project information and a complete view of their work. Most importantly, it enforces user-level permissions from Shortcut at the time of the query, ensuring every user only sees content they are authorized to access. All indexed Shortcut data is securely stored within your own cloud environment, ensuring your organizational data never leaves your infrastructure.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#features-and-limitations)

Features and Limitations

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#supported-objects)

Supported Objects

The Shortcut connector indexes the following entities:

*   Stories
*   Epics

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#key-features)

Key Features

*   **Comprehensive Indexing:** Indexes Shortcut stories and epics, including key metadata such as status, type, assignees, and a custom popularity metric.
*   **Native Permissions:** Inherits and strictly enforces user-level access permissions from Shortcut at the time of the query.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#limitations)

Limitations

*   **Cloud-Only:** This connector is designed exclusively for native integration with the cloud version of Shortcut. It does not support on-premises or self-hosted deployments.
*   **Entity Scope:** The connector’s scope is currently limited to core entities. It indexes stories and epics, but does not support additional entities such as comments, attachments, or workflows.
*   **Custom Field Support:** The connector does not support the indexing of custom objects or advanced field mapping.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#requirements)

Requirements

To set up the Shortcut connector, you must have the following administrative access and credentials.

*   **Shortcut Workspace:** An active Shortcut (Clubhouse) workspace using the standard cloud-based service.
*   **Glean Access:** Administrative permission to configure connectors within your Glean deployment.
*   **API Token:** A Shortcut API token with workspace-level read access to all relevant stories and epics.
*   **Permissions:** The user account providing the API token must have an admin role or a dedicated integration account with sufficient permissions to access all data intended for indexing.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#how-to-set-up-the-connector)

How to Set up the Connector

The setup process involves obtaining credentials from Shortcut and then configuring the connector in Glean. The process requires a **Shortcut API token** and a **Webhook secret**.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#step-1-obtain-the-api-token-from-shortcut)

Step 1: Obtain the API Token from Shortcut

1.  Log in to your Shortcut workspace.
2.  Navigate to profile or organization settings and find the **API Tokens** section.
3.  Generate a new API token, ensuring it has workspace-level read access to stories and epics. For more information, see [Shortcut API documentation](https://help.shortcut.com/hc/en-us/articles/205701199-Shortcut-API-Tokens)
    .

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#step-2-set-up-a-webhook-in-shortcut)

Step 2: Set up a Webhook in Shortcut

This step is required to generate the Webhook Secret needed for the Glean setup.

1.  Log in as an Administrator in your Shortcut instance.
2.  Go to the **Webhooks** section in your admin settings.
3.  Create a new webhook and configure it with the following:
    *   **Payload URL:** `https://<your-glean-instance>-be.glean.com/instance/SHORTCUT/events`
    *   **Secret:** Choose and enter a unique secret. **This is the Webhook Secret you will need for Glean.**
    *   **Scope:** Select **All workspaces** to ensure timely notifications for all changes.
4.  Save the webhook configuration. For more information, see [Setting up Outgoing Webhooks](https://help.shortcut.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005093963-Setting-up-Outgoing-Webhooks)
    .

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#step-3-configure-the-connector-in-glean)

Step 3: Configure the Connector in Glean

1.  In the Glean Admin Console, navigate to **Data Sources** and select **Add Data Source**, and select **Shortcut**.
2.  Enter the **API Token** you obtained in Step 1.
3.  Enter the **Webhook Secret** you created in Step 2.
4.  Click **Save**. Glean will automatically validate the credentials and begin the initial crawl.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/shortcut/home#permissions-&-security)

Permissions & Security

*   **Permission Enforcement:** The connector relies entirely on the permissions defined in Shortcut. Glean does not override these permissions, ensuring that users can only access content for which they are authorized.
*   **Data Storage:** All Shortcut data ingested by Glean is stored securely in your designated cloud environment. No content is stored outside of your infrastructure.

# Seismic - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#content-area)

Seismic

On this page

*   [Supported Features and Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#supported-features-and-limitations)
    
*   [Supported Objects/Entities](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#supported-objects%2Fentities)
    
*   [Key features](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#key-features)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#limitations)
    
*   [Crawling Strategy](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#crawling-strategy)
    
*   [Results Display](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#results-display)
    
*   [Requirements](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#requirements)
    
*   [Technical requirements](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#technical-requirements)
    
*   [Scope](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#scope)
    
*   [Preliminary setup](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#preliminary-setup)
    
*   [Setup instructions](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#setup-instructions)
    
*   [Register a new Seismic app for crawl](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#register-a-new-seismic-app-for-crawl)
    
*   [Get refresh and access token for the app](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#get-refresh-and-access-token-for-the-app)
    
*   [(Optional) Manage data](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#optional-manage-data)
    
*   [Configure content indexing by file extension](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#configure-content-indexing-by-file-extension)
    
*   [Configure folder indexing by greenlisting and redlisting](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#configure-folder-indexing-by-greenlisting-and-redlisting)
    
*   [Grant document access via custom properties](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#grant-document-access-via-custom-properties)
    
*   [Permissions & Security](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#permissions-%26-security)
    

The Seismic connector enables you to search published information in Seismic content profiles directly from Glean. By integrating Seismic with Glean, you can ensure that users only see content they are authorized to access, with permissions enforced down to the record level at query time. This connector is designed to help organizations surface relevant Seismic files while maintaining strict data privacy and access controls.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#supported-features-and-limitations)

Supported Features and Limitations
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Seismic connector is focused on indexing and searching published content profiles within Seismic. It leverages Seismic’s Reporting API and enforces user-level permissions to ensure secure and relevant search results.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#supported-objects/entities)

Supported Objects/Entities

| Object Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Content Profiles | Published information in Seismic content profiles |
| Library Documents | Documents within content profiles (library only) |

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#key-features)

Key features

*   **Content profiles:** Indexes published content profiles and Library Documents.
*   **Permission enforcement:** Honors granular Seismic permissions, ensuring users only see content they are allowed to access.
*   **Custom properties:** Supports using Seismic custom properties to grant document access to specific groups in Glean, enabling precise access control.
*   **Advanced crawling:** Efficiently syncs new and updated items through incremental crawling.
*   **Advanced configuration controls**: The connector offers granular control over indexed content:
    *   **File extensions (Greenlist):** You can specify file types (e.g., `pdf`, `docx`, `pptx`) whose content Glean should fully index.
    *   **Folder inclusion (Greenlist):** You can define specific Library Folder IDs that Glean should crawl **exclusively**.
    *   **Folder exclusion (Redlist):** You can define Library Folder IDs that Glean should explicitly **exclude** from crawling.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#limitations)

Limitations

*   **Content scope:** Only **published content profiles and library documents** are indexed. Workspace files and unpublished content are excluded.
*   **Embedded documents:** Embedded documents (e.g., Google Slides reformatted as PowerPoints) are surfaced only as links; their content is not crawled.
*   **User visibility:** Due to Seismic API constraints, **all published content profile links are made available to all Seismic users in Glean**. Users cannot view content in Seismic if they lack native access.
*   **Updates:** The connector does not support webhooks; all updates are detected through scheduled crawls.
*   **Licensing:** The **Seismic Reporting API**, which the connector leverages, may require a specific Seismic license.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#crawling-strategy)

Crawling Strategy

| Crawl type | Full Crawl | Incremental Crawl | People Data | Activity | Update Rate | Webhook | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Content Profile | Yes | Yes | No  | Yes | Scheduled | No  | Only published content profiles. No webhooks and activity through API |
| Library Docs | Yes | Yes | No  | No  | Scheduled | No  | Only library not workspace docs and incremental supported |

You can configure greenlist (include) or redlist (exclude) folders at the library level to control which folders are crawled.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#results-display)

Results Display

Search results from Seismic appear in Glean with document titles, metadata, and links to the original Seismic content. Embedded documents are shown as links, but their content is not indexed.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#requirements)

Requirements
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use the Seismic connector, you must meet certain technical, credential, and permission requirements. These ensure secure integration and proper enforcement of access controls.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#technical-requirements)

Technical requirements

*   **API access:** Your Seismic account must have the **Reporting API enabled**, which may require a specific Seismic license.
*   **Seismic permissions:** You must have **admin access** in Seismic to register and configure OAuth2 applications.
*   **Glean access:** You need administrative privileges in the Glean Admin Console.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#scope)

Scope

*   The Seismic app must be granted the following OAuth scopes:
    
    *   seismic.user.view
    *   seismic.user.manage
    *   seismic.configuration.view
    *   seismic.configuration.manage
    *   seismic.reporting
    *   seismic.delivery
    *   seismic.library.view
    *   seismic.library.manage
    *   seismic.workspace.view
    *   seismic.workspace.manage
    
    Do not use `seismic.self.view` or `seismic.self.manage` as these are insufficient for connector operation.
    
*   The authenticated user must have access to the content profiles you wish to index.
*   The Reporting API must be enabled for your Seismic account.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#preliminary-setup)

Preliminary setup

*   (Optional) Configure custom properties in Seismic to grant document access to specific groups in Glean.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#setup-instructions)

Setup instructions
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Follow these steps to configure and set up the Seismic connector in Glean. Most setup occurs in the Seismic admin console and the Glean admin interface.

*   In Glean, you need to enter the client ID and secret when adding the Seismic connector.
*   Complete the OAuth2 flow to authorize Glean to access your Seismic data.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#register-a-new-seismic-app-for-crawl)

Register a new Seismic app for crawl

To get the client ID and secret from your Seismic tenant for the Glean set up, run the following steps:

1.  Create and register a new app at [https://apps.seismic.com/](https://apps.seismic.com/)
     in the Seismic tenant. Name this app indicating that it is used for Glean integration. ![Seismic UI screenshot](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/E3lIHdVinO05oQPs/images/seismic-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=E3lIHdVinO05oQPs&q=85&s=3cf96dbda3c0329b084accdb0294e717)
2.  Setup the Authorization Code Flow
    1.  Select _OAuth2 - Authorization Code Flow_ in the **Authentication** tab. ![Seismic UI screenshot](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/E3lIHdVinO05oQPs/images/seismic-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=E3lIHdVinO05oQPs&q=85&s=cc6f66c7c910b6179789c79d1a0a538a)
    2.  On the _OAuth2 Information_ page, add the redirect URL as the tenant URL:
        1.  For example, [https://yourtenant.seismic.com/](https://yourtenant.seismic.com/)
            .
        2.  Client ID is generated automatically.
        3.  Generate the client secret. The clieny secret is hidden initially, but you can show and copy the secret. it. ![Seismic UI screenshot](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/E3lIHdVinO05oQPs/images/seismic-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=E3lIHdVinO05oQPs&q=85&s=da29338a6154157f31ba57be0d8e13ed)
    3.  In the scopes section, select the following scopes:
        *   seismic.user.view
        *   seismic.user.manage
        *   seismic.configuration.view
        *   seismic.configuration.manage
        *   seismic.reporting
        *   seismic.delivery
        *   seismic.library.view
        *   seismic.library.manage
        *   seismic.workspace.view
        *   seismic.workspace.manage. ![Seismic UI screenshot](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/E3lIHdVinO05oQPs/images/seismic-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=E3lIHdVinO05oQPs&q=85&s=3036b13e21c65a021b30ede5d744abab)
3.  With the information that you have gathered from the above steps:
    1.  Enter the tenant name into the **Tenant Name** input box. Ensure to not include _seismic.com_. For example, id the tenant name is `https://sciodemo.seismic.com`, enter only `sciodemo`.
    2.  Enter the client ID into the **Client ID** input box.
    3.  Enter the client secret into the **Client Secret** input box.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#get-refresh-and-access-token-for-the-app)

Get refresh and access token for the app

To get an access and a refresh token for the Glean set up, perform the following steps:

1.  Go the [Seismic documentation](https://developer.seismic.com/seismicsoftware/reference/authorization-code-login)
     for instructions and executions of POST requests.
2.  Run the URL given on [Get the user’s authorization](https://developer.seismic.com/seismicsoftware/reference/get-tokens-using-authorization_code-flow)
     to get an access code. Note that scopes must be separated by a space in the URL.
    
    Ensure you include **all** required scopes from the Permission Requirements section above when making the authorization request. The scope field should be `scope=seismic.reporting seismic.workspace.manage seismic.workspace.view seismic.library.manage seismic.library.view seismic.delivery seismic.configuration.manage seismic.configuration.view seismic.user.manage seismic.user.view offline_access`. Missing scopes will cause the connector to fail during crawling.
    
    *   You will be redirected and the URL will include the access code. For example: [https://sciodemo.seismic.com/app?code=sciodemo\_ABCDEF123&scope=seismic.reporting+offline\_access&state=blank#/home](https://sciodemo.seismic.com/app?code=sciodemo_ABCDEF123&scope=seismic.reporting+offline_access&state=blank#/home)
        .
3.  Exchange the authorization code for an access token
    1.  Go to the [Exchange the Authorization Code for an Access Token page](https://developer.seismic.com/seismicsoftware/reference/authorization-code-get-the-token)
        .
    2.  Enter the required information and click **Try it**.
    3.  The response will have the access token and refresh token. ![Seismic UI screenshot](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/E3lIHdVinO05oQPs/images/seismic-5.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=E3lIHdVinO05oQPs&q=85&s=3c54f68d8352281a2bf1020f5850069b)
    4.  Enter the access token into the **Access Token** input box.
    5.  Enter the refresh token into the **Refresh Token** input box.
4.  Click **Save** in Glean.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#optional-manage-data)

(Optional) Manage data
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the initial connector setup is complete, use the following steps to control which content is indexed and how document access is managed in Glean.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#configure-content-indexing-by-file-extension)

Configure content indexing by file extension

Specify which file types the connector should crawl for content. Only file extensions listed here will have their full content indexed.

1.  In the Glean Admin console, navigate to the **Seismic** data source and go to the **Manage data** tab.
2.  Expand the **File Extensions** section.
3.  In the **Greenlisted File Extensions** field, add the desired file types as a comma-separated list (e.g., `pdf, docx, pptx`).
4.  To apply changes to existing documents, run a fresh crawl after updating the list.
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/g4xTs4JaSZd8o8MO/images/connectors/native/seismic/file-extension.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=g4xTs4JaSZd8o8MO&q=85&s=975c555e4c881f0e55e939e2b97366de)
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#configure-folder-indexing-by-greenlisting-and-redlisting)

Configure folder indexing by greenlisting and redlisting

Restrict or permit crawling at the library folder level by using inclusion and exclusion rules.

1.  **Retrieve Folder IDs:** Navigate to the folder in Seismic. The Folder ID is the segment immediately following `/LIST/` in the browser URL. **Example URL:**  
    `https://<domain>.seismic.com/app#/contentmanager/main/.../<FolderID>/LIST/title`  
    Folder ID: `<FolderID>`
2.  **Define inclusion (Greenlist):**
    *   In the **Library folder inclusion rules** section, add the Folder IDs that Glean should crawl **exclusively**. If this list is left empty, all folders are crawled by default.
3.  **Define exclusion (Redlist):**
    
    *   In the **Library folder exclusion rules** section, add the Folder IDs that Glean should explicitly **exclude** from crawling. (Note: Exclusion rules are ignored if inclusion rules are populated.)
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/g4xTs4JaSZd8o8MO/images/connectors/native/seismic/library-folder.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=g4xTs4JaSZd8o8MO&q=85&s=fc1b406e0e1eabf454381cb57116a8b9)
    

Once you’ve made all the changes, click **Save**.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#grant-document-access-via-custom-properties)

Grant document access via custom properties

You can map document access based on group membership by configuring a custom property in Seismic and assigning it to documents.

1.  In Seismic, navigate to the **Custom Properties** page: `https://<domain>.seismic.com/apps/adminsettings/system/CustomPropertiesV2`.
2.  Create a new custom property (e.g., “Glean Content Profile”) with the following specifications:
    *   **Type:** Select **Multi-select**.
    *   **Values:** Add the exact names of the user groups (e.g., “G2 Delete Group,” “APAC Sales”) that will be granted access.
3.  Assign this custom property to the target documents either individually or via the bulk edit tool.
4.  In the property field (e.g., “Glean Content Profile”), select the groups that should have view access (e.g., “TSG 62,” “Group CP 120”).

Once the custom property setup and assignments are complete, reach out to your Account Executive team to enable this setting on the Glean deployment. After the Account Executive confirms deployment, run a fresh crawl. Group members listed in the custom property will now be able to view these documents in Glean.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/seismic/home#permissions-&-security)

Permissions & Security
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Data and Metadata Ingested:**

*   Published content profile documents, metadata (title, author, last modified), and links to embedded documents.

**Permission Propagation Logic:**

*   Glean enforces Seismic permissions at query time. If Seismic’s API does not expose content profile membership, all published content profile links are made available to all Seismic users in Glean. However, users cannot view content in Seismic if they do not have access.

**Security & Compliance Notes:**

*   OAuth2-based authentication.
*   All data remains in your cloud environment.
*   No webhooks. All updates are detected through scheduled crawls.

**Known Security Restrictions:**

*   No support for multi-instance scenarios.
*   All published content profile links are visible to all Seismic users in Glean due to API limitations.

**Data Privacy Implications:**

*   The connector does not expose unpublished or workspace content.
*   Users cannot access content in Seismic for which they do not have permission, even if it appears in Glean search.

If you have any issues or concerns, reah out to the [Glean support team](https://support.glean.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

Navigation

Native Connectors

Smartsheet

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Supported Features and Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#supported-features-and-limitations)
    
*   [Supported Objects/Entities](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#supported-objects%2Fentities)
    
*   [Supported API Endpoints/Features](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#supported-api-endpoints%2Ffeatures)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#limitations)
    
*   [Crawling Strategy](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#crawling-strategy)
    
*   [Results Display](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#results-display)
    
*   [Requirements](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#requirements)
    
*   [Preliminary Source/System Setup](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#preliminary-source%2Fsystem-setup)
    
*   [Configuration and Setup Instructions](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#configuration-and-setup-instructions)
    
*   [Authentication and Credentials](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#authentication-and-credentials)
    
*   [Step-by-Step Setup](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#step-by-step-setup)
    
*   [Crawl Configuration Options](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#crawl-configuration-options)
    
*   [Permissions & Security](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#permissions-%26-security)
    

Smartsheet is a cloud-based work execution platform that helps organizations manage projects, automate workflows, and collaborate efficiently. The Smartsheet connector enables you to index and search Smartsheet content—including sheets, dashboards, reports, and workspaces—directly within Glean, with strict enforcement of native permissions of Smartsheet at query time.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#supported-features-and-limitations)

Supported Features and Limitations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Smartsheet connector is designed to index key Smartsheet content types and enforce user-level access controls. This section outlines what you can expect from the integration.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#supported-objects/entities)

Supported Objects/Entities

| Object Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Sheets | Project planning documents containing rows, columns, attachments, and metadata. |
| Dashboards | Visual documents aggregating data from multiple sources. |
| Reports | Aggregated views across multiple sheets for analysis. |
| Workspaces | Top-level containers for organizing sheets, reports, and dashboards. |

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#supported-api-endpoints/features)

Supported API Endpoints/Features

*   Identity: Get Users, Get Groups, Get Token (refreshing access tokens)
*   Content: Get Sheets, Get Reports, Get Sights (Dashboards), Get Workspaces
*   Permissions: Get shares for Sheets, Reports, Sights, and Workspaces

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#limitations)

Limitations

*   Only text content is indexed and complex field types like dropdowns, attachments, comments may not be fully represented.
*   Owner information is only available for sheets due to Smartsheet API limitations.
*   Dashboards, reports, and workspaces may not display owner information in search results.
*   Activity crawls and webhook-based updates are not supported. As a result, permission changes and deletions may take up to a few hours or in some cases, days to propagate in Glean.
*   Documents shared through public view links like _Anybody can view/edit_ are not indexed or surfaced in Glean search.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#crawling-strategy)

Crawling Strategy

| Crawl Type | Full Crawl | Incremental Crawl | Identity | Activity | Update Rate | Webhook | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Smartsheet | 1h  | 10m | 10m | N/A | N/A | N/A | No webhook or activity crawl. Full content crawls are used for updates and deletions. |

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#results-display)

Results Display

In Glean’s search results, you can facet Smartsheet content by Type (Sheet, Dashboard, Report, Workspace), Owner (for sheets), and Workspace. Each result displays the document type icon and relevant metadata, helping you quickly identify and filter the content you need.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#requirements)

Requirements
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use the Smartsheet connector, you must meet certain prerequisites.

*   Smartsheet Business plan or higher API access required.
*   Glean platform access with admin privileges for connector setup.
*   Smartsheet system administrator account to authorize the Glean app through OAuth. OAuth 2.0 is required for secure, scoped access. Each user can optionally authenticate to enable indexing of their private documents.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#preliminary-source/system-setup)

Preliminary Source/System Setup

*   Register the Glean app in your Smartsheet account (handled during OAuth flow).
*   No additional API enablement or app registration is required beyond the standard OAuth process.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#configuration-and-setup-instructions)

Configuration and Setup Instructions
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Follow these steps to configure and deploy the Smartsheet connector in your Glean environment.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#authentication-and-credentials)

Authentication and Credentials

*   Setup is performed through OAuth 2.0. You will be prompted to authorize the Glean app with your Smartsheet admin credentials.
*   After initial setup, individual users can authenticate to enable indexing of their private Smartsheet documents.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#step-by-step-setup)

Step-by-Step Setup

1.  In Glean, navigate to the **Admin console**.
2.  Go to Workspace > Setup > Apps and search for _Smartsheet_. Or use this link [Smartsheet](https://app.glean.com/admin/setup/apps/smartsheet/)
    .
3.  Click “Create Authorization Link” and follow the instructions to open Smartsheet’s OAuth flow in a new tab.
4.  Complete the OAuth flow in Smartsheet. You will be redirected back to Glean.
5.  Once authorized, Glean will begin the initial crawl of all Smartsheet content accessible to the system admin.
6.  For help, contact Glean support or your customer success representative.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#crawl-configuration-options)

Crawl Configuration Options

*   The connector performs a full content crawl every hour and incremental crawls every 10 minutes.
*   You can configure which users’ content is indexed by controlling who authenticates with Smartsheet.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/smartsheet/home#permissions-&-security)

Permissions & Security

**Data and Metadata Ingested:**

*   Sheets, dashboards, reports, and workspaces (text content only).
*   User and group permissions for each document.
*   Metadata such as title, workspace, and (for sheets) owner.

**Permission Propagation Logic:**

*   Smartsheet’s workspace permissions cascade to all contained documents.
*   Glean enforces document-level permissions at query time, matching Smartsheet’s access model.

**Security & Compliance Notes:**

*   OAuth 2.0 authentication ensures secure, scoped access.
*   All data remains within GCP environment your organization.
*   No support for public or anonymous access and all access is authenticated and permissioned.

**Data Privacy Implications:**

*   The connector does not index or expose any content beyond what users are permitted to access in Smartsheet.
*   All data is processed and stored in compliance with Glean’s privacy and security policies.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

# Simpplr (AWS Version) - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Native Connectors

Simpplr (AWS Version)

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Key features](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#key-features)
    
*   [Supported objects/entities](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#supported-objects%2Fentities)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#limitations)
    
*   [Permission model](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#permission-model)
    
*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#prerequisites)
    
*   [Step-by-step instructions](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#step-by-step-instructions)
    
*   [Determine Simpplr hostnames and credentials](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#determine-simpplr-hostnames-and-credentials)
    
*   [Obtain hostnames (API and UI)](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#obtain-hostnames-api-and-ui)
    
*   [Generate and provide credentials](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#generate-and-provide-credentials)
    
*   [Stop crawling of previous Simpplr (Salesforce) setup](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#stop-crawling-of-previous-simpplr-salesforce-setup)
    
*   [Frequently asked questions (FAQs)](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#frequently-asked-questions-faqs)
    

The Glean Simpplr Connector enables your organization to index and search content from Simpplr’s modern intranet, directly within Glean. This new connector is specifically built for Simpplr’s AWS platform, replacing the old Salesforce-based integration. As Simpplr transitions all customers to AWS, Glean’s renewed connector ensures you maintain seamless intranet search and knowledge discovery.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#key-features)

Key features
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Design**: Works with Simpplr’s latest AWS APIs and infrastructure.

*   **Indexes Pages, Events, and Sites**: Search Simpplr’s core objects - Pages (knowledge/news), Events, and Sites. Metadata like likes/comments is supported, but not page view/activity analytics.
*   **Permission-aware:** Honors granular Simpplr permissions so users only see content they’re allowed to access.
*   **Incremental crawling:** Efficiently syncs new and updated items post initial import.
*   **Authentication**: Uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials.
*   **Access control:** Excludes inactive or frozen users from search results, **preventing permission leaks**.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#supported-objects/entities)

Supported objects/entities
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Object/Entity | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Pages | Authored documents within a site, including “knowledge” and “news” types |
| Events | Calendar entries with details such as date, location, and linked site |
| Sites | Primary groupings in Simpplr, which can be public, private, or unlisted |

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#limitations)

Limitations
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Page view/activity analytics are not indexed.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#permission-model)

Permission model
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Public sites:** Accessible to all users in your Glean data source.
*   **Private & unlisted sites:** Only visible to explicitly invited members.
*   **Admins:** Have access to all site content.

**Note:** Only users marked as “active” in Simpplr will have search access. Inactive or frozen users are excluded.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#prerequisites)

Prerequisites
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Your Simpplr environment **must be on AWS**. It is important to note that your Simpplr instance must be migrated to AWS if it is built on Salesforce.
*   Admin permissions in both Simpplr and Glean.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#step-by-step-instructions)

Step-by-step instructions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  In Glean, navigate to **Admin console** > **Add data source** > and select **Simpplr**.
2.  Enter a name and icon to your data source. Results for this data source will appear to all teammates under this name and icon.
3.  Provide the following configuration details about your Simpplr instance:
    *   API Hostname
    *   UI Hostname
    *   Client ID
    *   Client Secret

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#determine-simpplr-hostnames-and-credentials)

Determine Simpplr hostnames and credentials

This connector requires two distinct hostnames from your Simpplr environment and a dedicated OAuth 2.0 application.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#obtain-hostnames-api-and-ui)

Obtain hostnames (API and UI)

You must identify two different hostnames for the connector’s internal and external functions.

1.  **Determine the API Hostname:**
    1.  **Action:** Consult your Simpplr API documentation or admin settings to find the exact backend endpoint used for communication. The general format of Simpplr API URLs is: `https://<api-hostname>/v1/...`
    2.  **Purpose:** This hostname is used by the connector for backend API calls and authentication.
    3.  _Example Format:_ `platform.app.simpplr.com` or `platform.eu.simpplr.com`
2.  **Determine the UI Hostname:**
    1.  **Action:** Open Simpplr in your browser and check the URL bar. The hostname is the value between `https://` and the first single slash (`/`). The general format of Simpplr user URLs is: `https://<ui-hostname>/site/...`
    2.  **Purpose:** This hostname represents the user-facing URL for content links in Glean.
    3.  _Example Format:_ `mycompany.app.simpplr.com` or `intranet.mycompany.com`

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#generate-and-provide-credentials)

Generate and provide credentials

You must create an OAuth 2.0 Client Application in Simpplr to obtain the required access keys. For more information, see [Creating the Client Application](https://platform.simpplr.com/reference/creating-the-client-application)
.

1.  **Create the client application:**
    1.  Log in to Simpplr as an **App Manager**.
    2.  Select **Application settings** > **Application** > **Integrations** > **Client application**.
    3.  Click **Add client application** and select the **Client Credentials** type.
    4.  Provide a unique name and ID for the application (e.g., “Glean Connector”).
    5.  Click **Save**.
2.  **Copy credentials to Glean:**
    1.  **Copy** the newly generated **Client ID** and **Client Secret** provided by Simpplr.
    2.  **Paste** these credentials into the respective input fields in the Glean setup page.
    3.  **Click Save** in Glean to complete the configuration.

Glean will fetch an access token (valid 24h) and test connection automatically. Once connected, Glean will begin crawling and periodically sync changes.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#stop-crawling-of-previous-simpplr-salesforce-setup)

Stop crawling of previous Simpplr (Salesforce) setup
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Once crawls from the new Simpplr setup completes, you can stop crawling of the Salesforce setup.
*   You can delete previous data source once results from the new data source are visible in Glean search results.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/simpplr/home#frequently-asked-questions-faqs)

Frequently asked questions (FAQs)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Q: What happens to existing indexed Simpplr (Salesforce) data?**  
A: Once migrated to AWS, the legacy Salesforce connector won’t update content. Historic data may linger if the old instance is left online, but will eventually disappear. **Q: When should previous datasource needs to be stopped/deleted?**  
A: As soon as crawls for the new datasource is completed, you can stop crawling the Salesforce instances of Simpplr connector. Then verify some search results on Glean for the new datasource. Once verified, you can delete previous datasource. **Q: What will happen if the previous datasource is not deleted once the new datasource starts working?**  
A: If this is not done, then there will be duplicated data in search results. **Q: Are permissions respected?**  
A: Yes, all Simpplr sites and membership permissions are honored. Search results are only shown to people with access in Simpplr. Permissions rely on site membership lists. **Q: Do deleted users or content show up?**  
A: No. When a user or item is deactivated or deleted in Simpplr, it disappears from Glean’s index at the next sync.

YesNo

[Slack connectors overview](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/slack/about)

Search...

Collections

How Collections Work

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [What are Collections?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#what-are-collections)
    
*   [Create a Collection](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#create-a-collection)
    
*   [Add items to a Collection](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#add-items-to-a-collection)
    
*   [From the collection page](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#from-the-collection-page)
    
*   [From the search results page](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#from-the-search-results-page)
    
*   [Add descriptions to collection items](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#add-descriptions-to-collection-items)
    
*   [See Collections in search results](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#see-collections-in-search-results)
    
*   [View all collections across your company](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#view-all-collections-across-your-company)
    
*   [Organize Collections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#organize-collections)
    
*   [Edit sharing permissions on Collections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#edit-sharing-permissions-on-collections)
    
*   [Questions? Feature requests?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#questions-feature-requests)
    

![Collections Overview](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=10dc0f03ac908eb49819fdef271eb288)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#what-are-collections)

What are Collections?
===============================================================================================================================

Collections are an easy way to organize related documents across all your tools by a single topic. You can think of them as a way to curate a set of documents for other teammates to find and use easily. **Have a big launch coming up?** Group that planning doc, Jira epic, design folder, and marketing communications in a single collection, so your cross-functional team stays aligned. **Have someone new joining your team?** Organize all the onboarding information that a new employee needs to get up and running into a collection, and share the link with them on their first day. **Have documents related to a horizontal or vertical scattered everywhere?** Group those docs into a collection so your teammates can find what they need quickly. The use cases are endless! Let’s take a look at how collections work.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#create-a-collection)

Create a Collection
============================================================================================================================

Open the [Collections dashboard](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/collections)
, and click “New Collection” in the upper right-hand corner. Then create the collection’s title (choose something that will describe its contents), select an emoji to be used as the collection’s icon and then enter a description. Don’t worry if you don’t have the perfect idea for these yet! You can change them any time.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#add-items-to-a-collection)

Add items to a Collection
========================================================================================================================================

![Adding Items to Collections](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=7b1201be38a77a0f6efac7a44c5839dd)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#from-the-collection-page)

From the collection page
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can add links to a collection by clicking on one of the five options in the ”+ Add ” drop-down menu seen above. Clicking “Document” will pull up a search box that allows you to look for any files you want that Glean indexes. Click on the “Add to Collection” button next to a search result to add it to the collection. From this view, you can add multiple items to a collection at once: keep searching and adding until you’ve found everything you want to include. (There is no technical limit on the number of items in a collection, but we recommend keeping them to no more than 30 items for readability purposes.) You can also add content you can’t find searching Glean by using the “🌐URL” option in that same drop-down menu. This helps organize content like external media mentions or sites that don’t make sense to include in Glean’s index, like an expense reporting system or benefits portal. When added via search, Glean will automatically populate the item’s title, offer an integrated preview on the collection page, and show that the item is tagged in a collection on search results so users can easily find related information. Items added to a collection via URL will only show a link and will not include the integrated preview or search result tags.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#from-the-search-results-page)

**From the search results page**
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Search to pull up the document(s) you want to add to your collection. Hover over the document, click on the three dot menu, and use the “**Add to Collection**” quick action from the results. ![Search Results Add to Collection](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=a2ef602326c2bd6a99323bb54588a723) You can add Answers and documents from connected workplace apps to Collections. In the modal that appears, you can quickly filter to add the document to a Collection that already exists or create a new one.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#add-descriptions-to-collection-items)

Add descriptions to collection items
==============================================================================================================================================================

Once an item is in a collection, you can give it a brief, one-paragraph description, so other people viewing the collection can understand its purpose and why it has been included. For example, if you’ve created an onboarding collection for your team, use these description fields to help guide people through the onboarding process, provide instructions about setting up their GitHub profile, or fill out a form to request office supplies.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#see-collections-in-search-results)

See Collections in search results
========================================================================================================================================================

When a document is part of a Collection, you’ll see the Collection on the search result. ![Collections in Search Results](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=6e5f82dd853b231f85e777e599c6dec1) You can also filter by Collection using the filters under the search box or use the search operator `app:collections`

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#view-all-collections-across-your-company)

View all collections across your company
======================================================================================================================================================================

You can view all of your company’s collections from the Collections dashboard, which can be [found here](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/collections/)
. From this dashboard, you can create new collections, search for collections other people have created using the “Filter” box directly above the gallery table, and delete collections. Deleting a Collection won’t delete or affect any of the underlying documents within it - it will just remove the Collection from Glean. ![Collections Dashboard](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-5.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=2a5ca12c421f29162e8912bf9ac15318)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#organize-collections)

Organize Collections
==============================================================================================================================

You can organize Collections into sections using [Headers](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers)
 or create [Subcollections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections)
 to nest collections within. ![Organizing Collections](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-6.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=0e6068b694c395f0bdf631758fc215dc)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#edit-sharing-permissions-on-collections)

Edit sharing permissions on Collections
====================================================================================================================================================================

You can update permissions for which teammates or departments should have edit access to the contents of any collection using the Share button. ![Sharing Permissions](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work-7.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=4e7463eacf0ac707c78ae5d8174b62ed)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work#questions-feature-requests)

Questions? Feature requests?
============================================================================================================================================

Get in touch with us via the Messenger 👉

*   [Choose an audience for your Pin](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#choose-an-audience-for-your-pin)
    
*   [View Pins in search results](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#view-pins-in-search-results)
    
*   [View Pins across your company](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#view-pins-across-your-company)
    
*   [Removing a Pin](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#removing-a-pin)
    

![Pins Overview](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=e1356d2dd07fd4ad1c66c86ff330221b)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#what-are-pins)

What are Pins?
===================================================================================================

Pins are results that always appear at the top of a search results page for a query and audience that you choose. Public pins appear for you and other users at your company, while private pins are only visible to you.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#create-a-pin)

Create a Pin
================================================================================================

You can create a pin from the search results page or the [pin dashboard](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/pins)
 in Glean’s knowledge tab.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#create-a-pin-from-a-search-results-page)

Create a Pin from a search results page
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

From the search results page for a query, hover over the result you would like to pin and click on the pushpin icon. ![Create Pin from Search](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=e81310f3ede2546e98c41dbeab813b5b)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#create-a-pin-from-the-pins-dashboard)

Create a Pin from the Pins dashboard
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Navigate to [Knowledge > Pins](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/pins)
. Click the New Pin button, and you’ll be prompted to search for and select the resource you would like to pin. ![Pins Dashboard](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=b516f192db8d1dcc5bf9f7c7a61e7443) You can input additional queries for your Pin by clicking Add a query.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#choose-an-audience-for-your-pin)

**Choose an audience for your Pin**
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once you’ve selected the result to pin, you’ll be able to specify the audience it shows up for. ![Pin Audience Selection](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=3f265f7d1008d8ec50fcf01df3a1ba8e)

*   Selecting “Only Me” will create a private pin that will only appear at the top of search results for you and nobody else.
*   Selecting “Teammates” will create a public pin that appears for other users within your company. The audience dropdown allows you to choose which departments at your company see the pin; leaving it blank will have the pin appear for everyone at your organization.

If you don’t see “Teammates” as an option, your organization’s Glean admin has locked public pin creation. Contact them to get access.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#view-pins-in-search-results)

View Pins in search results
==============================================================================================================================

If a Pin is visible to you for your query, you’ll see it at the top of the search results. Each pin will have the name of the person who pinned the result, as well as the person who last edited it. Private pins will show that they are only visible to you. Glean respects all source permissions for pinned items, so users will only see pins in search results when they have access to the underlying item.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#view-pins-across-your-company)

View Pins across your company
==================================================================================================================================

You can see all pins that you are in the audience for in the [pins dashboard](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/pins)
, including public and private pins. Admins and users with the pin moderator role will be able to see all public pins across the company as well.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/pins/how-pins-work#removing-a-pin)

Removing a Pin
====================================================================================================

To remove a Pin, click the more menu on the right hand side of the Pin on the [Pins dashboard](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/pins)
, and select “Delete Pin”. This button may be disabled depending on the permissions set for Pins by your Glean admin.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

About Glean

On this page

*   [Is Glean approved by IT?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#is-glean-approved-by-it)
    
*   [How did Glean get access to the apps I’m able to search over?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#how-did-glean-get-access-to-the-apps-i%E2%80%99m-able-to-search-over)

*   [I noticed that Glean has some results from my browser history. How did it get that?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#i-noticed-that-glean-has-some-results-from-my-browser-history-how-did-it-get-that)
    
*   [I have more questions, who can I talk to?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#i-have-more-questions-who-can-i-talk-to)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#is-glean-approved-by-it)

Is Glean approved by IT?
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Quite simply, yes. If you are using Glean at this point, your IT and Security team did a thorough review and approved the rollout of the product. We do not have a model where an end user can connect to Glean by themselves.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#how-did-glean-get-access-to-the-apps-i%E2%80%99m-able-to-search-over)

How did Glean get access to the apps I’m able to search over?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After your IT team approved rolling the product out, they worked with us to connect each of the different data sources.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#how-do-permissions-work-in-glean)

How do permissions work in Glean?
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean fully replicates the permissions on the original app. That means that you’ll never be able to search for something that you don’t already have access to.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#how-do-i-connect-more-apps)

How do I connect more apps?
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Message us via the ? button in the bottom right if you have a particular tool you want to connect. We can then work with your IT team on getting it set up.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#i-noticed-that-glean-has-some-results-from-my-browser-history-how-did-it-get-that)

I noticed that Glean has some results from my browser history. How did it get that?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you install the browser extension, Glean will use your history to make it easy to navigate to recent documents in enterprise apps that we do not have full support for such as Notes (e.g. Evernote), no-code apps (e.g. Airtable, Coda) and general collaboration (Notion, etc). This is based on your browser history and this data is personal to you so it is not stored anywhere else.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/first-day-faqs#i-have-more-questions-who-can-i-talk-to)

I have more questions, who can I talk to?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Just message us via the ? icon in the bottom right and we’d be happy to chat.

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

About Glean

Accessibility Statement

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

On this page

*   [Our goal](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#our-goal)
    
*   [Our approach](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#our-approach)
    
*   [Get in touch](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#get-in-touch)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#our-goal)

Our goal
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At Glean, our mission is to bring people the knowledge they need to make a difference in the world. This includes empowering individuals with disabilities by incorporating accessibility throughout our products and services. That’s why we’ve started an initiative to bring our web application into compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#our-approach)

Our approach
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean’s design process incorporates accessibility principles from the start. Our accessibility champions collaborate with our product and engineering teams throughout the development process. Our testing involves a variety of assistive technologies, including screen readers, magnifiers, and alternative navigation methods such as keyboard-only and voice control.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility#get-in-touch)

Get in touch
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We welcome any questions or feedback at [a11y@glean.com](mailto:a11y@glean.com)
. You can also chat with us directly from Glean using the Messenger. Please include information about the site, web application, desktop application, or mobile apps you’re trying to access and the assistive technology you’re using (e.g., NVDA with Chrome on Windows, VoiceOver with Firefox on Mac OS, etc…).

Looking for the original version of this page? You can find the archived version [here](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/accessibility/glean-accessibility-statement)
.

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

About Glean

Quick Start Guide

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

On this page

*   [What is Glean?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#what-is-glean)
    
*   [What can Glean do?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#what-can-glean-do)
    
*   [Access instructions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#access-instructions)
    
*   [Step 1: Get started](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#step-1-get-started)
    
*   [Step 2: Install the browser extension](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#step-2-install-the-browser-extension)
    
*   [Step 3: Optional enhancements](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#step-3-optional-enhancements)
    
*   [Tips & Tricks](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#tips-%26-tricks)
    
*   [Example prompts](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#example-prompts)
    
*   [Frequently Asked Questions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#frequently-asked-questions)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#what-is-glean)

What is Glean?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean provides an Enterprise Generative AI and Search solution connected directly to your company’s knowledge. This solution functions as your own secure AI work assistant. It is tailored to your workplace data to deliver insightful answers and automate tasks across your organization.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#what-can-glean-do)

What can Glean do?
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use Glean to perform the following tasks at work:

*   Quickly find information, summarize documents, draft content, pull meeting materials, make sense of long Slack threads, and more.
*   Chat conversationally to ask questions, iterate, and refine your requests for better answers.
*   Use Agents to automate common multi-step tasks and workflows and explore available Agents from the Help Center navigation.
*   Run Deep Research when you need longer-form, multi-source analysis.
*   Verify answers with source citations so you can trust results and click through to the originals.
*   Work from wherever you are with the browser extension like the sidebar search, address-bar search, new tab page and optional desktop quick chat.
*   Switch to World Knowledge mode when you need information outside your company resources.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#access-instructions)

Access instructions
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Access may vary by organization. Follow any specific internal steps provided by your admin.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#step-1-get-started)

Step 1: Get started

*   Go to [Glean](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/app.glean.com)
     in your browser and sign in with your work account.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#step-2-install-the-browser-extension)

Step 2: Install the browser extension

*   Installing the extension unlocks sidebar search from any page and integrated search in tools like Google Drive, Jira, and Confluence if enabled.
*   Install the browser extension from [Glean extension](https://app.glean.com/)
    , use the shortcut to open Glean as a sidebar in your browser:
    *   Command + J (Mac)
    *   Alt + J (Windows)
*   The extension can optionally set Glean as your browser’s _New Tab page_.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#step-3-optional-enhancements)

Step 3: Optional enhancements

*   Get the desktop app for dedicated chat access.
    *   Search excels when you know what to look for.
    *   Chat excels when you want synthesis or creation.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#tips-&-tricks)

Tips & Tricks
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  When asking a question or prompting Glean, be specific with your instructions. For more information, see [Example prompts](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/example-prompts)
    .
2.  Glean Chat is conversational. Keep the conversation going and ask follow-ups, add constraints, or request edits. For more information, see [A Beginner’s Guide to Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/a-beginner-s-guide-to-chat)
     .
3.  If you know which resource you want Glean to use to respond, use the ”@” symbol to tag the resource directly rather than including a URL.
4.  Glean Search and Glean Chat have slightly different capabilities and if one is not giving you the answer you need, explore the alternative to learn which one is better suited to your task.
5.  Enabling [New Tab Page](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/faq#how-do-i-use-glean-as-my-new-tab-page)
     and [enabling Glean Search](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/search-from-wherever-you-work)
     for G-Drive, Jira and Confluence. You need to install the [extension](https://app.glean.com/)
     which allows users to take advantage of several other Glean features, including searching Glean from your URL bar, and using sidebar search from where you can search Glean from any document/website. This helps you to stop losing time switching between apps looking for the information you need. Watch video [Glean video](https://www.loom.com/share/b02085989e3b4a8d8c0cd0f419602c6d?sid=06ac7c7d-7b61-42a5-a605-87868ae4700c)
     for more information.
6.  Use **Public Knowledge** mode when you need external context beyond the sources of your company.
7.  Explore Agents to automate multi-step workflows and tool-based actions. For more information, see [Glean Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
    .

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#example-prompts)

Example prompts
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In this video, we demonstrate how to effectively use prompt engineering to maximize the benefits of Glean’s generative AI features. By engaging in specific dialogues with [Glean Chat](https://app.glean.com/chat)
, we show how being clear and detailed in requests leads to more informative and organized outputs.

*   Summarize \[tag document\] for me. Add a section called “Key Takeaways” with 3 bullet points at the end of the summary.
*   Help me write a product requirements document on \[brief explanation about project and its goals\]. I want it to include sections called “Context” “Objective” and “Open Questions.”
*   I want to contact the person who is the subject matter expert on \[topic\] to ask about \[describe question\]. Who is the best person to talk to?
*   What is \[technical terminology\]? Explain it to me in simple terms as a non-technical person.
*   I am onboarding onto \[project or team name\]. I want to get up to speed and understand what’s happening at a high-level. Write a section that describes important historical context I should know, documents that I should read to get started, and people I should talk to.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/end-user-quick-start-guide#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Can people see what I'm searching?

No, your search history is private.

Will my private data or documents be searchable by others?

No, your private information remains private. Glean mirrors the permissions in our connected applications. For example, some Slack channels are private; only members of a private channel will see conversations shared in that channel.

How secure is my data with Glean? Do I need to be sensitive about sharing our trade secrets or private information like I would if I were using ChatGPT?

Glean operates under a strict agreement with your organization to ensure that your data is not used for training or testing purposes. Glean also uses LLM models that offer a zero-day retention policy.

Can I verify where Glean is getting its answers?

Yes! Glean’s answers reference the source materials within our content so you can always check that it is referencing the latest and greatest. For more information, see [Citations](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations)
.

What is Glean connected to?

The list of connectors is always evolving and is configured by your admin.

I want to provide feedback on how Glean responded to my question. How can I do that?

Giving a thumbs up and down button helps improve the quality of your future responses. When you can, leave comments so Glean’s team can investigate.

My colleague and I asked Glean the same question, but we got different answers: why?

Glean searches and generates a response based on the specific documents and resources that each person can access, what that person interacts with the most, and more, so answers can vary because the user’s context can vary.

What are Agents and how are they different from Chat?

Agents are purpose-built assistants you can select for specific tasks and workflows; Chat is an open-ended conversational interface for asking questions and generating content. Explore available Agents from the Help Center navigation.

Was this page helpful?

On this page

Improve search quality by giving feedback on results

Help us improve search quality at your company by giving feedback on results. Within your first few searches, you may see this little message in the top right corner asking for feedback about the quality of the results shown. You can share feedback using that button at any time to help us improve search quality for your entire company. We review this feedback regularly to inform our product roadmap. You can also message us anytime if you have feedback on search quality - your feedback is always welcome and appreciated!
