# Customizing your Glean profile - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Basics

Customizing your Glean profile

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Personal information:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#personal-information)
    
*   [Bio:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#bio)
    
*   [Collaboration widgets:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#collaboration-widgets)
    

Setting up your profile is easy to do in Glean. To get to your profile, click on your avatar in the top right corner of Glean, and click on your name. You can also get to your profile from the [Directory](http://app.glean.com/directory)
. To change your profile, click the edit button to the right of your name. That will enable editing all of the customizable elements within your profile, including your bio, contact information, banner photo and collaboration widgets. When you’re done, click the publish button at the top of the page to save your changes. Here is the breakdown of each editable profile attribute:

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#personal-information)

Personal information:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking the edit button to the right of your name once you’ve entered the profile editor will allow you to customize your preferred name, pronouns and links to external social networks that will appear alongside contact information from work systems.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#bio)

Bio:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clicking the edit button to the right of the “bio” label will allow you to create a custom bio for yourself that all users will see when visiting your profile. Bios can include rich text, headings, lists, images and hyperlinks, so be creative! Remember to click the “Apply” button below your bio to save your changes. After applying the changes to your bio, click the “Publish” button at the top of the page to save the changes to your profile.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/customizing-your-glean-profile#collaboration-widgets)

Collaboration widgets:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

On the right hand side of your profile, you’ll find the collaboration widgets:

*   **At a glance** will show your calendar availability (if a user has permission to view your calendar), and allow them to schedule a meeting with you.
*   The **related people** widget will show you and other users who you work with most closely, based on signals from the systems connected to Glean.
*   **Worked together on** will show another user browsing your profile what the two of you have worked on together.
*   **Recent activity** will show you the items you’ve worked on most recently. Other people browsing your profile will see the items you’ve worked on most recently that they have permission to view. That way, if you have worked on a document that isn’t shared with them, they won’t see it in that widget.

If you want to hide any of these widgets from appearing on your profile, click the toggle on the bottom right-hand corner of the widget. Users with more than 50 total reports will have the related people and recent activity widgets disabled by default. To save these changes to your profile, click the “Publish” button at the top of the page.

# Info Access - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Assistant

Info Access

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Glean Search](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#glean-search)
    
*   [Glean Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#glean-chat)
    
*   [Influencing Results](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#influencing-results)
    

Glean gathers information from multiple sources to create unique responses based on several inputs, such as your access permissions, search and chat settings, prompt, and any additional information you provide in the chat box. Just as every person is unique, so are their search results! Glean offers two distinct methods for searching content: Glean Chat and Glean Search. Depending on your needs, you might prefer one over the other.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#glean-search)

Glean Search

Glean Search functions like a traditional search engine. You enter a query and receive results that you can explore independently. This method is useful when you want to browse through various documents and information sources.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#glean-chat)

Glean Chat

Glean Chat acts as a conversational tool where you can ask questions and receive answers. There are two types of chat modes in Glean: Company Knowledge and World Knowledge:

*   **Company Knowledge** Uses the underlying large language model alongside your accessible company data to provide responses. When using Company Knowledge, Glean’s sources include the LLM’s inherent knowledge and the files within your company that you have access to.
*   **World Knowledge** Uses the underlying large language model to provide responses based on general world knowledge. In World Mode, you can enhance the LLM’s understanding by selecting the Web Search option when typing your query, allowing Glean to search the internet for answers and incorporate the results into its responses. When using World Knowledge, Glean’s sources include the LLM’s inherent knowledge and the internet.

You can control what information Glean accesses by selecting **Company Knowledge**, **World Knowledge**, or the **Web Search** option with world knowledge in the chat box.

Web search is only available if your administrator has enabled it.

Glean personalizes search results based on your activity and the permissions set in your company’s data sources. This means that the results you see are tailored to the documents and information you have access to and have interacted with. For example, if you frequently access certain documents or collaborate with specific colleagues, those documents and related content are more likely to appear in your search results. Your coworker might have different access permissions or have interacted with different documents, leading to different responses. To better understand how Glean integrates information based on your selections, refer to the following diagram: ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/assistant/different-chat-modes.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=b173d2272163c2394df71494685e2758)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/how-glean-accesses-info#influencing-results)

Influencing Results

You can create collections and verify documents to influence the likelihood they will be used in results. Glean respects the permissions set in your company’s data sources. If you have permission to view a document in Google Drive or a thread in a public Slack channel, it will appear in your search results. If your coworker has different permissions, they might see different results. Additionally, if any permissions change, Glean’s results will reflect those changes quickly. Glean’s responses can vary even for the same query, depending on the chat settings and the context of previous interactions. By understanding these features, you can effectively utilize Glean Search and Glean Chat to find the information you need efficiently.

On this page

*   [Content Updates](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/faq#content-updates)
    
*   [Home Page](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/faq#home-page)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/faq#content-updates)

Content Updates
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

How long does it take for new data to be updated in Glean?

Let’s say a teammate created a new document a few minutes ago, or a customer opened a new ticket today. How long will it take for these changes to appear in Glean?The answer depends on the specific app. For most apps (Jira, Confluence, Slack, etc), their APIs enable us to index the new content within 15 mins of the content changing.For other apps like Zendesk and Salesforce, due to API restrictions we index content changes within an hour of the change.Have more questions or seeing delays in content showing up in your search?Message us for help and if you feel comfortable, please share URLs of example content that are not showing up so we can debug.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/faq#home-page)

Home Page
------------------------------------------------------------------------

How do I use Glean as my new tab page?

Keep Glean close at hand plus get quick access to your calendar and work tools.Here’s how to enable it:

1.  First, check you have the Glean chrome extension installed by [heading here](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/installing-the-browser-extension)
    .
2.  Second, head to [Your account](https://app.glean.com/settings)
     and enable “New tab page” under “General”. ![Glean Settings](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/basics/faq-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=f7b6584dc0c91b2d57b87f4f68f15296)

You can also enable or disable Glean as your new tab page from the Home page settings icon directly on the Glean new tab page:![Glean Home Page Settings](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/basics/faq-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=b55254d3961e79f9eaf56ae38a9fd9c7)

How can I get my old new tab page back?

Although the Glean new tab page is an essential part of finding what you need from all your tools and quickly accessing relevant content, we understand sometimes you just want your old new tab back.After you log in to Glean, on the home or new tab page, click the Home page settings icon in the top right corner. In the right panel, you can turn off Glean as your new tab entirely by flipping the toggle.![Glean Home Page Settings Toggle](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/basics/faq-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=a84f73dee1cd582429cec0275df69082)If you had a previous extension that replaced your new tab and prefer that over Glean, you’ll have to uninstall the extension that gave you that previous new tab experience (Momentum, Scratchpad, Earth View, etc). Then, reinstall that same extension and your previous new tab should be restored.After you’ve reset your new tab, don’t forget that there are other entry points for Glean!

*   Typing gl in the address bar followed by your search
*   CMD + J for the sidebar
*   [app.glean.com](https://app.glean.com/)
    
*   [Desktop app](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app)

Quickly resolve the most difficult cases.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Search for error codes and past mentions of issues, so you can find what you need to close cases faster. ![glean-for-support-0](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/about/glean-for-support-0.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=8dd7f2ad3079ac736c0dd2707ac4593d)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/glean-for-support#accelerate-response-efficiency)

Accelerate response efficiency.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Respond with confidence knowing the content you find is verified as up-to-date. ​ ![glean-for-support-1](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/about/glean-for-support-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=6d974458839f47ddea93718baf72b0e2)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/glean-for-support#reduce-time-for-onboarding)

Reduce time for onboarding.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get up and running quickly with content collections and answers for new products and releases. ![glean-for-support-2](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/about/glean-for-support-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=7dafcee6c203df8f6d1afe79dabfdae5)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/glean-for-support#use-glean-wherever-you-work)

Use Glean wherever you work
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use our handy ⌘J / ALT-J shortcut to search in our sidebar without missing a beat on your customer issue. ![glean-for-support-3](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/about/glean-for-support-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=d1a67c98ca42ece4b5881903c8689b6c)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/glean-for-support#quickly-navigate-to-known-docs)

Quickly navigate to known docs
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Know exactly what you’re looking for? Glean is a faster way to navigate to your workplace documents. ![glean-for-support-4](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/about/glean-for-support-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=ad8f72aba217850c30c7a0b80d660d79)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/glean-for-support#learn-about-your-teammates)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/installing-the-browser-extension#downloading-browser-extension)

Downloading browser extension
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Install the Glean browser extension to use the sidebar, new tab, and companion features. You have two options to install the extension:

*   Visit [https://app.glean.com/setup](https://app.glean.com/setup)
     and follow the guided installation process for your browser.
*   Install the extension through your browser’s extension app store:
    *   [Chrome Web Store](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/glean/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)
        
    *   [Firefox Browser Add-ons](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/glean/)
        
    *   [Safari](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/glean-for-safari/id6444195239)
        
    *   [Edge](https://microsoftedge.microsoft.com/addons/detail/glean/lkhkfgdfpoijiiinpjgcgacdkgjeieak)

# Chat Best Practices - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Assistant

Chat Best Practices

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Additional Best Practices](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#additional-best-practices)
    

This guide outlines best practices for interacting with Glean Chat, AI apps, and Search to ensure you receive the most accurate and relevant results.

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#)

Use Question Marks

Always conclude questions with a question mark (?). This punctuation helps Glean recognize that you’re seeking information or clarification.

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#)

Specify Connector Names

Include the relevant connector name in your question for more precise results.For example, instead of: “Provide me some sample support tickets”Use: “Can you provide me some sample support tickets from Zendesk?”

![Example of search without connector name specification](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-/images/chat/chat.20240204194215597.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-&q=85&s=3a1ec09c6348796e275670b433f4d608)

![Example of search with connector name specification](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-/images/chat/ai-answers.20240204194356907.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-&q=85&s=587055026fefe8d21ded54e981625b94)

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#)

Use Application Filters

Filter by application to narrow down search results and enhance precision.

![Application filter example](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-/images/chatfilterdatasource.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-&q=85&s=45eebd788b97c8c6b5eb699aa07d4a7f)

4

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#)

Utilize Connector Parameters

For global searches, use connector names as search parameters to find specific results. Use the format `app:connectorname` (e.g., “app:zendesk” or “app:gdrive”).

![Connector parameter search example](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-/images/chatbestpractice2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-&q=85&s=0b5dd300b1d04c27347b8f547e486dfd)

5

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#)

Tag Relevant Documents

To ask questions about a specific document, enter the URL of the document or type in @ followed by the name of the document.

![Tag documents example](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/paIC5vpLKUOP9Yk7/images/chattagdocuments.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=paIC5vpLKUOP9Yk7&q=85&s=3f08895c1b4d2cdb958a2be1af271fec)

6

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#)

Access Worldwide Knowledge

Toggle to “Search the web” mode to expand your search to worldwide knowledge.

![Public Knowledge toggle](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-/images/chatbestpractice3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-&q=85&s=de27806d2d14a1f3621f802047ba9ffa)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices#additional-best-practices)

Additional Best Practices
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use Clear Language
------------------

Write questions in clear, simple language and avoid slang or abbreviations.**Instead of:** “What’s the ROI on implementing AI-driven CRM?”**Better:** “Could you explain the expected benefits of using AI in customer relationship management systems?”

Break Down Complex Questions
----------------------------

If your question involves multiple parts, consider breaking it down into smaller, more focused questions to get more accurate responses.

Provide Context
---------------

Include relevant background information to help Glean understand your question better and provide more relevant answers.

Use Quotation Marks for Emphasis
--------------------------------

Place important keywords in double quotes to emphasize them in your search. For example: `Please provide recent customer tickets for the "XYZ" customer from the ticketing tool or datasource "ABC".`

If you need assistance, please reach out to our support team by submitting a request through our [support portal](https://support.glean.comhttps//support.glean.com/hc/en-us)
.

On this page

*   [Glean browser extension sidebar](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#glean-browser-extension-sidebar)
    
*   [Using the sidebar screenshot](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#using-the-sidebar-screenshot)

*   [Knowledge tools](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#knowledge-tools)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#glean-browser-extension-sidebar)

Glean browser extension sidebar
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Glean browser extension sidebar enhances your research and discovery capabilities directly within your web browser on both indexed and unindexed web pages. It consolidates essential functionalities into an interface accessible via keyboard shortcuts or the Glean icon. The sidebar features two distinct tabs:

*   **Chat tab:** Provides an AI assistant that uses contextual and company knowledge to enhance research.
*   **Search tab:** Enables searches for relevant content, documents, and experts.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#using-the-sidebar-screenshot)

Using the sidebar screenshot
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Capture and use content that can’t be copied or highlighted on the web page.

*   Screenshot images, non-selectable text, or specific sections from the current page in just a few clicks.
*   Focus on part of a page and bring more content into Glean’s chat and tools.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#accessing-the-sidebar)

Accessing the sidebar
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are three ways to access the Glean browser extension sidebar:

*   Use the keyboard shortcut: Command + J (Mac) or Alt + J (Windows).
*   Click the blue Glean icon on the sticky tab on the right side of the screen.
*   Navigate to the Glean extension icon (puzzle piece) in the navigation bar.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/extension-sidebar#knowledge-tools)

Knowledge tools
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The content tab provides knowledge tools that enhance your ability to organize and access information:

*   **Collections:** Add or view collections this document is relevant to, helping you organize related content for easy discovery later.
*   **Go Links:** Create or view Go Links to the current page you are on, enabling quick access through memorable shortcuts.

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Search

Browser History Results

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

With the [Glean Extension](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/installing-the-browser-extension)
, you can see results and suggestions from certain workplace apps in your browser history. Because these are history-based results, you will only be able to view content you’ve seen before in these workplace apps. Glean will search across the titles of the pages you’ve visited. Results from your browser history are completely secure and private to you. Only you will see these results. Your coworkers and company will not see these results. You will be able to see results from these workplace apps if they appear in your browser history:

*   Aha
*   Airtable
*   Bynder
*   Clubhouse (Shortcut)
*   Coda
*   Datadog
*   DocuSign
*   Evernote
*   Freshservice
*   GCP
*   Google Datastudio
*   Google Sites
*   Invision
*   Jenkins
*   JFrog
*   Lucid
*   Miro
*   OpsGenie
*   Phabricator
*   Quip
*   Smartsheet
*   Tableau
*   Testrail
*   Trello
*   Workday
*   Zeplin

Looking for the original version of this page? You can find the archived version [here](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/faq/browser-history-apps)
.

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

Navigation

Glean Assistant

Contextual Images

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Key benefits](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#key-benefits)
    
*   [Example use cases](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#example-use-cases)
    
*   [How it works](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#how-it-works)
    
*   [Permissions & privacy](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#permissions-%26-privacy)
    
*   [Frequently asked questions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#frequently-asked-questions)
    
*   [Further reading](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#further-reading)
    

**Contextual Images** is a feature in Glean Assistant that surfaces diagrams, charts, screenshots, and other images found in your company’s indexed documents directly in chat responses. These visuals appear inline with supporting answers, helping you quickly understand technical concepts or visualize referenced content without leaving your workflow.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#key-benefits)

Key benefits

*   **Instant Visual Context:** See diagrams, process maps, and screenshots exactly when you need them.
*   **Improved Collaboration:** Reduce back-and-forth over missing visuals during technical troubleshooting or brainstorming.
*   **Time Savings:** No need to open multiple files or search folders for referenced images.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#example-use-cases)

Example use cases

*   Requesting a system architecture diagram for a product or service.
*   Viewing a whiteboard photo from a design review captured in a meeting notes doc.
*   Troubleshooting a support issue with step-by-step screenshots extracted from internal documentation.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#how-it-works)

How it works

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#)

Scan Data Sources

Glean Assistant scans your connected data sources (Google Drive, SharePoint, and OneDrive) for images that match your query context.

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#)

Display Relevant Images

When an image is relevant and you have viewing permissions, it appears automatically in your Assistant response.

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#)

Support Multiple Formats

Images are supported if they are standalone files (PNG, JPG, GIF, etc.) or embedded inside Google Docs, PDFs, Word, and PowerPoint documents (except Google Drawings).

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#permissions-&-privacy)

Permissions & privacy

*   You will _only_ see images if you have access to the underlying source document.
*   The feature honors your company’s sharing and security policies automatically.

This feature may not be available for your deployment. Please contact your admin if you do not see images rendered.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently asked questions

Can users upload their own images?

Contextual Images surfaces existing images from indexed sources; user uploads via chat are not currently supported for rendering.

Which image formats are supported?

Standard image formats (PNG, JPG, GIF, etc.) from Google Drive, OneDrive, SharePoint, and those embedded in PDFs, DOCX, and PPTX files are supported (excluding Google Drawings).

Do I have to do anything to prompt images?

Just ask a question; for best results, reference the concept or process you want to visualize (e.g., “Show me the pipeline diagram for project Mercury”).

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images#further-reading)

Further reading

For further details, troubleshooting tips, and admin cost controls, visit:

*   [Contextual Images Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/assistant/features/contextual-images)
    
*   [Community and Support](https://community.glean.com/)
    

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[Image Generation in Glean Assistant](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation)

# Why does the Chrome extension ask for those permissions? - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Apps

Why does the Chrome extension ask for those permissions?

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Read and change all your data on websites you visit](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#read-and-change-all-your-data-on-websites-you-visit)
    
*   [Replace the page you see when opening a new tab](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#replace-the-page-you-see-when-opening-a-new-tab)
    
*   [Read and change your browsing history](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#read-and-change-your-browsing-history)
    
*   [Read and change your bookmarks](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#read-and-change-your-bookmarks)
    

You might’ve noticed our Chrome Extension asks for several permissions you might be unfamiliar with, so let’s break down the details together.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#read-and-change-all-your-data-on-websites-you-visit)

Read and change all your data on websites you visit

Glean has a variety of features that enhance the user experience and shown in the native tool. Some examples are:

*   Native search replacement where Glean powers search directly in tools like Atlassian and Drive
*   Our Glean sticky tab appears on workplace apps where you can verify a document.
*   To use [Go Links](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-go-links-work)
    , it asks for an additional permission to read and change your data on “go,” which enables redirection to shortened links

In order to provide these features, it requires this broad permission – but the access is limited to only workplace applications that work with Glean.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#replace-the-page-you-see-when-opening-a-new-tab)

Replace the page you see when opening a new tab

We replace your new tab page with our search experience so you can bring up anything from any tool you use in an instant. You can still search Google from the browser’s URL bar or disable Glean acting as the new tab at any time from the settings.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#read-and-change-your-browsing-history)

Read and change your browsing history

We only use the “read” part of this permission so we can autocomplete search results for you and and improve search result ranking for individual users. The system only uses history from the workplace apps you’ve connected to Glean and other well known productivity apps that we don’t currently index (e.g. Notion, Airtable, etc), and ignore all other history. This data will never be shared with any 3rd party.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/why-does-the-chrome-extension-ask-for-those-permissions#read-and-change-your-bookmarks)

Read and change your bookmarks

We show a dismissible bookmarks bar on our new tab page, since replacing Chrome’s default new tab page also disables the Google bookmarks feature which only shows the bookmarks bar on a new tab.

On this page

*   [What is Glean?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#what-is-glean)
    
*   [How did Glean become my new tab page?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#how-did-glean-become-my-new-tab-page)
    
*   [How can I get my old new tab page back?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#how-can-i-get-my-old-new-tab-page-back)

*   [I want to go back to my previous custom new tab (e.g. Momentum, Scratchpad, Earth View):](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#i-want-to-go-back-to-my-previous-custom-new-tab-e-g-momentum-scratchpad-earth-view-)
    
*   [How can I continue to use Glean?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#how-can-i-continue-to-use-glean)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#what-is-glean)

What is Glean?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean is the AI-powered workplace assistant that searches all of your apps across your entire company, understanding context, language behavior, and relationships with others, to find personalized answers to all your workplace questions. You can read more about what we do and how to use us [here](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#how-did-glean-become-my-new-tab-page)

How did Glean become my new tab page?
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Glean extension was installed into your browser by your company which has vetted the product’s effectiveness, security, and ability to save you time.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#how-can-i-get-my-old-new-tab-page-back)

How can I get my old new tab page back?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Although the Glean new tab page is an essential part of finding what you need from all your tools and quickly accessing relevant content, we understand sometimes you just want your old new tab back.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#i-want-to-go-back-to-my-old-blank-browser-new-tab)

I want to go back to my old blank browser new tab:

After you log in to Glean, on the home or new tab page, click the **Home page** **settings icon** in the top right corner to open the settings panel. From there, you can turn off Glean as your new tab entirely by flipping the toggle.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#i-want-to-go-back-to-my-previous-custom-new-tab-e-g-momentum-scratchpad-earth-view-)

I want to go back to my previous custom new tab (e.g. Momentum, Scratchpad, Earth View):

If you had a previous extension that replaced your new tab and prefer that over Glean, you’ll have to uninstall the extension that gave you that previous new tab experience (Momentum, Scratchpad, Earth View, etc). Then, reinstall that same extension and your previous new tab should be restored.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-extension-via-company-push#how-can-i-continue-to-use-glean)

How can I continue to use Glean?

After you’ve reset your new tab, don’t forget that there are other entry points for Glean!

*   Typing \*\*`gl`\*\*in the address bar followed by your search
*   CMD + J for the sidebar
*   [app.glean.com](https://app.glean.com/)
    
*   [Desktop app](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app)

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Assistant

Memory and personalization

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [How memory works](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#how-memory-works)
    
*   [Memory categories](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#memory-categories)
    
*   [Benefits of memory](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#benefits-of-memory)
    
*   [Manage your memories](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#manage-your-memories)
    
*   [View memories in Settings](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#view-memories-in-settings)
    
*   [Add memories using chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#add-memories-using-chat)
    
*   [View memories using chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#view-memories-using-chat)
    
*   [Delete memories](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#delete-memories)
    
*   [Runtime controls in chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#runtime-controls-in-chat)
    
*   [Privacy and security](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#privacy-and-security)
    
*   [Known limitations](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#known-limitations)
    
*   [Frequently asked questions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#frequently-asked-questions)
    

Assistant memory is a persistent, privacy-safe continuity layer that enables Glean Assistant to remember your preferences, work context, and ongoing projects. Memory helps the Assistant provide more relevant, personalized responses without requiring you to repeat information across sessions.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#how-memory-works)

How memory works
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean Assistant uses two types of memory to personalize your experience:

*   **Saved memories**: Explicit, user-controlled information you tell the Assistant to remember (for example, “Remember that I prefer concise answers” or “I’m a Sales Engineer in EMEA”).
*   **Extracted memories**: Implicit insights mined from your chat sessions and work activity, within retention guardrails. These include your response preferences, role and responsibilities, active projects, and recent topics.

Memory improves response quality and efficiency for tasks that benefit from personalization, including tone, style, role awareness, and continuity across conversations.

Memory does not grant the Assistant access to documents you cannot access. Memory only stores user-level facts and preferences.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#memory-categories)

Memory categories
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Assistant organizes memories into the following categories:

| Category | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Role and responsibilities** | A summary of your role, job function, and key areas of focus |
| **Response preferences** | Your preferred tone (concise vs. detailed), formatting (bullets, tables), formality level, and style |
| **Active projects** | Current projects and initiatives you’re working on |
| **Recent topics** | Topics and focus areas from your recent work activity |
| **Other** | Additional context and facts that don’t fit other categories |

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#benefits-of-memory)

Benefits of memory
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Memory helps you work more efficiently by:

*   **Reducing repetition**: You don’t need to re-explain your role, preferences, or project context in each conversation.
*   **Providing continuity**: The Assistant remembers context from previous discussions, so you can continue where you left off.
*   **Personalizing responses**: Responses match your preferred communication style and level of detail.
*   **Improving relevance**: Recommendations and suggestions are tailored to your work focus areas and past requests.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#manage-your-memories)

Manage your memories
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can view and delete your saved memories from Settings, and you can view, add, and edit memories directly in chat.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#view-memories-in-settings)

View memories in Settings

1.  Click your profile icon and select **Settings**.
2.  Select **Personalization**.
3.  Review your memories organized by category.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#add-memories-using-chat)

Add memories using chat

Tell the Assistant what to remember using natural language:

*   “Remember that I prefer comprehensive responses in tabular format”
*   “Remember that I work on the enterprise sales team”
*   “Remember that I always want code examples in Python”

The Assistant confirms when it saves a memory.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#view-memories-using-chat)

View memories using chat

Ask the Assistant what it remembers about you:

*   “What do you remember about me?”
*   “Show me my saved memories”

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#delete-memories)

Delete memories

You can delete individual memories or all memories: **Delete a specific memory:**

*   In Settings > Personalization, click the delete icon next to the memory you want to remove.
*   In chat, tell the Assistant: “Forget that I prefer responses in bulleted format”

**Delete all memories:**

*   In Settings > Personalization, click **Delete all** to remove all memories in a category.

Deleted memories may be regenerated from your activity data during the weekly memory update cycle.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#runtime-controls-in-chat)

Runtime controls in chat

You can control memory usage during a conversation using natural language:

*   “Don’t use memory in this chat”
*   “Use memory for this response”
*   “What memories are you using right now?”

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#privacy-and-security)

Privacy and security
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Memory is designed with privacy and security as core principles:

*   **User-level data**: Memories are stored at the individual user level. There is no cross-user memory sharing.
*   **No document access**: Memory stores facts and preferences, not document content. Memory does not grant access to documents you don’t have permission to view.
*   **Retention policies**: Extracted memories are persisted for 30 days and regenerated weekly. Saved memories persist until you delete them.
*   **Sensitive data**: The system does not extract sensitive personal attributes. Extraction is scoped to work context and preferences.
*   **Admin controls**: Your organization’s admin can configure memory settings, including enabling or disabling memory features at the organization level.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#known-limitations)

Known limitations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Possible duplication**: You may see duplicate entries in memory views. These are deduplicated during the weekly update cycle.
*   **Deleted memories may reappear**: Extracted memories may be regenerated from activity data after deletion.
*   **Fast Mode**: Memories cannot be created or referenced when using Fast Mode.
*   **Memory edits in UI**: User memory edits through chat commands are supported; edits directly in the Settings UI are view-only except for deletion.
*   **Weekly updates**: Extracted memories are regenerated weekly. Changes may not appear immediately.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/memory-personalization#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently asked questions
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

How is memory different from chat history?

Chat history stores your previous conversations so you can reference them later. Memory extracts and stores durable facts, preferences, and context that persist across all conversations, not just within a single chat thread.

Will the Assistant use memory in agent conversations?

Yes, agents can access your memories to provide personalized responses, subject to the agent’s configuration and your memory settings.

Can my admin see my memories?

Your admin can configure memory settings for the organization but cannot view individual user memories. Memories are private to each user.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[Installing the browser extension](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/installing-the-browser-extension)

On this page

*   [Who can use image generation](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#who-can-use-image-generation)

*   [Occasional link-only responses](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#occasional-link-only-responses)
    
*   [Activity-based prompts and broken links](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#activity-based-prompts-and-broken-links)
    
*   [Image safety and blocked requests](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#image-safety-and-blocked-requests)
    
*   [Frequently asked questions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#frequently-asked-questions)
    
*   [Further reading](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#further-reading)
    

Glean Assistant includes built-in image generation, allowing you to create custom visuals from natural-language prompts without leaving your workflow. Whether you need to illustrate concepts, create quick mockups, or add visuals to documentation, you can generate images alongside your questions and tasks.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#who-can-use-image-generation)

Who can use image generation
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Image generation is available to:

*   **Glean Key customers on GCP** with access to Glean Assistant and Agents
*   End users in tenants where the feature is enabled

Admins can enable or disable image generation tenant-wide from:

> **Admin Console** → **Assistant** → **Settings** → **Image generation**

From the end user’s perspective, there’s nothing to install. If your admin has enabled the feature, you’ll see image responses directly in Glean Assistant.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#where-you-can-generate-images)

Where you can generate images
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use image generation anywhere you use Glean Assistant:

*   **Assistant chat** in the Glean web application
*   **Plan & Execute note** in Agents when creating or refining multi-step plans

Image generation works in both:

*   **Fast mode** – optimized for speed
*   **Thinking mode** – optimized for deeper reasoning and richer use of your enterprise context

You don’t need to change modes or tools to access image generation. Simply ask the assistant for an image in your prompt.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#how-to-generate-an-image)

How to generate an image
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You generate images using natural language prompts, just like any other question to the assistant.

1

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#)

Open Glean Assistant

Navigate to Glean Assistant in your Glean application.

2

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#)

Write your prompt

Type a prompt that clearly asks for an image. Specify style, level of realism, audience, or any other constraints.

3

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#)

Submit and review

Submit your prompt. The assistant will generate the image using Nano Banana Pro and return it inline in the chat, typically with a link to view or download.

4

[](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#)

Download or refine

Click the image or link to open it in full size, then download it, paste it into docs or slides, or regenerate with updated instructions.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#example-prompts)

Example prompts

**Using your work context:**

*   _“Search for my scope and what I work on. Then generate an image that describes it visually.”_

This lets the assistant ground the image in your recent work—projects, artifacts, and topics tied to your account. **Purely creative:**

*   _“Generate a photorealistic image of a hamster flying a plane.”_

Good for illustrative or playful visuals where you don’t need enterprise context. **Explaining a concept:**

*   _“Create a simple diagram that explains the enterprise agent development lifecycle, with clear stages from value to launch and monitoring.”_

**Documentation support:**

*   _“Generate a clean, line-art style diagram that shows ‘My Work’ at the center with Security, Agents, Connectors, and Customer Outcomes around it, suitable for a product overview slide.”_

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#prompting-tips-and-best-practices)

Prompting tips and best practices
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To get better results from image generation:

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#be-explicit-about-subject-and-style)

Be explicit about subject and style

Specify visual style, detail level, and format to guide the model. _Examples: “flat illustration, minimal text, pastel colors” | “photorealistic” | “simple black-and-white line drawing”_

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#specify-the-audience)

Specify the audience

Tell the assistant who will view the image to adjust complexity and tone. _Examples: “for an executive audience” | “for a new engineer onboarding doc”_

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#limit-on-image-text)

Limit on-image text

Short labels or headings work best. Long paragraphs should live in your document, not on the image.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#iterate-with-feedback)

Iterate with feedback

Ask the assistant to refine instead of starting from scratch. _Examples: “regenerate with fewer details” | “simplify the diagram” | “change the color palette”_

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#use-your-enterprise-context-when-it-helps)

Use your enterprise context when it helps

For work-related visuals, reference your internal data so the assistant can ground the image in what you actually do. _Examples: “my recent docs” | “my roadmap” | “my team’s projects”_

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#credits-pricing-and-admin-controls)

Credits, pricing, and admin controls
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Credits:** Each generated image consumes Flex Credits, similar to other LLM-powered features.
*   **Visibility:** Image generation appears as a distinct line item in Admin Console usage and billing interfaces, so admins can track usage and adjust budgets or policies.
*   **Controls:** Admins can enable or disable image generation globally in Assistant settings and combine this with existing Assistant and Agents permissions for finer-grained control.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#known-limitations-and-quirks)

Known limitations and quirks
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Most of the time, images render directly in your chat. However, there are a few quirks to be aware of:

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#occasional-link-only-responses)

Occasional link-only responses

In some cases, you may see a clickable image link instead of an inline thumbnail. Clicking the link should still open the image in a new tab. If you open **“Show work”**, you may see a valid image link there even if the main response only shows the URL.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#activity-based-prompts-and-broken-links)

Activity-based prompts and broken links

Prompts that ask the assistant to analyze your recent activity or personal graph and then generate an image (for example, _“Use my personal graph over the last 3 months, then generate an image”_) can sometimes produce an image link that doesn’t load correctly or returns a browser error. **If this happens:**

*   Try regenerating with a simpler prompt that doesn’t rely on “my last N months of work”
*   If the problem persists, report it via the feedback controls in the chat (thumbs down with a brief description) so we can track it

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#image-safety-and-blocked-requests)

Image safety and blocked requests

Requests that violate safety policies (explicit content, hateful or harassing imagery, real-person impersonation, or sensitive personal data) may be refused or heavily modified. **For best results, avoid prompts that include:**

*   Real coworkers’ faces or full names
*   Sensitive customer data, secrets, or production credentials
*   Harmful, illegal, or discriminatory scenarios

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#frequently-asked-questions)

Frequently asked questions
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Can I upload an image and ask Glean to edit it?

Yes, you can attach an image and ask the assistant to adjust style, colors, or add/remove elements, subject to the same safety policies.

Which image model is used?

Image generation currently uses **Nano Banana Pro** by default. Additional models may be introduced over time for more advanced creative or marketing use cases; when available, they will be surfaced via the normal Assistant experience.

Where are images stored?

Generated images are stored and served through Glean’s image infrastructure and follow the same enterprise-grade security and access controls as other Glean content.

Do I need to switch modes to generate images?

No. Image generation is available in both Fast and Thinking modes, so you can choose speed or depth without losing access to image generation.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/image-generation#further-reading)

Further reading
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [A Beginner’s Guide to Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/a-beginner-s-guide-to-chat)
    
*   [Glean Assistant Best Practices](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices)
    
*   [Community and Support](https://community.glean.com/)
    

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[Contextual Images](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/contextual-images)
[Code Generation](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/code-generation)

⌘I

Glean Basics

How to search in Glean

On this page

*   [Filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#filters)

*   [2\. Find a person associated with the document with “from”](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-find-a-person-associated-with-the-document-with-%E2%80%9Cfrom%E2%80%9D)
    
*   [3\. Find specific document types with “type”](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#3-find-specific-document-types-with-%E2%80%9Ctype%E2%80%9D)
    
*   [4\. Find things you’ve previously viewed with “my history”](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#4-find-things-you%E2%80%99ve-previously-viewed-with-%E2%80%9Cmy-history%E2%80%9D)
    
*   [5\. Filter by app](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#5-filter-by-app)

*   [Search for teammates](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#search-for-teammates)

*   [2\. Learn more about a document owner](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-learn-more-about-a-document-owner)
    
*   [Search from wherever you work](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#search-from-wherever-you-work)

*   [2\. Search from any location](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-search-from-any-location)
    
*   [Share knowledge](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#share-knowledge)
    
*   [Advanced features](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#advanced-features)
    
*   [1\. Advanced filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#1-advanced-filters)
    
*   [2\. Filter out certain apps](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-filter-out-certain-apps)
    
*   [4\. Zoom meeting results](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#4-zoom-meeting-results)
    

We’ve put together a quick guide on all our favorite ways of searching in Glean:

*   Search basics
*   Filters to narrow down on results
*   Searching for teammates
*   Search from wherever you work
*   Sharing knowledge from Glean
*   Advanced features

Let’s dive right in!

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#search-basics)

Search basics
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#1-search-for-words-from-anywhere-in-the-document)

1\. Search for words from anywhere in the document

First things first - Glean doesn’t just search document titles! You can search for whatever you remember about a document (i.e. specific phrases or keywords) and watch Glean work its magic ✨ _Pro tip: You can run queries as either keywords or phrased as questions._

When you enter a short, keyword‑style query in Chat like a title or name, Glean shows a concise set of top search results directly in the conversation so you can open the right document without switching to the Search page. For more information, see [Search results in Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#search-results-in-chat)
.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-navigate-to-documents-via-autocomplete)

2\. Navigate to documents via autocomplete

If you’ve visited a doc before, just start typing some words from the title to see it appear as a suggestion in autocomplete. _Bonus: With the [Glean extension](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/scio-search/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)
, you’ll also see docs from enterprise apps that Glean hasn’t indexed._

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#3-use-quotes-around-a-word-to-require-it-being-present)

3\. Use quotes around a word to require it being present

To ensure that a word or a phrase are present in all results, include a quote around the word or the phrase that needs to be present. For example: **`featureX "featureY"`** or **`featureX "some phrase"`** Two words in quotes will require that those words show up in the same order right next to each other in the doc. If you just want to make sure that the words show up independently anywhere in the then put separate quotes around each of the words.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#filters)

Filters
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below we’ll walk through 6 examples of filters you can use in Glean. Note that there are two general ways to filter in Glean:

1.  Directly from the query box by typing filter keywords
2.  Clicking on filters after you see results

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#1-find-more-recent-results-with-%E2%80%9Cupdated%E2%80%9D)

1\. Find more recent results with “updated”

Use the _**updated**_ filter to find documents updated in a certain time frame, e.g. **`updated:yesterday`.** Updated time is one of the common filters shown across the top of the results page.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-find-a-person-associated-with-the-document-with-%E2%80%9Cfrom%E2%80%9D)

2\. Find a person associated with the document with “from”

Use the flexible _**from**_ filter to find people associated with a document across any tool, e.g. either Owner or Contributor for a Drive/Dropbox/Box doc, or Assignee, Reporter, or Commenter in a Jira. You can either use the _**from**_ filter on top of the results page, or you can include **`from:`** as you’re formulating your query and select one of the names that appear in autocomplete suggestions. _Pro tips:_

*   _Use **`from:me`** to find something related to you_
*   _If you need to narrow it down to only a specific field (e.g. the Owner of the document), we recommend using the app-specific filters discussed in later sections._

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#3-find-specific-document-types-with-%E2%80%9Ctype%E2%80%9D)

3\. Find specific document types with “type”

Use the _**type**_ filter to narrow by a specific document type (such as a **presentation** in Drive or a **bug** in Jira). You can either use the _**type**_ filter on top of the results page, or you can include **`type:`** as you’re formulating your query and select an autocomplete suggestion.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#4-find-things-you%E2%80%99ve-previously-viewed-with-%E2%80%9Cmy-history%E2%80%9D)

4\. Find things you’ve previously viewed with “my history”

Use the _**my history**_ filter to narrow down your results to only docs that you’ve viewed. Type \*\*`my:history`\*\*into the query box or use the filters at the top of the results page. This currently will only filter to docs you’ve viewed in the past 6 months.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#5-filter-by-app)

5\. Filter by app

Click on the app name in the right side filters to narrow to results from that app. _Pro tip: use **`app:`** in the query to pre-filter to a specific data source without needing to click on it._

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#6-use-dynamically-suggested-filters-within-an-app)

6\. Use dynamically suggested filters within an app

Once you narrow down to a specific app, you’ll see dynamically populated filters based on the results on the page. You can use this to get more specific about what you’re looking for. All filters can also be typed while you’re formulating the query.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#search-for-teammates)

Search for teammates
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#1-find-contact-info-and-details-about-the-person)

1\. Find contact info and details about the person

Search for a person’s name to quickly find contact info, see their org chart, and recent documents and activity from the person. You can also search by a department or role, e.g. `Engineering`.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-learn-more-about-a-document-owner)

2\. Learn more about a document owner

When you see a name next to a document, you can click on the person’s name to search for the document owner.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#search-from-wherever-you-work)

Search from wherever you work
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#1-search-directly-from-slack)

1\. Search directly from Slack

Share a doc quickly from anywhere in Slack with **`/glean`** followed by query terms. The **results will only be visible to you**unless you select a document to share in the channel.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-search-from-any-location)

2\. Search from any location

Use **Cmd+J** on Mac (or **Alt-J**on Windows) to pop out our sidebar and search from anywhere you’re working on the web. The search experience is the same as the web app. This only works if you have the [Glean extension](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/scio-search/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)
. Note: If you have the Firefox extension installed, use the shortcut **Control+J** (Mac)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#share-knowledge)

Share knowledge
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you want to send results to someone else, just copy the URL of your Glean query and send it to anyone on your team, e.g. [https://app.glean.com/search?q=example](https://app.glean.com/search?q=example)
 Remember that their results are personalized so they won’t see any sensitive content they don’t have access to, even if you see it in your results.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#advanced-features)

Advanced features
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#1-advanced-filters)

1\. Advanced filters

For complex workflows, you might be using apps with a lot of custom fields in them, like Salesforce, Zendesk, and Jira. Good news! We extract those custom fields when we index your content so you can use them to do more advanced searches. Click the “info” icon in the right corner of the search box to see the fields that are available for each app. You can actually create a query that just filters by a single advanced filter. Build on top of it with additional filters (either the standard ones on the right side or other advanced filters). You can even add in some keywords on top of it! **Example:** If you’re in sales, quickly answer the common question _“Does anyone have a customer that has…?”_ Just search for **`status:"closed won" vertical:"xyz" feature name`** and narrow down to Salesforce to run an ad-hoc report looking for Won deals in the “xyz” vertical that have any mention of a specific feature. For advanced searches, see [Advanced search filters in Glean](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter)
 for the full syntax, app‑specific operators like Jira, Drive, Slack, and more, copy‑paste examples, and documented limitations with workarounds.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#2-filter-out-certain-apps)

2\. Filter out certain apps

Too many results? If you know certain apps aren’t relevant for your query, you can remove them from your search results. Click on the filter icon at the top of the app filters, and change the list of apps you’d like to see results from. ​​### 3. Everything is searchable! Really, we mean it! If you’re looking for a folder, but don’t feel like using a special search operator like we showed earlier, **you can still type what you’re searching for in plain text.** For example, if you’re looking for the Product folder, then just search **`product folder`** _Pro tip: Type in something like **pptx** or **pdf** at the end of your query to focus on specific document types._ This will generally work, but if for some reason it doesn’t filter it down as you had hoped, then you can use the specific filters mentioned earlier. Happy searching! May you find everything you need - near and far.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean#4-zoom-meeting-results)

4\. Zoom meeting results

When relevant, Zoom meeting transcripts and their recording links appear in Search. Click a result to open the transcript destination in Zoom. For more information, see [Use Zoom meeting transcripts in Search, Chat, and Home](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts)

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[Accessibility Statement](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/accessibility)
[Search from wherever you work](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/search-from-wherever-you-work)

⌘I

Glean Apps

Get The Desktop App

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)

[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
