[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [About the desktop app](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#about-the-desktop-app)
    
*   [Setting up the app](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#setting-up-the-app)
    
*   [Important notes:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#important-notes)
    
*   [Controlling automatic updates](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#controlling-automatic-updates)
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#about-the-desktop-app)

About the desktop app

Glean’s desktop app brings the power of Glean to your Mac and Windows machine, whenever you need it. Invoke Glean from any application using _Cmd-Shift-J_, and type your query to run a search or start a chat conversation with Glean Assistant. ![get-the-desktop-app-0](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app-0.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=b4b46932613557ab8bd04dd203dfc535)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#setting-up-the-app)

Setting up the app

You can download the desktop app from [Glean](https://app.glean.com/settings/install)
. After installing the app, you will have to sign in to Glean. In the app’s settings, you can make the following adjustments to your experience:

*   **Quick search shortcut** controls the keyboard shortcut that opens up the quick search bar anywhere on your Mac. it is _Cmd-Shift-J_ by default, but can be changed to any desired key combination.
*   **Hide menu bar icon** hides the Glean menu bar icon when enabled.
*   **Hide from dock** hides the Glean icon from appearing in the Dock. If the app is pinned to the Dock, its icon will not be hidden, even if this setting is enabled.
*   **Open quick search results in browser** will cause search and chat queries from the quick search bar to open in your default browser, rather than the Glean app window, similar to Apple’s default Spotlight behavior.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#important-notes)

Important notes:

*   Glean will not search local files, such as content on your desktop.
*   The app is only available for macOS 10.15 (“Catalina”) or later.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/get-the-desktop-app#controlling-automatic-updates)

Controlling automatic updates

We provide system preferences to control automatic updates. You can disable automatic updates on a per-user basis. This may be helpful for testing new versions of the app before releasing them, or for gradually rolling out app updates. To maintain app reliability and security, we recommend updating Glean whenever a new app version is released. To prevent Glean from automatically checking for updates, run the following terminal command as each user who will be running the Glean desktop app:

Copy

    defaults write /Users/$USER/Library/Preferences/com.glean.desktop GleanNoAutoUpdates -bool YES
    

Use the following command to re-enable automatic updates:

Copy

    defaults write /Users/$USER/Library/Preferences/com.glean.desktop GleanNoAutoUpdates -bool NO
    

By default, automatic updates will be enabled.

Glean Apps

Glean companion

On this page

*   [Introduction](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#introduction)
    
*   [Using Glean companion](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#using-glean-companion)
    
*   [Getting Started](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#getting-started)
    
*   [Managing Glean companion](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#managing-glean-companion)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#introduction)

Introduction
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean companion provides seamless access to your company’s knowledge and insights directly within your browser. This floating widget contextualizes any text you highlight, allowing you to quickly understand terms, find related information, identify experts, improve your writing, summarize content, and translate text without leaving your current application or document. ![glean-companion-0](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/apps/glean-companion-0.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=b077cfd5a85fe3c48ecee8391398d406)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#using-glean-companion)

Using Glean companion
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To activate the companion, simply highlight any text in your browser. A Glean icon will appear next to the highlighted text. Clicking this icon opens companion, offering six key functionalities:

*   **Explain**: Highlight any unfamiliar term or technical phrase to understand its meaning within your company’s context.
*   **Find related documents**: Highlight a short phrase to discover relevant documents indexed in Glean that discuss similar concepts.
*   **Find experts**: Highlight a key concept to identify subject matter experts on that topic within your organization.
*   **Improve writing**: Highlight a sentence or paragraph to have Glean refine and rewrite the content for grammar and clarity.
*   **Summarize this**: Highlight a paragraph or an entire page to generate a concise summary.
*   **Translate**: Translate any highlighted text into 13 different languages.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#getting-started)

Getting Started
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean companion is accessible by downloading the [Glean browser extension](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/installing-the-browser-extension)
. For troubleshooting, please contact your Glean representative.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/glean-companion#managing-glean-companion)

Managing Glean companion
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Disabling companion on a specific webpage**: To disable companion on a particular website, highlight any text to activate it, click the Power icon, and select ‘Hide for this site’.
*   **Disabling companion**: To disable companion across all webpages, navigate to the Glean sidebar settings (command+J on macOS or alt+J on Windows) and toggle off the companion option within the companion settings.
*   **Data privacy**: Glean companion does not automatically read the content of webpages. It only accesses content when you explicitly highlight text and invoke the feature. Glean does not store any personal information through companion and strictly adheres to its 30-day data retention policy.
*   **Default availability**: Glean companion will be automatically enabled on these web pages. Additional permissions can be granted to have the Glean Companion be available on all web pages.

Copy

    https://app.box.com/*
    https://*.asana.com/*
    https://*.atlassian.net/*
    https://*.docebosaas.com/*
    https://docs.google.com/*

# Glean Deep Research - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Assistant

Glean Deep Research

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Core technology](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#core-technology)
    
*   [When to use Deep research](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#when-to-use-deep-research)
    
*   [How it works](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#how-it-works)
    
*   [Getting started](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#getting-started)
    
*   [Step 1: Access Deep research](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-1-access-deep-research)
    
*   [Step 2: Write your query](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-2-write-your-query)
    
*   [Step 3: Answer clarifying questions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-3-answer-clarifying-questions)
    
*   [Step 4: Wait for your report](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-4-wait-for-your-report)
    
*   [Technical specifications](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#technical-specifications)
    

Glean Deep research is an AI agent that generates detailed, citation-rich reports by synthesizing information from **internal enterprise systems** and the **Web**. It is designed to tackle complex, multi-source queries that require deeper analysis and broader context than traditional search. A typical Deep Research report spans 5–10 pages and includes fully linked citations, making it an ideal tool for strategic planning, competitive analysis, and deep learning.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#core-technology)

Core technology
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deep Research is built on [Glean’s Enterprise Knowledge Graph](https://www.glean.com/resources/guides/glean-knowledge-graph)
 and integrates with over 100 enterprise systems, including Google Drive, Slack, and ServiceNow. It utilizes GPT 5.1, the leading reasoning model from OpenAI, which excels at in-depth research and instruction following.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#when-to-use-deep-research)

When to use Deep research
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use Deep Research when your query requires synthesis, analysis, or strategic insight. It is not intended for basic fact lookups, which are better suited for [Glean Chat.](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat)

| Category | Use Case Example |
| --- | --- |
| **Track** | **Project Tracking & Risk Management:** Analyze recent issues faced by a customer. Review data from Jira, Gong, and Slack to identify the issues and their current status. |
| **Learn** | **Competitive Analysis:** Identify new product launches in a specific feature area by a competitor. Review their blogs, marketing materials, and news releases. |
| **Analyze** | **Strategic Planning:** Analyze a specific business area to uncover untapped opportunities for expansion. Examine Gong calls, Slack threads, and Salesforce activity to find feature requests, recurring usage patterns, or customer-led workarounds. |

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#how-it-works)

How it works
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The agent performs a multi-step process to generate a report, ensuring thoroughness and accuracy:

*   **Searches Multiple Sources:** Glean finds and retrieves relevant information from your enterprise knowledge base and the web.
*   **Synthesizes Information:** The agent synthesizes outputs from multiple AI models to analyze the retrieved information.
*   **Generates a Detailed Report:** The final output is a structured, detailed report with fully linked citations.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#getting-started)

Getting started
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deep research must be enabled for your organization. Admins can [enable or disable Deep Research](https://docs.glean.com/administration/assistant/configuration/deep-research)
 for users in the Admin Console.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-1-access-deep-research)

Step 1: Access Deep research

1.  In Glean, click **Chat** and select **Deep research** mode.
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/VgdlNLqe8-hCdBNL/images/deep-research/deep-research1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=VgdlNLqe8-hCdBNL&q=85&s=0aa4e5ed6879af63fecd1aa7c245d855)
    

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-2-write-your-query)

Step 2: Write your query

Deep research performs best when you provide more detail. A strong query should clearly state your research goals and, if possible, include specific sources to reference.

*   **State your goals:** Clearly explain what you want to achieve.
*   **Provide context:** Add details by referencing specific documents, teams, or systems.
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/VgdlNLqe8-hCdBNL/images/deep-research/deep-research2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=VgdlNLqe8-hCdBNL&q=85&s=a76af40ece43707c65ac2fbff66dbf1b)
    

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-3-answer-clarifying-questions)

Step 3: Answer clarifying questions

After you submit a query, the agent may ask a few clarifying questions to ensure the report it generates is detailed and meets your needs. Once you provide the necessary details, Deep research will begin its work.

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/VgdlNLqe8-hCdBNL/images/deep-research/deep-research3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=VgdlNLqe8-hCdBNL&q=85&s=b00bc659a8d03ce4d0db6072b3e6f5d0)

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#step-4-wait-for-your-report)

Step 4: Wait for your report

After submitting your query, the agent will begin its research. Generating a full report typically takes 5–30 minutes, depending on the complexity of the query and the number of sources.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/deep-research#technical-specifications)

Technical specifications

This section provides key details on the product’s access, usage, and technology.

*   **Supported platforms:** Deep Research is currently supported on our web application only. We will roll out to other platforms, such as mobile and desktop, in the coming months.
*   **LLM key support:** Deep Research is built to work exclusively with the **Glean Universal Key**. It is not compatible with legacy keys (Azure, Vertex, or AWS). Customer-hosted keys are also not supported.
*   **Web search:** Deep Research exclusively uses **Brave Web Search** for all web content.

# Providing Informational Hierarchy Using Subcollections - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Collections

Providing Informational Hierarchy Using Subcollections

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [What are SubCollections?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#what-are-subcollections)
    
*   [How to create SubCollections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#how-to-create-subcollections)
    
*   [Moving SubCollections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#moving-subcollections)
    
*   [Accessing Subcollections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#accessing-subcollections)
    
*   [Number of Subcollections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#number-of-subcollections)
    

[Collections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/how-collections-work)
 are an easy way to organize related documents across all your tools and any URL from the web by a single topic. As teams start creating Collections with more than a few items, it can become harder for people to navigate and find the most relevant or important item within the Collection. Here’s how you can use Subcollections to better organize.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#what-are-subcollections)

What are SubCollections?
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SubCollections are nested collections under a main collection, providing ways to build a more robust collection with a large number of items. It prevents a single collection from being too long to navigate even with Headers, while also providing informational hierarchy. For example, you can create a subcollection for:

*   Subteams, subtopics, or subprojects under the main Collection for a team or topic. For example, creating a subcollection for a product feature within a Collection for a product team
*   When there are different audiences under the same umbrella topics. i.e. creating a subcollection for BDR and AE under “Onboarding” header/topic
*   When you have more than 5 items (documents and links) under the same topic, making it too long to be organized under a Header
    *   We also encourage organizing using subcollection when the main collection becomes a list of 20+ documents and links.

See a demo of Subcollections [here](https://www.loom.com/share/5969473e092d4779b5655bae5fa181f2)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#how-to-create-subcollections)

How to create SubCollections
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can create a Subcollection the same way you add any documents and links to a collection by clicking ”+ Add New Collection” button on the top right corner of the Collection. You can also insert Subcollection between existing documents or add to the bottom of the page by clicking the **+---** button from the place you want to add a Subcollection to. ![SubCollections Creation](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=99c9b27adcee1626bbcdb405dfb698b3)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#moving-subcollections)

Moving SubCollections
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Collections can be nested under other Collections by clicking on the 3-dot menu on the Collections dashboard and selecting “Move” and moving the Collection to an appropriate parent Collection. Please note that there can only be one parent Collection for every Subcollection. ![Moving SubCollections](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=211711727af76b291bdd9f6a34d7abb2)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#accessing-subcollections)

Accessing Subcollections
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   See SubCollections in search results
    *   Subcollection will appear on the search results based on relevancy
    *   When a document is part of a SubCollection, you’ll see the SubCollection on the search result
*   SubCollection will not appear on the main Collections dashboard in order to help organization of the dashboard

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/providing-informational-hierarchy-using-subcollections#number-of-subcollections)

Number of Subcollections
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can create up to 20 Collections within a collection and up to 2 sublevels (Main Collection -> sub collections -> collection within a sub collection) for a 3 layer of Collections hierarchy

# Organizing Collections Using Headers - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Collections

Organizing Collections Using Headers

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)

[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [What are Headers?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers#what-are-headers)
    
*   [How to create Headers](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers#how-to-create-headers)
    

Collections are an easy way to organize related documents across all your tools and any URL from the web by a single topic. As teams start creating Collections with more than a few items, it can become harder for people to navigate and find the most relevant or important item within the Collection. Here’s how you can use Headers to better organize.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers#what-are-headers)

What are Headers?
=======================================================================================================================================

Headers are used to label different sections within a Collection to provide more context on a group of documents. For example, you can use headers to:

*   Group documents by order of importance using “Start here” “Must reads”
*   Group documents by different projects
*   Group documents by topics such as “Onboarding” “Technical resources” “Project backgrounds”

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers#how-to-create-headers)

How to create Headers
================================================================================================================================================

You can create headers the same way you add any documents and links by clicking on the **\+ Add** button on the top right corner of the Collection. You can also insert Headers between existing documents or add Headers to the bottom of the page by clicking the **+---** button from the place you want to add headers to. ![Organizing Collections Using Headers](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=482a24cbfc6c173e4e0a0a67a7c568da) If you are looking for ways to organize by creating hierarchy of information, you can use [Subcollections](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/organizing-collections-using-headers)
 to create Collections within a Collection.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

# Glean Desktop App Quick Chat for macOS - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Apps

Glean Desktop App Quick Chat for macOS

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Configuration and customization](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#configuration-and-customization)
    
*   [Step 1: Access settings](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-1-access-settings)
    
*   [Step 2: Define quick entry behavior](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-2-define-quick-entry-behavior)
    
*   [Step 3: Define quick chat behavior](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-3-define-quick-chat-behavior)
    
*   [Step 4: Provide permissions for screen capture](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-4-provide-permissions-for-screen-capture)
    
*   [Usage](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#usage)
    
*   [Access Assistant from anywhere](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#access-assistant-from-anywhere)
    
*   [Attach visual context](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#attach-visual-context)
    

The [Glean Desktop App](http://app.glean.com/settings/install)
 for **macOS** is designed to provide **chat-first access** to the Glean Assistant from anywhere on your machine. This experience is delivered via the following components:

*   **Quick entry for Glean Assistant:** The universal shortcut (`⌘+⇧+J` by default) that launches a search bar can be configured to default to a Glean Assistant. ![spotlight-chat-first](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/PqfA7qz0q84plwPi/images/user-guide/apps/quick-entry.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=PqfA7qz0q84plwPi&q=85&s=812570e4370176552865a533bc8b1877)
*   **Quick chat:** A dedicated, assistant floating window that **always stays on top** of all other applications. It provides a persistent, uninterrupted space for conversations with the Glean Chat Assistant.
*   **Chat with screenshot:** The ability to attach a screenshot directly to your query within quick chat, giving the Assistant visual context for faster, more accurate answers. ![ask-assistant](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/PqfA7qz0q84plwPi/images/user-guide/apps/quick-entry1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=PqfA7qz0q84plwPi&q=85&s=70e40e70a06a9aec10c08fa8923966b1)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#configuration-and-customization)

Configuration and customization
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All features are managed from the **Desktop App** settings panel.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-1-access-settings)

Step 1: Access settings

1.  In the Glean application, click the **Glean icon** (bottom-left) and then click **Your settings**. ![settings-assistant](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/PqfA7qz0q84plwPi/images/user-guide/apps/settings-assistant.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=PqfA7qz0q84plwPi&q=85&s=bec816faf82e9ba0668d2966eb30c421)
2.  Click the **Desktop app** section.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-2-define-quick-entry-behavior)

Step 2: Define quick entry behavior

Configure the shortcuts and default modes for quick entry.

1.  In the **Quick entry** > **Quick entry shortcut** field specify the key combination for the the **Quick entry shortcut** (e.g., `⌘+⇧+J`).
2.  Set the **Default mode:** for the quick entry shortcut:
    *   **Chat:** Launches the interface directly into the Assistant chat composer (recommended).
    *   **Search:** Launches the Glean Chat Assistant in the Glean application.
3.  In **Open new chats**, select the default destination for all new chat queries:
    *   **In Quick Chat:** Launches the conversation in the persistent floating window.
    *   **In Main Window:** Launches the conversation in the Glean application window.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-3-define-quick-chat-behavior)

Step 3: Define quick chat behavior

1.  In the **quick chat** > **aompanion chat shortcut:** field specify the key combination for the **quick chat shortcut** (e.g., `⌃+⇧+G`).
2.  Set the default **Quick chat** behavior.
    *   Toggle window
    *   Open new window

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#step-4-provide-permissions-for-screen-capture)

Step 4: Provide permissions for screen capture

1.  Under **Permissions**, toggle on **Screen Capture** to enable the **chat with screenshot** feature.

Once configured, the feature is immediately accessible via the shortcut. **Note:** After making changes to the settings, you may need to **quit and relaunch the app** for the new configuration to take effect.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#usage)

Usage
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#access-assistant-from-anywhere)

Access Assistant from anywhere

*   **Quick entry:** Press the defined **Quick entry shortcut** (`⌘+⇧+J`) to instantly launch the chat composer and submit your query.
*   **Quick chat:** Press the defined **quick chat shortcut** (`⌃+⇧+G`) to launch the floating Assistant window.

#### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/chat-first-experience#attach-visual-context)

Attach visual context

1.  Open the **Quick chat** window.
2.  Click the **screenshot** button (camera icon) in the chat composer. The captured screenshot attaches directly to your query, providing visual context to the Assistant.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

# Updating Permissions On Collections - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Collections

Updating Permissions On Collections

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Who Can Change Permissions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#who-can-change-permissions)
    
*   [How to Change Permissions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#how-to-change-permissions)
    
*   [How Permissions on Child Collections Work](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#how-permissions-on-child-collections-work)
    

Collections are an easy way to organize related documents across all your tools and any URL from the web by a single topic. When you want to use Collections as a source of truth on a particular topic, you may want to restrict which teammates can edit contents of that Collection. Here’s how you can control who has edit access over a single Collection.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#who-can-change-permissions)

Who Can Change Permissions
=========================================================================================================================================================

1.  **Owners:** Owners can add or remove other owners and editors, ensuring that the right people have the appropriate level of access.
    *   The creator of a collection is automatically assigned as the Owner and has the ability to manage permissions.
    *   The company’s overall Collection Moderator has Owner rights to all Collections by default.
2.  **Editors:** Editors can add or remove other editors from the Collection

**Note:** All Collections will always be viewable by the entire company. You can restrict any individual from viewing a specific Collection.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#how-to-change-permissions)

How to Change Permissions
=======================================================================================================================================================

If you’re the an owner or editor of a Collection

1.  **Open the Collection**: Navigate to the collection for which you want to change permissions.
2.  **Click the Share Button**: Locate and click the Share button on the collection page. ![Share Button](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=5a34eb2e2acf439a4061b0df28e07324)
3.  **Manage Permissions**: In the sharing settings, you can:
    
    *   Add individual owners and editors.
    *   Change the permission level of the entire company from editors to viewers.
    *   Remove access for specific users or departments.
    
    ![Manage Permissions](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=fa7e502392bd8a03bd59ae96225ac787)
4.  New permissions are auto-saved. Click ‘Done’ to exit.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections#how-permissions-on-child-collections-work)

How Permissions on Child Collections Work
=======================================================================================================================================================================================

Child Collections (Collections created under a top parent Collection) automatically inherit the permissions of their parent Collection. This means:

*   **Inherited Permissions**: Any permissions set on the parent Collection will apply to the child Collection.
*   **Adding Permissions**: Owners and editors of the child Collection can add new editors or owners to the child collection.
*   **Non-Removable Inherited Permissions**: Inherited permissions cannot be removed from the child collection.

![Child Collection Permissions](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/collections/updating-permissions-on-collections-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=63330dc1caf25264f18a03094bf5a95c)

# Customizing Your Glean Home Page - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Advanced

Customizing Your Glean Home Page

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Customize the Home page directly from the home page](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#customize-the-home-page-directly-from-the-home-page)
    
*   [Set your default mode between Search and Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#set-your-default-mode-between-search-and-chat)
    
*   [Customize home page settings using the settings icon](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#customize-home-page-settings-using-the-settings-icon)
    
*   [Customize the Home page using the Preferences Menu](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#customize-the-home-page-using-the-preferences-menu)
    
*   [Home page settings customization options](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#home-page-settings-customization-options)
    
*   [General](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#general)
    
*   [Theme](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#theme)
    
*   [Background](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#background)
    
*   [Layout](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#layout)
    
*   [Preferred language](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#preferred-language)
    
*   [Default composer selection](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#default-composer-selection)
    
*   [Tips](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#tips)
    

Your Glean home page is fully customizable to match your workflow, aesthetic preferences, and productivity needs. Some of the customization options are available directly from the home page, others are available in the Preferences menu.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#customize-the-home-page-directly-from-the-home-page)

Customize the Home page directly from the home page
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

some settings can be customized directly from the home page, others are available in the Preferences menu.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#set-your-default-mode-between-search-and-chat)

Set your default mode between Search and Chat

You can **pin** your preferred default, **Search** or **Chat**, directly from the **Home page composer** so Glean starts the way you like every time. Pinning sets your pinned mode as the default at the start of each new Glean session, and moves the pinned mode to the left in the composer toggle for easier access. On the Glean Home page, select the pin icon next to either Search or Chat on the composer.

*   The pin shows up on the mode which is currently not the pinned tab. If you don’t see the pin icon, you are already pinned to the mode you selected.
*   Your organization has a global default set by your admin (for example, Chat-first), you will see that mode on the left by default. You can still change your personal default by pinning the mode you prefer and that will override the global default.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#customize-home-page-settings-using-the-settings-icon)

Customize home page settings using the settings icon

In the upper right hand corner of the home page, you will see a settings icon (slider filter icon). Clicking this will open a drawer with the following settings:

*   **General**: Control basic behavior of the Glean home page.
*   **Theme**: Choose your preferred theme for the Glean home page.
*   **Background**: Choose from a variety of static and scenic wallpapers to personalize your background.
*   **Layout**: Customize how content is displayed on your home page.
*   **Default composer selection**: Choose the default mode for the composer (the box where you type search queries or chat prompts) each time you load the Home Page.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#customize-the-home-page-using-the-preferences-menu)

Customize the Home page using the Preferences Menu
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Preferences Menu contains more advanced settings for customizing your home page. To access the **Preferences Menu**:

1.  Select your profile icon in the lower left corner of the home page.
2.  Select **Your Settings**.
3.  Ensure you’re on the **Preferences** tab.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#home-page-settings-customization-options)

Home page settings customization options
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can customize the following home page settings using either the settings icon or the Preferences Menu:

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#general)

General

Control basic behavior of the Glean home page.

*   **Use Glean as new tab page**: When toggled on, Glean will appear every time you open a new tab in your browser.
*   **Show bookmarks bar**: Toggle this to show or hide the bookmarks bar for quick access to saved links.
*   **Show shortcuts**: Toggle this to show or hide shortcuts to commonly visited pages or apps.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#theme)

Theme

*   **Light**: A bright, classic look.
*   **Dark**: A sleek, low-light theme that’s easy on the eyes.
*   **System**: Automatically matches your operating system’s light or dark mode setting.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#background)

Background

Choose from a variety of static and scenic wallpapers to personalize your background.

*   Tap any image to apply it.
*   To remove wallpaper, select the blank option in the top-left.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#layout)

Layout

Customize how content is displayed on your home page.

*   **Simple**: A minimal and focused layout—great if you prefer a clean workspace.
*   **Informational** (default): Displays helpful widgets like your calendar, mentions, trending content, and more.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#preferred-language)

Preferred language

Choose your preferred language for the Glean home page.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#default-composer-selection)

Default composer selection

Choose the default mode for the composer (the box where you type search queries or chat prompts) each time you load the Home Page. This gives you faster access to your preferred workflow, whether you’re a search power user or rely on Glean Assistant for chat.

*   **Search (default)**: Composer defaults to a search mode—ideal if you primarily use Glean for quick lookups.
*   **Chat** : Composer opens ready for a conversation with Glean’s assistant.
*   **Sticky:** Glean will remember your last used mode—Search or Chat—and default to that next time.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/customizing-home-page#tips)

Tips
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Changes take effect immediately. You don’t need to save.
*   You can return to the settings panel at any time to make updates.

# Searching from the address bar - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/searching-from-address-bar#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Navigation

Advanced

Searching from the address bar

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Chrome](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/searching-from-address-bar#chrome)
    
*   [Firefox](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/searching-from-address-bar#firefox)
    

You will need the [Glean Extension](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/apps/installing-the-browser-extension)
 installed to use this feature.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/searching-from-address-bar#chrome)

Chrome
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In Chrome, just type **`gl`** **`space`** or **`gl`** **`tab`** into the address bar, followed by your query in Glean. ![Chrome Glean Search](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/advanced/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=c57b695dd0cc2809a7959ef1620a037a) If you also start typing “app.glean…”, you can also tab to search directly in Glean from there.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/searching-from-address-bar#firefox)

Firefox
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Go to [app.glean.com](http://app.glean.com/)
 and click into the address bar. Right click, and you’ll see an option to **Add “Glean”** at the bottom of the menu. ![Firefox Add Glean Search](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/advanced/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=653f7109d916cf0442049e5e15ade1b3) You can also click into the address bar, and click on the Glean icon in the bottom row of the menu (with the green plus button). ![Firefox Glean Icon](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/advanced/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=36f04ef91d6bf2562fa150fa697a3edf) Then, navigate to the search section, under your Firefox Preferences (or just type **about:preferences#search** into the address bar). Double click on “Glean” in the list, and you’ll be able to add your own keyword there. ![Firefox Search Preferences](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/advanced/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=628ac7c80ad583a00d6af23d9b34827f) We recommend setting this up at @gl (short, easy to type). ![Firefox Glean Keyword](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/advanced/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar-5.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=f4558ba8d98f67033cf3c3893d0485c3) You’ll then be able to search directly with Glean from the address bar! ![Firefox Glean Search Result](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/advanced/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar-6.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=9e7ba642073e456d6335173f76f8c1db)

After opening a new window/Firefox tab, pressing the tab button will not focus on the Glean search box. Unfortunately, this is a known problem on the Firefox end. Please use `F6 + Tab` to focus on the Glean search box.

Looking for the original versions of this page? You can find the archived versions [here](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/faq/pressing-tab-is-not-focusing-on-glean-search-box-in-firefox)
 and [here](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/faq/how-do-i-search-glean-directly-from-the-address-bar)
.

Answers

How To Write Effective Answers

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [1\. Think of common questions you or your team get asked](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#1-think-of-common-questions-you-or-your-team-get-asked)
    
*   [2\. Phrase your question completely](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#2-phrase-your-question-completely)
    
*   [3\. Write your answer](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#3-write-your-answer)
    
*   [Other ways to create answers](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#other-ways-to-create-answers)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#1-think-of-common-questions-you-or-your-team-get-asked)

1\. Think of common questions you or your team get asked
==========================================================================================================================================================================================================

These might be a category of questions (i.e. “Where is XYZ”), questions related to a workflow, or questions that come up at certain times of the year. **Some examples to get you started 👇**

*   Where can I find…?
*   What existing work has been done on…?
*   What’s our process for…?
*   What’s the policy for…?
*   How do I configure…?
*   How do I set up…?
*   How do I troubleshoot…?

**Pro tip ✨** If your team has a public Slack channel that others use to collaborate with/ask questions of your team, or a private Slack channel for questions among yourselves, look through that for ideas. Adding answers for those kinds of questions will save time for you and your teammates in the future!

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#2-phrase-your-question-completely)

2\. Phrase your question completely
================================================================================================================================================================

In the **New Answer** modal, write your question in a complete sentence. When others search for that question or keywords/queries that match the question, they’ll see your answer at the top of their results. Your question will also be used as a **title** for your answer.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#3-write-your-answer)

3\. Write your answer
====================================================================================================================================

You can add a link, text, or both to your answer. The way your answers looks in the text editor is exactly like how others will see your answer at the top of their search results. ![Answer Creation Interface](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=333fbf9b65035c25e48e7a7a2df05555) Then just save and you’re done! You can always edit your answer from the search results, or click **Answers** in the top navigation bar to view and edit your answers.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers#other-ways-to-create-answers)

Other ways to create answers
====================================================================================================================================================

**Create answer from result** Use this if you’ve already searched for something, and see a result you want to appear at the top for everyone when they search for the same thing. Hover over the result, click the “more” menu with the three dots, and click **Create Answer**. ![Create Answer Option](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/answers/how-to-write-effective-answers-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=30b9c15659266e81f318a5906704ed03)

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/configuring-dark-mode#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Advanced

Configuring dark mode

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

Glean supports dark mode across its desktop and mobile applications to allow users to tailor their experience. This guide will help you understand what options Glean provides, as well as how to configure them. By default, Glean will mirror your system’s settings. When your computer or phone is set to dark mode, Glean will also be in dark mode. You can also set Glean to remain in dark mode or light mode by navigating to [your settings](https://app.glean.com/settings)
 and selecting the appropriate option. Glean applies these preferences across its desktop, [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.glean.app)
 and [iOS](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/glean-work/id1582892407)
 apps. You can change these settings at any time.

Advanced search filters in Glean

On this page

*   [Essential syntax rules](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#essential-syntax-rules)
    
*   [Core filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#core-filters)

*   [Natural language](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#natural-language)
    
*   [Specific dates](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#specific-dates)
    
*   [Boolean logic rules](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#boolean-logic-rules)
    
*   [People and entity filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#people-and-entity-filters)
    
*   [Text matching patterns](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#text-matching-patterns)
    
*   [Use search filters in Glean Agents](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#use-search-filters-in-glean-agents)
    
*   [Critical Rules](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#critical-rules)
    
*   [Good vs Bad Examples](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#good-vs-bad-examples)
    
*   [Tips and tricks](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#tips-and-tricks)
    
*   [Query Building Strategy](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#query-building-strategy)
    
*   [Performance Tips](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#performance-tips)
    
*   [Discover filters through the Search UI:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#discover-filters-through-the-search-ui)
    
*   [Known limitations and workarounds](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#known-limitations-and-workarounds)
    
*   [Quick reference table](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#quick-reference-table)
    
*   [Common search patterns](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#common-search-patterns)
    
*   [Application specific filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#application-specific-filters)
    

[Search filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean)
 are useful ways to find exactly what you need across all company documents. Instead of browsing hundreds of results to find the single document you are looking for, use filters to say “show me only Google Drive presentations from Jane updated last week.” You can use filter to:

*   Perform detailed and specific queries in Glean Search.
*   Create _Manual_ search queries for Agent, allowing fine-grained specification of exactly which data Company Search, Web Search, and other Agents Actions/Steps will retrieve.

Basic syntax: `filter:value` (like `app:gdrive` or `from:"Jane Doe"`). Include negation filters by adding a `-` operator to any query (e.g. `-app:gmail` excludes results from Google Mail). Combine filters to get precise results — it’s like giving Glean specific instructions to understand exactly what you’re looking for. This comprehensive article is designed to be your go-to reference for [Glean Search Filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean)
:

*   **New to Glean?** Start with [Essential syntax rules](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#essential-syntax-rules)
     and [Core filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#core-filters)
     to learn the basics.
*   **Building Agents?** Jump to [Use search filters in Glean Agents](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#use-search-filters-in-glean-agents)
     for critical rules and examples.
*   **Need app-specific help?** [Application specific filters](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#application-specific-filters)
     covers 9+ applications with examples.
*   **Looking for examples of common use-cases?** [Common Search Patterns](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#common-search-patterns)
     has ready-to-use queries for frequent scenarios.
*   **Stuck?** Check [Known limitations and workarounds](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#known-limitations-and-workarounds)
     for known issues and workarounds.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#essential-syntax-rules)

Essential syntax rules
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The essential syntax rules include how to handle spaces, exact phrases, and word matching logic. Quote multi-word values, use exact phrases for precise matches, and understand that unquoted words search for ANY while quoted words search for ALL.

Copy

    filter:value                    → Basic filter syntax
    filter:"value with space"       → ALWAYS quote multi-word values  
    "exact phrase"                  → Find this exact phrase
    "word1" "word2"                 → Find ALL these words (any order)
    word1 word2                     → Find ANY of these words
    -filter:value                   → EXCLUDE certain results.
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#core-filters)

Core filters
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These are the fundamental filters that work across all applications:

Copy

    app:appname                    → Limit to specific app(s)
    type:typename                  → Filter by document type  
    from:person                    → From specific person
    from:me                        → Your documents
    updated:timeframe              → Recently modified
    my:history                     → You've viewed (6 month limit)
    collection:"Name"              → In a Glean collection
    has:golink                     → Has a Go link
    in:"Folder Name"               → Docs in specific container/space/folder
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#date-and-time-expressions)

Date and time expressions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#natural-language)

Natural language

Copy

    updated:today                  → Current day only
    updated:yesterday              → Previous day only
    updated:past_week              → Last 7 days
    updated:past_month             → Last 30 days
    updated:past_3_days            → Last 3 days
    updated:past_quarter           → Last 90 days
    updated:this_week              → Current calendar week
    updated:last_month             → Previous calendar month
    after:"2024-01-01"             → After a specific date
    before:"2024-01-01"             → Before a specific date
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#specific-dates)

Specific dates

Copy

    updated:"2024-01-15"                    → Specific date
    updated:"2024-01"                       → Entire month
    updated:"2024"                          → Entire year
    updated:"2024-Q1"                       → Quarter
    after:"2024-01-01" before:"2024-01-31"  → Date range
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#boolean-logic-rules)

Boolean logic rules
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Same filter** = OR: `type:doc type:sheet` (docs OR sheets).
*   **Different filters** = AND: `app:jira from:me` (Jira AND from me).
*   **No way to force AND on same filter** (platform limitation).
*   **No parentheses or custom grouping** (platform limitation).
*   **Negation filter**: Add a `-` operator to remove certain keywords from queries (e.g. employee experience `-customer` → exclude results that mention “customer”.).

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#people-and-entity-filters)

People and entity filters
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Filters for finding people by title, department, location, reporting structure, and combining with document searches.

Copy

    # Job & Organization
    title:"Software Engineer"      → Job title
    department:engineering         → Department
    businessunit:sales             → Business unit
    team:"Platform Team"           → Specific team
    
    # Location
    location:"San Francisco"       → City/office
    country:"United States"        → Country
    state:"CA"                     → State/province
    region:"EMEA"                  → Region
    
    # Reporting & Employment
    reportsto:"Manager Name"       → Direct manager
    level:[ic|manager|director]    → Seniority level
    startafter:"2023-01-01"        → Start date after
    startbefore:"2024-01-01"       → Start date before
    employmenttype:[fulltime|contractor|intern]
    
    # Combining with documents
    from:[title:"Product Manager"] type:presentation
    from:[department:sales] updated:past_week
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#text-matching-patterns)

Text matching patterns
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    # Exact phrase
    "quarterly business review"     → Exact phrase in order
    
    # All words required
    "quarterly" "business" "review" → All 3 words, any order
    
    # Any words
    quarterly business review       → ANY of these words
    
    # Special characters (use quotes)
    "user@company.com"
    "Q4-2024"  
    "$1,000,000"
    "C++"
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#use-search-filters-in-glean-agents)

Use search filters in Glean Agents
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Crafting manual search queries in Glean Agents is a powerful approach for achieving accurate, highly relevant information retrieval across organizational knowledge. Unlike AI-generated queries, manually constructed searches allow you to specify exactly which parameters and filters to apply, leading to precise results that align tightly with business objectives. Quoting multi-word values—such as account:“Acme Corp” ensures the system interprets search intent correctly, while explicit filter inclusion helps target the right data set from the start. This level of control not only minimizes noise and irrelevant results but also supports reproducibility and transparency in automated processes. Manual queries allows you to confidently surface critical insights, troubleshoot with nuance, and create efficient workflows that are robust to context changes—making them indispensable for high-stakes use cases within Glean. Hence, for the `Company Search` step in a Glean Agent, it is a core best practice to _default to a Manual search query_ instead of an _AI-generated_ one. Manual queries provide deterministic, repeatable, and reliable results, which is critical for any automated workflow.  
Use the `AI-generated` option only when search parameters, like a specific date, must be created dynamically during the Agent’s run.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#critical-rules)

Critical Rules

Always quote multi-word values, test syntax in Glean Search UI first, and be explicit with all needed filters for reliable Agent results.

*   **Always quote multi-word values**: `account:"Acme Corp"`.
*   **Test in Glean Search UI first**, copy exact syntax.
    
    Any manual search query that will work in Agents will also work in Glean Search. Consequently, this pattern of testing manual queries is quite effective.
    
*   **Use Manual Search** (not AI-generated) when possible.
*   **Be explicit** - include all needed filters.
*   **Handle empty results** in your Agent logic.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#good-vs-bad-examples)

Good vs Bad Examples

Proper quoting makes the difference between successful and failed searches. Quoted multi-word values ensure accurate interpretation while unquoted ones cause parsing errors.

Copy

    ✅ GOOD: account:"Acme Corporation" status:"in progress"
    ❌ BAD:  account:Acme Corporation status:in progress
    
    ✅ GOOD: app:jira type:bug label:"high-priority" 
    ❌ BAD:  app:jira type:bug label:high priority
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#tips-and-tricks)

Tips and tricks
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Build queries incrementally starting simple, use app filters first for performance, and discover new filters through the UI’s “All filters” option.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#query-building-strategy)

Query Building Strategy

The incremental layering approach - starting with keywords, then systematically adding app, type, person, and time filters - mirrors how Glean’s search engine processes queries for optimal performance. This methodology prevents the “too many results” problem that overwhelms users and ensures each filter layer meaningfully refines the result set rather than creating contradictory constraints.

1.  Start simple: `project phoenix`
2.  Add app: `project phoenix app:confluence`
3.  Add type: `project phoenix app:confluence type:page`
4.  Add person: `project phoenix app:confluence type:page from:"PM"`
5.  Add time: `project phoenix app:confluence type:page from:"PM" updated:past_week`

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#performance-tips)

Performance Tips

Begin with app filters to narrow scope, be specific with document types, limit date ranges, and use exact phrases for known titles.

*   Use `app:` filter first to narrow scope
*   Be specific with types
*   Limit date ranges when possible
*   Use exact phrases for known titles

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#discover-filters-through-the-search-ui)

Discover filters through the Search UI:

1.  Run a search.
2.  Click All filters.
3.  Select options.
4.  Copy the syntax from the search bar.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#known-limitations-and-workarounds)

Known limitations and workarounds
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Limitation | What doesn’t work | Practical workaround |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No complex boolean | `(A OR B) AND (C OR D)` | Run multiple targeted searches |
| No same‑field AND | “Docs with Jane AND John” | Use text search for both names |
| No regex | Pattern matching | Use multiple exact phrases |
| History scope | `my:history` > 6 months | Combine with other filters |
| GDrive recency edge cases | Newly updated files may not appear with broad time filters | Combine time with `from:` or `type:`; add keywords for critical searches |

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#quick-reference-table)

Quick reference table
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These are the 8 common use cases with their exact filter combinations for quick copy-paste:

| Need | Filter combination |
| --- | --- |
| My recent work | `from:me updated:past_week` |
| Team documents | `from:[department:"Dept"] type:document` |
| Open bugs | `app:jira type:bug status:"open"` |
| Customer intel | `"Acme Corp" app:salescloud app:zendesk` |
| Meeting notes | `"meeting" type:document updated:past_month` |
| Shared drives | `app:gdrive folder:"Shared"` |
| Recent Slack | `app:slack updated:yesterday` |
| My history, slides | `my:history type:presentation` |

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#common-search-patterns)

Common search patterns
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These are ready-to-use queries for team documents, customer intelligence, project tracking, bugs, sales, and knowledge search. Recent team documents

Copy

    from:[department:"Engineering"] updated:past_week
    from:[reportsto:"Manager Name"] "meeting notes"
    from:me app:gdrive type:document updated:past_month
    

Customer intelligence

Copy

    "Acme Corp" app:salescloud app:zendesk app:gong
    account:"Acme Corp" type:opportunity status:"open"
    "Acme Corp" app:slack channel:"#customer-success"
    

Project tracking

Copy

    "Project Phoenix" app:confluence app:jira
    "Project Phoenix" updated:past_week -type:email
    "Project Phoenix" from:[team:"Phoenix Team"]
    

Bug management

Copy

    app:jira type:bug status:"open" priority:highest
    app:jira type:bug label:customer-reported assignee:me
    app:jira type:bug status:"resolved" updated:past_week
    

Sales pipeline

Copy

    app:salescloud type:opportunity stage:!"closed lost"
    app:salescloud type:opportunity amount:>100000
    app:salescloud closedate:"2024-Q1" owner:me
    

Knowledge search

Copy

    "how to" type:document app:confluence label:tutorial
    collection:"Engineering Playbooks" "best practices"
    has:golink "onboarding"
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/advanced/advanced-search-filter#application-specific-filters)

Application specific filters
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following are detailed filter syntax for Google Drive, Slack, Jira, Confluence, Salesforce, GitHub/GitLab, Teams, Gong, and Zendesk. Google Drive

Copy

    app:gdrive
    type:[document|spreadsheet|presentation|pdf|image|video|folder]
    folder:"Folder Name"
    owner:email@company.com
    # Example:
    app:gdrive type:spreadsheet folder:"Finance" updated:past_week
    

Slack

Copy

    app:slack
    channel:"#channel-name"   # include the #
    from:@username            # include the @
    type:[conversation|channel|dm]
    # Example:
    app:slack channel:"#engineering" from:@john "deployment"
    

Jira

Copy

    app:jira
    type:[bug|story|epic|task|subtask]
    status:"Status Name"
    assignee:"Person Name"
    reporter:"Person Name"
    project:"PROJECT-KEY"
    label:labelname
    component:componentname
    priority:[highest|high|medium|low|lowest]
    sprint:"Sprint Name"
    # Example:
    app:jira type:bug status:"in progress" priority:high assignee:me
    

Confluence

Copy

    app:confluence
    type:[page|blogpost|space]
    space:"Space Name"
    author:"Person Name"
    label:labelname
    # Example:
    app:confluence space:"Engineering Wiki" author:"Jane" label:howto
    

Salesforce

Copy

    # Sales Cloud
    app:salescloud
    type:[opportunity|account|lead|contact|case]
    status:"Status Name"
    stage:"Stage Name"
    owner:"Person Name"
    account:"Account Name"
    amount:>100000
    closedate:"2024-Q1"
    
    # Service Cloud
    app:servicecloud
    type:case
    priority:[high|medium|low]
    # Example:
    app:salescloud type:opportunity stage:"negotiation" amount:>50000
    

GitHub / GitLab

Copy

    app:github   # or app:gitlab
    type:[issue|pr|commit|repo|discussion]
    repo:"repository-name"
    author:"username"
    assignee:"username"
    label:labelname
    status:[open|closed|merged]
    # Example:
    app:github type:pr repo:"main-app" status:open label:bug
    

Microsoft Teams

Copy

    app:teams
    team:"Team Name"
    channel:"Channel Name"
    from:"Person Name"
    type:[conversation|file|channel]
    # Example:
    app:teams team:"Product Dev" channel:"General" type:file
    

Gong

Copy

    app:gong
    account:"Account Name"
    participant:"Person Name"
    type:[call|email]
    # Example:
    app:gong account:"Acme Corp" type:call updated:past_week
    

Zendesk

Copy

    app:zendesk
    type:ticket
    status:[new|open|pending|solved|closed]
    priority:[urgent|high|normal|low]
    assignee:"Agent Name"
    requester:"Customer Name"
    # Example:
    app:zendesk type:ticket status:open priority:urgent
    

Was this page helpful?

YesNo
