# Troubleshooting Go Links - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Go Links

Troubleshooting Go Links

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [If you are seeing an error saying “This site can’t be reached”](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting#if-you-are-seeing-an-error-saying-%E2%80%9Cthis-site-can%E2%80%99t-be-reached%E2%80%9D)
    
*   [If you are seeing a page that says “Oops, no Go Link here!”](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting#if-you-are-seeing-a-page-that-says-%E2%80%9Coops-no-go-link-here-%E2%80%9D)
    

If you’re having trouble accessing a [Go Link](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-go-links-work)
, this guide will walk you through potential solutions to that problem.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting#if-you-are-seeing-an-error-saying-%E2%80%9Cthis-site-can%E2%80%99t-be-reached%E2%80%9D)

If you are seeing an error saying “This site can’t be reached”
==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

Check to make sure you have the Glean Browser Extension installed and enabled. Go Links require the latest version of the extension to function. In Chrome, go to [chrome://extensions](chrome://extensions)
, and look for Glean. If it’s not there, download the extension from the Chrome Web Store. ![The Glean extension in Chrome, with a toggle showing that it is enabled](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting-go-links-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=b19ecff8e81f4aeea86adaff547939bd) If it is there, make sure that it’s enabled: the toggle in the lower right corner should be switched on and over to the right. After that, quit and reopen Chrome, and try accessing the Go Link again. If that still doesn’t work, go to the [Go Links directory](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/golinks)
. You may need to enable additional permissions from the banner at the top of the directory to make Go Links function in your browser.

* * *

![Screenshot of text showing "Oops, no Go Link here" taken from the Go Links error page](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting-go-links-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=86c483632e860db9feaf049193d7a48d)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/troubleshooting#if-you-are-seeing-a-page-that-says-%E2%80%9Coops-no-go-link-here-%E2%80%9D)

If you are seeing a page that says “Oops, no Go Link here!”
===================================================================================================================================================================================================================

Check to make sure you typed the Go Link correctly. If you have typed it correctly, it’s possible that you were accidentally given the wrong Go Link. You can search Glean for the correct resource, or filter the [Go Links Directory](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/golinks)
 for your organization to find it. If someone else gave you the Go Link, check with them to make sure they sent you the right one.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

# Citations - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Citations in Glean Chat

Citations

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Citation omissions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#citation-omissions)
    
*   [Troubleshooting steps](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#troubleshooting-steps)
    
*   [FAQs](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#faqs)
    

A citation is a reference to a source Glean used. They appear within the response, immediately following statements that need backing, and provide a brief pointer to the document, file, or person so readers can locate and evaluate the source. Citations never grant new access. You can only open items you already have permission to view. Source previews and links inherit data controls and retention policies of your organization. Glean Chat cites the following resources:

*   Documents and pages like Google Docs, Confluence, SharePoint pages.
*   Files like PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets where a specific portion supports the claim.
*   People records when the answer reflects a person as a source like an author or owner reference.

You see citations in the assitant responses as:

*   Inline markers and a **View sources** section show the sources used for the answer.
*   Hover over a citation to preview context.
*   Opening a source shows a preview so you can confirm you are looking at the right place before you click through.
*   Clicking a source opens the original item in its native application like Google Drive, Confluence, SharePoint which is subject to your access.

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/BqhwexhcdIE-TMD5/images/citations.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=BqhwexhcdIE-TMD5&q=85&s=7be859dfb3e6a8b5af14cc293c1f0e63)

When deep linked citations are enabled, you are directed to the exact passage in the source that supports the statement, with the referenced text so that you can verify it.

*   Hover previews highlight the cited text and show surrounding context.
*   For certain file types, the link may jump to a specific section, for example, a slide within a presentation when available.
*   Page numbers are also displayed for the appropriate sources.

Deep linking availability and behavior can vary by data source and admin settings.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#citation-omissions)

Citation omissions

*   If no enterprise sources are searched and the answer relies solely on world knowledge, citations are not generated and this is an expected behavior.

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/8nhcLIfHfoAjQsqY/images/no-citations.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=8nhcLIfHfoAjQsqY&q=85&s=a35fbcbb5f742bb687862cd659caefe9)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#troubleshooting-steps)

Troubleshooting steps

*   Missing citations: Rephrase your question to anchor to company specific knowledge. Add or tag the relevant documentation URLs, for example, “According to our FY25 strategy doc…”.
*   Incorrect or low‑quality citations: If you see incorrect citations, share your feedback by clicking on the **thumbs down** button and select the category as _Used incorrect citation or source_.

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/BqhwexhcdIE-TMD5/images/citation-feedback.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=BqhwexhcdIE-TMD5&q=85&s=0e8173f6b9e4794199b0043a0f80df35)

*   Can not open a cited item: You can only open items you already have permission to view. Citations should not come for sources that you do not have access to, however they will remain in history if permission is revoked. Source previews and links inherit data controls and retention policies of your organization.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/glean-citations#faqs)

FAQs

Are citations always present?

Citation are not always returned. Citations appear when the answer relies on enterprise sources. Responses that only come from public knowledge will not have citations.

Do citations affect answer quality?

Citations have been shown to steer responses to being more truthful. However, the absence of citations is not an indication that a response is hallucinated or wrong.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[Deep-linked citations (Beta)](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations)

⌘I

*   [Always Verify Solutions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#always-verify-solutions)
    
*   [Use Specific Ticket References](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#use-specific-ticket-references)
    
*   [Prioritize Recent Information](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#prioritize-recent-information)
    
*   [Combine Sources](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#combine-sources)
    
*   [Request Confidence Indicators](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#request-confidence-indicators)
    
*   [Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#troubleshooting)
    
*   [Related Resources](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#related-resources)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#overview)

Overview
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

Support teams use Glean’s MCP server to quickly find solutions, understand ticket context, and deliver accurate responses by accessing the full enterprise knowledge base.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#prerequisites)

Prerequisites
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Recommended connectors:**

*   Zendesk or ServiceNow (for ticketing)
*   Slack
*   Confluence or Notion (for internal knowledge bases)
*   GitHub (for technical issues)
*   Jira (for bug tracking)
*   Google Drive (for documentation)

**Supported MCP hosts:**

*   Claude Desktop
*   ChatGPT
*   Any MCP-compatible interface
*   Embedded in support workflows

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#use-cases)

Use Cases
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#1-ticket-triage-and-next-steps)

1\. Ticket Triage and Next Steps

Get immediate recommendations for how to handle incoming tickets based on past resolutions.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Given Zendesk ticket #12345, use Glean to: 
    1. Summarize the customer's issue 
    2. Find similar resolved tickets from the past 
    3. Check for related bugs in Jira 
    4. Identify the likely root cause 
    5. Suggest next steps and who to escalate to if needed 
    6. Provide confidence level for your recommendation.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Use Glean to triage this ticket. Search for similar issues, known bugs,
    and workarounds. Tell me if this is a quick fix or needs engineering.
    

Copy

    This customer is reporting [error]. Search Glean for past tickets with
    this error and show me how they were resolved.

**What it does:**

*   Analyzes ticket content and metadata
*   Finds similar historical tickets
*   Checks for known bugs or ongoing incidents
*   Recommends resolution path and priority
*   Suggests escalation if confidence is low

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#2-find-similar-resolved-tickets)

2\. Find Similar Resolved Tickets

Quickly locate past solutions without manual searching.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Search Glean for Zendesk tickets about login errors in the last 90 days.
    Show me the top 3 most common solutions and which customers were affected.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Use Glean to find all resolved tickets about [specific error code].
    What's the pattern? Is there a common fix? 
    

Copy

    This issue looks familiar. Search Glean for similar tickets from enterprise customers and
    show me verified solutions.

**What it does:**

*   Semantic search across ticket history
*   Identifies resolution patterns
*   Filters by customer segment or date
*   Shows verified solutions

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#3-build-contextual-ticket-timeline)

3\. Build Contextual Ticket Timeline

Synthesize full customer context across all systems.

Example Prompt

Copy

    For customer [Company Name], use Glean to create a timeline of: 
    1. All open and recent closed support tickets 
    2. Escalations in the #customer-success Slack channel 
    3. Recent Salesforce activity and account health 
    4. Product usage or feature requests from sales calls Highlight any
    patterns or recurring issues.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Build a detailed support ticket timeline for ticket #12345. Use Glean to
    find related Slack threads, Jira bugs, and any internal discussions. 
    

Copy

    Search Glean for everything related to [customer name]: tickets,
    escalations, bugs, and sales notes. Summarize their experience.

**What it does:**

*   Aggregates customer interactions across systems
*   Creates chronological timeline of issues
*   Identifies recurring themes
*   Surfaces relevant internal discussions

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#4-draft-ticket-responses)

4\. Draft Ticket Responses

Generate accurate, context-aware responses based on knowledge base and past solutions.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Draft a response to Zendesk ticket #12345. Use Glean to: 
    1. Find our official documentation about this feature 
    2. Check for similar tickets and how they were resolved 
    3. Look for any known issues in Jira 
    4. Use our response tone guidelines from the support wiki Keep it concise, empathetic,
    and include relevant links.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    This customer is asking about [feature]. Use Glean to draft a response
    with links to help docs and relevant examples. 
    

Copy

    Search Glean for our troubleshooting guide for [issue] and draft a step-by-step response I can
    send to this customer.

**What it does:**

*   Pulls from official documentation
*   Incorporates successful past responses
*   Matches your support tone and style
*   Includes appropriate links and resources

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#5-identify-knowledge-base-gaps)

5\. Identify Knowledge Base Gaps

Find recurring issues that need better documentation.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Use Glean to analyze closed tickets from the last 30 days. 
    
    Identify: 1. Common issues that took multiple back-and-forths to resolve 
    2. Topics where agents had to escalate to engineering 
    3. Questions not covered in our current help docs 
    4. Feature confusion points mentioned repeatedly Suggest new knowledge base articles we should create.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for Zendesk tickets about [topic]. Are there gaps in our
    documentation? What questions keep coming up? 
    

Copy

    Use Glean to find all tickets where agents said "let me check with engineering." What documentation would prevent these escalations?

**What it does:**

*   Analyzes ticket patterns
*   Identifies documentation gaps
*   Finds frequently escalated issues
*   Recommends content creation priorities

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#6-technical-troubleshooting)

6\. Technical Troubleshooting

Access technical documentation and past solutions for complex issues.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Customer is reporting error code ERR_AUTH_401. Use Glean to: 
    1. Search technical docs for this error code 
    2. Find GitHub issues or PRs related to authentication errors 
    3. Check Slack #engineering for recent authentication issues 
    4. Look for similar Zendesk tickets and their resolutions 
    
    Provide a troubleshooting checklist and known solutions.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Use Glean to help debug this technical issue. Search our runbooks,
    GitHub discussions, and past tickets for solutions.
    

Copy

    This looks like a backend issue. Search Glean for recent incidents, related Jira bugs, and
    engineering team notes about this service.

**What it does:**

*   Searches technical documentation and runbooks
*   Connects to engineering systems (GitHub, Jira)
*   Finds past technical resolutions
*   Provides systematic troubleshooting steps

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#7-customer-sentiment-analysis)

7\. Customer Sentiment Analysis

Understand customer satisfaction trends and identify at-risk accounts.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Use Glean to analyze customer sentiment from tickets and escalations for
    [customer name]. 
    
    Check: 
    1. Recent ticket volume and resolution times 
    2. Escalations or negative feedback in Slack 
    3. CSAT scores from resolved tickets 
    4. Mentions in customer success check-ins Is this account at risk?
    
    What are the main pain points?
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for all tickets from enterprise customers in the last
    quarter. Which accounts are opening the most tickets? What's the theme?
    

Copy

    Use Glean to check sentiment across all support channels for [customer].
    Are they happy with our support?

**What it does:**

*   Aggregates feedback across channels
*   Identifies sentiment trends
*   Flags at-risk customers
*   Surfaces recurring pain points

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#8-onboarding-new-support-agents)

8\. Onboarding New Support Agents

Help new team members ramp up quickly with instant access to tribal knowledge.

Example Prompt

Copy

    I'm new to the support team. Use Glean to: 1. Find our support process
    documentation 2. Show me examples of well-handled complex tickets 3. Locate
    our product documentation and common troubleshooting guides 4. Tell me who
    the subject matter experts are for different product areas
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Use Glean to explain our escalation process. When should I escalate to
    engineering vs customer success vs product? 
    

Copy

    Search Glean for training materials and best practices for handling [type of issue]. Show me
    examples of good responses.

**What it does:**

*   Locates onboarding documentation
*   Finds example tickets and responses
*   Identifies subject matter experts
*   Surfaces process guidelines

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#9-bug-verification-and-reporting)

9\. Bug Verification and Reporting

Determine if a ticket is a bug and gather information for engineering.

Example Prompt

Copy

    This ticket might be a bug. Use Glean to: 1. Check if this is a known
    issue in Jira 2. Find similar reports from other customers 3. Search for
    recent code changes that might be related 4. Look for engineering
    discussions about this area Should I file a new bug report? If yes, draft it
    with all relevant context.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for other reports of [issue]. Is this a pattern? Gather
    repro steps and affected customers for a bug report. 
    

Copy

    Use Glean to check if engineering knows about this issue. Search Jira, GitHub, and Slack
    #engineering for mentions.

**What it does:**

*   Checks for existing bug reports
*   Finds duplicate reports from other customers
*   Gathers reproduction steps
*   Drafts comprehensive bug reports

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#10-feature-request-validation)

10\. Feature Request Validation

Understand the scope and priority of feature requests.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Customer is requesting [feature]. 
    
    Use Glean to: 
    1. Check if this is already on the roadmap (Jira, product docs) 
    2. Find how many other customers have requested this 
    3. Look for related sales conversations or escalations
    4. Search for any product strategy docs mentioning this area 
    5. Summarize demand and provide an informed response to the customer.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for all feature requests about [capability]. How many
    customers want this? What's the business impact? 
    

Copy

    Use Glean to check our product roadmap and recent planning docs. Is [feature] planned? When
    might it ship?

**What it does:**

*   Checks roadmap and planning docs
*   Aggregates feature request volume
*   Assesses business impact
*   Provides informed customer responses

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#best-practices)

Best Practices
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#always-verify-solutions)

Always Verify Solutions

Copy

    After Glean suggests a solution, verify it matches current product behavior
    before sending to customers. Documentation can be outdated.
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#use-specific-ticket-references)

Use Specific Ticket References

Copy

    ✅ "Given Zendesk ticket #12345..."
    ✅ "For customer Acme Corp (SFDC account 001...)..."
    ❌ "This customer has a problem..." (too vague)
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#prioritize-recent-information)

Prioritize Recent Information

Copy

    Search Glean for solutions from the last 30 days - our product changes
    frequently and older solutions may not apply.
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#combine-sources)

Combine Sources

Copy

    Search both our public help docs and internal troubleshooting guides.
    Internal docs often have more detailed technical steps.
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#request-confidence-indicators)

Request Confidence Indicators

Copy

    Include your confidence level and reasoning. If confidence is low, suggest
    I escalate to [specific team/person].
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#troubleshooting)

Troubleshooting
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Getting irrelevant ticket results?**

*   Use ticket status filters (“resolved tickets only”)
*   Specify date ranges (“in the last 90 days”)
*   Add customer segment context (“enterprise customers”)

**Responses too technical or too vague?**

*   Reference your response guidelines: “Use our support tone guide from Confluence”
*   Specify audience: “Draft a response for a non-technical user”
*   Provide examples: “Similar to how we handled ticket #98765”

**Missing internal context?**

*   Verify Slack channels are indexed (#support, #customer-success, #engineering)
*   Check that internal wikis and runbooks are connected
*   Ensure Jira connector includes relevant projects

**Slow response times?**

*   Break complex queries into smaller steps
*   Search specific data sources rather than everything
*   Use ticket IDs or error codes for faster lookups

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/support#related-resources)

Related Resources
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [MCP Setup Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/usage)
     - Initial configuration

     - Overview of support use cases
*   [Chat Best Practices](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices)
     - Prompting tips

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#discoverability)

Discoverability
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#go-links-extension-autocomplete)

Go Links extension autocomplete

With the Glean extension, teammates can search for Go Links directly from their URL bar. Type in **gl + space** to activate the Glean URL bar shortcut, then search for a Go Link. Below is an example of typing **gl + space**, then **go/product**. Glean’s autocomplete suggests Go Links matching **go/product**. ![Go Links Autocomplete](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=7d6b8342a976334c902be4b5e3cd959f)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#go-links-spellcheck)

Go Links spellcheck

When a teammate misspells a Go Link or wants to check if a certain Go Link already exists, Glean will show a page informing that the Go Link was not found. It will also suggest similar Go Links. Below is an example of typing **go/feedback**, then getting recommendations of similar Go Links. ![Go Links Spellcheck](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=3460ec1c0f83f7ea13067a6044284293)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#permissions)

Permissions
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#view-and-edit-permissions)

View and edit permissions

Teammates can choose to [unlist their Go Link](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/unlisted-go-links)
 so it cannot be seen by other teammates as well as restrict edit access to only themselves. Go Links moderators and admins are still able to edit Go Links with restricted edit access.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#go-links-moderator-role)

Go Links moderator role

Admins can set certain teammates as Go Links moderators. As a moderator, they can edit Go Links, even those that have been set to be non-editable by their creators. This is useful in cases where a Go Link needs to be updated or corrected, but the original creator is not available or has left the organization Watch how to set teammate permissions on the [Admin Guide: Teammates Tab](https://docs.glean.com/administration/identity/roles/manage-users)
 page.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links#go-links-dashboard)

Go Links Dashboard

Create and manage all Go Links from the dashboard which can be found under [Content > Knowledge > Go Links](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/golinks)
 Filter Go Links by creator, department, and Go Link type (Unlisted, Public, Variable). ![Go Links Dashboard](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/how-to-make-the-most-of-go-links-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=49b6a5869f8e413d2a34d87b0cf91c51)

# Unlisted Go Links - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/unlisted-go-links#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Go Links

Unlisted Go Links

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [How to create an Unlisted Go Link](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/unlisted-go-links#how-to-create-an-unlisted-go-link)
    

Unlisted Go Links are Go Links that are only visible to the creator of the Go Link and admins of Glean. Unlisted Go Links are great for private projects you don’t want others to accidentally discover.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/unlisted-go-links#how-to-create-an-unlisted-go-link)

How to create an Unlisted Go Link

![Create Unlisted Go Link](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/unlisted-go-links-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=14279067136e3f2f46e1fb4544252ff7)

*   Click on “**New Go Link**” on your [Go Links dashboard](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/golinks)
     or on the Glean Home page
*   Fill out the form with a URL, name, and the description
*   Check “**Unlist**”

Your Go Link will no longer be discoverable on the Go Links dashboard by other users. A user who knows the Go Link and types it directly into the address bar can still access the Go Link. To make an unlisted Go Link discoverable to others again, simply uncheck the “**Unlist**.”

# Create Variable Go Links - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/create-variable-go-links#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Go Links

Create Variable Go Links

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [How to create a Variable Go Link](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/create-variable-go-links#how-to-create-a-variable-go-link)
    
*   [Examples](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/create-variable-go-links#examples)
    

Variable Go Links redirect to specific destinations when a custom value like a ticket # are added, powering hundreds of pages within a website. Users can make any part of an URL variable with a denotation and append Go Link URL to target specific destinations such as search results page or support tickets.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/create-variable-go-links#how-to-create-a-variable-go-link)

How to create a Variable Go Link

1.  Click on “New Go Link” on your [Go Links dashboard](https://app.glean.com/knowledge/golinks)
     or Glean Home Page.
2.  Fill in the Destination URL and identify the part of the URL (parameter) to make variable and replace it with **`{*}`.** You will see “**Variable**” pop up when it detects a\*\*`{*}`\*\*.

*   Example: **`glean.atlassian.net/123`** -> **`glean.atlassian.net/{*}`**

3.  Fill in “Default Go Link to”. This is to specify the default destination URL for the Go Link without a variable.

*   Example: You can specify **go/jira** to be redirected to **glean.atlassian.net**

Note: When typing a variable Go Link, the substitution would be denoted by another “/” following go/jira (E.g. **go/jira/123**). There can be multiple variable substitutions in a single URL (E.g. **go/jira/123/abc**) ![Create Variable Go Link](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/create-variable-go-links-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=270efd4b3148c6e26ca22e4a8eee88cd)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/go-links/create-variable-go-links#examples)

Examples

Use Variable Go Links to quickly navigate to search result pages

*   `https://app.glean.com/search?q={*}` -> `https://app.glean.com/search?q=expense-policy`
*   `https://github.com/search?q={*}` -> `https://github.com/search?q=fastapi`

\*Note that Variable Go Links support spaces (E.g. you can search for “how do go links work” using “_go/glean/how do go links work”_ Use Variable Go Links to quickly navigate to specific issues and tickets

*   `https://app.atlassian.net/browse/PROD-{*}` -> `https://app.atlassian.net/browse/PROD-79845`
*   `https://github.com/product/{*}/issues/{*}` -> `https://github.com/product/featureX/issues/BUG-1234`

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

Navigation

Enable or disable Deep Research

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

You can enable or disable Deep Research for your organization or specific users in the Admin Console. Perform the following steps:

1.  Navigate to [Admin Console → Platform → Assistant → Settings](https://app.glean.com/admin/setup/gleanassistant?tab=settings)
    
2.  Expand the **Deep Research** section.
3.  Select one of the following:
    *   **On for everyone** (recommended if Assistant is enabled)
    *   **On for some teammates** (test group via allowlist)
    *   **Off** (disable for everyone)
4.  Select **Save**.

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Home Page

Celebrations Widget

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)

On this page

*   [What is the celebrations widget?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#what-is-the-celebrations-widget)
    
*   [How can I congratulate my teammate?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#how-can-i-congratulate-my-teammate)
    
*   [Who shows up on my widget?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#who-shows-up-on-my-widget)
    
*   [What determines the order of the celebrations shown on the widget?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#what-determines-the-order-of-the-celebrations-shown-on-the-widget)
    
*   [I don’t want others to know about my celebration. How do I opt out?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#i-don%E2%80%99t-want-others-to-know-about-my-celebration-how-do-i-opt-out)
    
*   [Where does the celebration data come from?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#where-does-the-celebration-data-come-from)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#what-is-the-celebrations-widget)

What is the celebrations widget?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The celebrations widget helps you and your teammates celebrate each other by recognizing new hires and work anniversaries. The celebration widget appears in the right-hand column of the Glean home page and displays a carousel of teammates who have reached a new work anniversary or recently joined the team. ![Celebrations Widget](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/what-is-the-celebrations-widget-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=fb6ebe619397947e69ea45bc4ef2283c)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#how-can-i-congratulate-my-teammate)

How can I congratulate my teammate?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can click on the “say welcome/congrats” button to send a message to the celebrated teammate through your organization’s chat application such as Slack or Teams. If the teammate does not have a Slack or Teams profile, clicking on the button will launch your organization’s email application instead.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#who-shows-up-on-my-widget)

Who shows up on my widget?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Glean will display up to 10 celebrations in the widget at any time. The selection is personalized, so you will see celebrations for teammates with whom you have a relationship. Cards will be visible for the entire week of the celebration.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#what-determines-the-order-of-the-celebrations-shown-on-the-widget)

What determines the order of the celebrations shown on the widget?
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are being celebrated, your card will always appear first. Next, the widget will show teammates who have anniversaries or new start dates today, followed by teammates who have upcoming anniversaries, and then teammates whose anniversaries have past.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#i-don%E2%80%99t-want-others-to-know-about-my-celebration-how-do-i-opt-out)

I don’t want others to know about my celebration. How do I opt out?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Select “Your settings” from the avatar dropdown menu. ![Your Settings](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/what-is-the-celebrations-widget-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=7a29236c59c2c63655b46435814093a8)
2.  Under “Privacy,” toggle off the option to feature your celebrations on the home page. ![Privacy Settings](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/what-is-the-celebrations-widget-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=9cb1a8bb3ffd38a6d35a9fb5560206b9)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/celebrations-widget#where-does-the-celebration-data-come-from)

Where does the celebration data come from?
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The celebrations widget uses start date data from your company’s people data source (HR or IT system) to determine when to showcase particular teammates. If that source data is incorrect, you will see celebrations appear on incorrect days.

# Introduction to Glean Chat - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Chat

Introduction to Glean Chat

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Key features](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#key-features)
    
*   [Search results in Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#search-results-in-chat)
    
*   [Accessing Glean Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#accessing-glean-chat)
    

![Glean Chat Interface](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-/images/chat/chat.20240204194215597.webp?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=NkMg3HtjZdwnwbZ-&q=85&s=3a1ec09c6348796e275670b433f4d608)

Glean Chat serves as the primary interface for interacting with Glean Assistant, offering a familiar chat-like experience similar to contemporary AI assistants. This interface enables users to engage with their company’s knowledge base through natural conversations, as they would with a colleague.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#key-features)

Key features
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Personalized responses based on each user’s permissions.
*   High-confidence answers with verifiable citations.
*   Natural language interaction with your organization’s knowledge base.
*   Glean Chat surfaces a short set of high‑confidence search results directly in the chat window for keyword or search‑like queries. For more information see, [Search results in Glean Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#search-results-in-chat)
    .

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#search-results-in-chat)

Search results in Chat

Glean Chat now surfaces a short set of high‑confidence search results directly in the chat window for keyword or search‑like queries. This helps you jump straight to relevant documents and people without switching to the Search page or waiting for a full conversational answer. You will see top results inline when you enter a keyword or search‑style query, for example, a document title, team name, or a short term. Use specific keywords like _Quarterly plan_ or _OKR 2025_ to trigger the inline results. **How it works**

1.  Type a keyword or short, search‑like query in Glean Chat.
2.  A compact set of top results appears inline above the streamed chat answer.
3.  Click any result to open it in a new tab. You can keep chatting as usual if you still want a conversational response.
4.  To browse more results, select **View all search results** to open the full Search results page.
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/BYMz8zHTqh9_yeQ0/images/chat-search-result.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=BYMz8zHTqh9_yeQ0&q=85&s=5e9fbb86a7ab4fcf84f2e8f0c64b820f)
    

When available, Glean Chat can use Zoom transcript content you have permission to access. For more information, see [Use Zoom meeting transcripts in Search, Chat, and Home](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts)
.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat#accessing-glean-chat)

Accessing Glean Chat
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can access Glean Chat in two ways:

Navigation Menu
---------------

Click the message bubble icon in the left-side menu of the Glean UI

[Direct Access\
-------------\
\
Visit the Glean Chat interface directly](https://app.glean.com/chat)

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Meetings & Calendar

Use Zoom meeting transcripts in Search, Chat, and Home

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#prerequisites)
    
*   [How to retrieve meeting information using Glean?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#how-to-retrieve-meeting-information-using-glean)
    
*   [Find meeting transcripts in Search](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#find-meeting-transcripts-in-search)
    
*   [Ask questions in Glean Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#ask-questions-in-glean-chat)
    
*   [Summarize past meetings from Home](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#summarize-past-meetings-from-home)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#limitations)
    
*   [Privacy and security](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#privacy-and-security)
    
*   [Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#troubleshooting)
    
*   [Additional information](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#additional-information)
    

When the Zoom connector is enabled, Glean will help you find and use the Zoom meeting transcripts and their recording links across **Search**, **Chat**, and the **Home** page:

*   Glean Chat can use Zoom transcript content that you have permission to access to answer questions.
*   You can summarize past Zoom meetings from the Calendar widget on the Glean Home page.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#prerequisites)

Prerequisites
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The Zoom connector is installed and authorized by your admin. For more information, see [Zoom connector](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/zoom/about)
    .
*   Your Zoom plan supports cloud recordings and transcription, and cloud recording is enabled in Zoom.
*   Access is permission-aware. Glean will only surface meetings and transcript content you are allowed to access in Zoom.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#how-to-retrieve-meeting-information-using-glean)

How to retrieve meeting information using Glean?
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#find-meeting-transcripts-in-search)

Find meeting transcripts in Search

*   Search naturally, for example, “quarterly roadmap review decisions” to see relevant Zoom meetings in your results.
*   Meeting results include a link to play the cloud recording in Zoom and are ranked by relevance alongside your other work content.
*   Opening a Zoom meeting result takes you to the transcript destination in Zoom. Depending on your Zoom configuration, the transcript and recording are hosted and viewed in Zoom.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#ask-questions-in-glean-chat)

Ask questions in Glean Chat

*   Ask questions like “What did we decide about Q1 hiring in last week’s sales leadership sync?” or “Summarize the action items from the customer escalation call.”
*   Chat uses the transcript content you have permission to access and includes citations as usual within Glean.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#summarize-past-meetings-from-home)

Summarize past meetings from Home

*   On the Glean Home page, open the Calendar widget.
*   Select a past meeting to generate a concise summary of key topics, decisions, and action items.
*   For more information, see [Calendar widget](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings/calendar-widget)
    .

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#limitations)

Limitations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Transcript visibility is currently limited to the meeting organizer.
*   Transcript and recording availability depend on your Zoom plan and settings, cloud recordings must be enabled.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#privacy-and-security)

Privacy and security
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Glean enforces your enterprise permissions and Zoom access controls. Users only see meetings and transcripts they are allowed to access.
*   Connector data is stored in the cloud environment of your organization as per your Glean deployment model.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#troubleshooting)

Troubleshooting
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   I can not see transcripts I expect:
    *   Confirm you were the meeting organizer. Only organizers can access transcripts in Glean today.
    *   Check that the Zoom connector is successfully connected and crawled in the Glean Admin Console.
*   I can not authorize the Zoom app:
    *   Your Zoom admin may need to approve the _Glean Search Crawler for Zoom_ in the Zoom App Marketplace per your tenant policies.
*   Search results open in Zoom instead of a preview:
    *   This is expected, Zoom hosts the transcript and recording. Glean links you to the Zoom destination.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/meetings-and-calendar/zoom-meeting-transcripts#additional-information)

Additional information
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Zoom connector](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/native/zoom/about)
    .
*   [Calendar Search Actions setup](https://docs.glean.com/administration/assistant/actions/calendar-search-actions-setup)
    .
*   [How to search in Glean](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/basics/how-to-search-in-glean)
    .

# Chat documentation autocomplete - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Chat

Chat documentation autocomplete

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Privacy and permissions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#privacy-and-permissions)
    
*   [Get started](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#get-started)
    
*   [FAQs](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#faqs)
    

Chat autocomplete in Glean Assistant suggests relevant documents as you type in the chat input. You can click a suggestion to insert it into your message or open the document directly without sending a separate query. As you type keywords or phrases, Assistant surfaces real‑time document suggestions below the input. Suggestions are selected by matching your input and context with document titles, content, and metadata so the most relevant items appear first.

Suggestions currently focus on documents and related files that match your input and context.

With this feature you can:

*   Find the right document faster while composing a prompt without switching tabs or context.
*   Insert a suggested document into your message to give Assistant immediate context.
*   Open a suggested document in a new tab to review details before you ask a question.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#privacy-and-permissions)

Privacy and permissions
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Autocomplete suggestions only include content that you are allowed to see. Glean is permission aware and follows the data governance rules of your organization across all surfaces.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#get-started)

Get started
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Open Glean Assistant and start a chat.
2.  In the input, type a keyword, for example, project, doc title, or team name.
3.  Pick a suggestion to either:
    
    *   Insert the document into your message, or
    *   Open the document to view it.
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/ZU_FxjxCM0Ero0Ky/images/autocomplete-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=ZU_FxjxCM0Ero0Ky&q=85&s=ed5b61f9511946ec7435aa01ce6a327d)
    
    ![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/ZU_FxjxCM0Ero0Ky/images/autocomplete-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=ZU_FxjxCM0Ero0Ky&q=85&s=6192a28404f90b2c67950154bbdd8b90)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-autocomplete#faqs)

FAQs
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

How are suggestions ranked?

By relevance based on your input and context, using signals from titles, content, and metadata to prioritize likely matches.

Do I need to send my question first to get suggestions?

No, suggestions appear as you type, and you can insert or open a document before sending your message.

# Deep-linked citations (Beta) - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Navigation

Citations in Glean Chat

Deep-linked citations (Beta)

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Enabling deep-linked citations for Glean Chat](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#enabling-deep-linked-citations-for-glean-chat)
    
*   [Prerequisites](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#prerequisites)
    
*   [Step](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#step)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#limitations)
    

A citation is a reference to a source Glean Chat used. It includes a brief pointer to the document, allowing you to locate and evaluate the information. A deep-linked citation links directly to the exact passage that supports the statement. This allows you to instantly verify the response and explore the surrounding context without searching the entire document. With deep-linked citations, hovering over a citation shows significant details about the cited content.

![](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/1sli_BkG9psiygr5/images/deep-link-citation.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=1sli_BkG9psiygr5&q=85&s=edf7e18946f816b8fc81724cfa8af44f)

You will see the cited text highlighted in blue, with the rest of the paragraph visible for full context. In addition to the source information, the citation popover displays the page number. You can also select the **Expand** button in the top-right corner to view a preview of the source. For certain file types, the link may jump to a specific section, for example, a slide within a presentation when available.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#enabling-deep-linked-citations-for-glean-chat)

Enabling deep-linked citations for Glean Chat
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#prerequisites)

Prerequisites

*   Ensure that agentic loop for Glean Chat is turned on.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#step)

Step

As deep-linked citation is a beta feature, to opt-in for this feature, contact your AE.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/glean-chat-citations/deep-linked-citations#limitations)

Limitations
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Deep-linked citations for complex content Currently, deep-linked citations sourced from non-plaintext content such as tables and code blocks—are displayed as simple plaintext. This presentation may make the cited information difficult to read or parse.
*   Source Navigation The ability to click on a citation and navigate directly to the cited page is not yet supported in this Beta release.

On this page

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#overview)
    
*   [Key Capabilities](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#key-capabilities)
    
*   [How It Works](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#how-it-works)
    
*   [Use Cases](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#use-cases)
    
*   [Benefits](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#benefits)
    
*   [Additional resources](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#additional-resources)
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#overview)

Overview

Glean canvas is a feature that enables users to draft, iterate, and refine written content directly within the Glean platform. Canvas is designed to streamline the process of collecting your ideas, creating documents, reports, proposals, and other written materials by leveraging Glean’s AI-powered writing assistance. This feature is available to users as part of the Glean Assistant experience and is intended to help individuals and teams produce high-quality content efficiently. No configuration is needed. Canvas and the personalized writing features work out of the box using your existing data.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#key-capabilities)

Key Capabilities

*   **Drafting Content**: Users can start a new document and receive AI-powered suggestions to help structure and compose their writing.
*   **Direct Editing:** Users can type directly into the canvas interface if they want to add their own information to the document. All changes are automatically saved.
*   **Targeted Editing:** Users can highlight a section of the canvas and prompt Assistant to make changes based on the highlighted area.
*   **Iterative Editing**: Canvas supports multiple rounds of editing, allowing users to refine their drafts with feedback and suggestions from the Assistant.
*   **Personalized Writing Style**: Assistant adapts to each user’s unique writing style by analyzing existing communications across connected platforms. This ensures that generated content matches the user’s tone, structure, and vocabulary, requiring minimal manual revision. This is done automatically and does not require any additional setup.
*   **Seamless Integration**: Canvas is integrated into the Glean workspace, making it easy to access and use alongside other Glean features.
*   **Citations:** View exactly where the content came from with a sources section at the bottom of the document.
*   **Version History:** Want to go back to a previous canvas and pull in some information? You can easily go back to previous versions with version history in the upper right-hand corner.
*   **Discoverability:** Look for the canvas icon in your conversation thread history to see which threads have a canvas at a glance.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#how-it-works)

How It Works

1.  **Start a New Document**: Users can trigger a canvas one of two ways:

*   Tell Assistant you want to work on some writing-oriented task, such as, “Write a report summarizing everything I accomplished this week.”
*   Manual trigger by clicking the + menu in the composer and selecting canvas from there.
*   If you got a response in chat that you want to work on within a canvas, you can also click the “Edit in canvas” button at the bottom of the response.

2.  **Receive AI Assistance**: The Assistant provides suggestions for structure, phrasing, and content based on the user’s intent and previous writing patterns.
3.  **Finalize and Share**: Once the document is complete, users can export it to Google Docs or Microsoft Word, share it via email or Slack, or continue collaborating within the Glean platform.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#use-cases)

Use Cases

*   **Drafting Emails and Reports**: Quickly compose professional emails, reports, or proposals that reflect your authentic voice.
*   **Content Personalization**: Ensure that all written materials align with individual or organizational communication standards.
*   **Creating Meeting Summaries and Action Items**: Generate meeting recaps with key decisions and next steps by pulling from transcripts, notes, and follow-up messages.
*   **Building Knowledge Base Articles**: Draft internal documentation and how-to guides that maintain consistent terminology while referencing existing company knowledge.
*   **Generating Status Reports**: Compile progress updates by aggregating information from project tools, emails, and conversations to highlight accomplishments, blockers, and milestones.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#benefits)

Benefits

*   **Maintains Authenticity**: Content generated in canvas sounds like the user, not generic AI output.
*   **Saves Time**: Reduces the need for extensive manual editing by producing drafts that closely match the user’s style.
*   **Consistent Branding**: Supports a unified communication style across all documents and teams.
*   **No Configuration Required**: The Assistant works immediately, using existing data from connected platforms—no setup or sample uploads needed.

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/canvas#additional-resources)

Additional resources

For more information or assistance with Glean canvas, please contact your Glean customer success representative or support team.

# Code Search - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/code-search#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Chat

Code Search

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Security](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/code-search#security)
    
*   [Usage examples](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/code-search#usage-examples)
    

The Code Search capability in Glean primarily runs as an automatic tool inside Glean Assistant, helping you find and reason over code across your connected repositories. You can also invoke it explicitly through the Code Search action in Agents when you need tighter control or custom query patterns. There is no initial setup required for Assistant to start using Code Search. After you connect your code hosts like GitHub, GitHub Enterprise Server, GitLab or GitLab server, the Assistant uses Code Search automatically. Code Search is supported for GitHub cloud, GitHub Enterprise cloud, GitLab On-prem, GitHub On-prem enterprise server, Gitlab self-managed On-prem, Bitbucket cloud, Bitbucket On-Prem and for Glean deployments on GCP, AWS and Azure. This functionality is designed to assist with various code-related tasks, such as:

*   **Finding and understanding code**: Helps users locate specific code snippets, understand code changes, and navigate through code repositories.
*   **Creating code snippets**: Allows users to generate code snippets based on their queries.
*   **Debugging**: Assists in identifying recent changes that may be causing errors by searching through recent pull requests and commits.
*   **Feature status checks**: Provides information on whether certain features have been implemented and how they can be enabled by searching related code files and documentation.

The Assistant automatically detects when you have a question about your code. For example, if you ask,

*   “What changed in X?”
*   “Where is Y implemented?”
*   “How do I add Z endpoint?” It runs a Code Search and uses the code from your connected hosts. The Code Search feature respects the access permissions to give you the right results.

With Code Search, the Assistant gives answers with code context and uses its understanding of your code, commits, and pull requests to give you a full summary. It can suggest likely code locations, show code differences (diffs) and point to previous implementations.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/code-search#security)

Security
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Code Search protects private repositories as it inherits the same security model as your code connectors. Glean respects your privacy and operates with high security guardrails so processing stays within your environment. **All data is stored in your cloud project—no data leaves your environment.** Glean also enforces user access permissions at query time, ensuring users only see data they are authorized to view.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/code-search#usage-examples)

Usage examples
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Debugging Errors**: A user can search for recent code changes that might have introduced errors into a specific component. The tool will look through recent commits and pull requests to identify potential causes and summarize them. **Example**: “What recent changes could have caused errors in runtime configs?” The tool might identify PRs that addressed configuration errors and suggest reviewing those changes.
*   **Feature Implementation Status**: A user can inquire about the implementation status of a specific feature. The tool can identify if the feature has been implemented, locate the documentation, and even provide instructions on enabling it. **Example**: “Is indexing of code content from GitHub files enabled?” The agent would confirm if the feature is active and outline the steps to enable it.
*   **Code Implementation Guidance**: A user, unsure where to start with implementing a new feature, can get guidance on which files to modify based on past similar changes. **Example**: “Where can I add a new endpoint in QE?” The tool would search for similar past implementations and provide sample code snippets and file locations to get started.

For information on using Code Search as an action in agents, see [Code Search action](https://docs.glean.com/agents/actions/glean/code-search)
.

# Search chat history - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Glean Chat

Search chat history

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Requirements and availability](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#requirements-and-availability)
    
*   [Searching your chat history](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#searching-your-chat-history)
    
*   [Filtering your chat history](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#filtering-your-chat-history)
    
*   [Troubleshooting steps](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#troubleshooting-steps)
    
*   [Limitations](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#limitations)
    

The chat history search feature lets you search across your own past Glean Assistant chats and agent runs directly from the chat history panel. This helps you revisit earlier threads and pick up from where you left faster. You can search chat threads and agent runs by title.

The current release searches titles only. Message bodies are not searched yet.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#requirements-and-availability)

Requirements and availability
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   No admin setup required: If your organization has Chat History enabled, chat history search works automatically.
*   Supported across deployments: Available for all supported clouds (AWS, Azure, GCP) and with both Glean Key and customer‑managed keys.
*   Your own history only: Chat history search returns threads and runs from your account, respecting permissions and data controls of your organization.

If your organization disables Chat History, or if retention has removed older threads, those conversations will not appear in results.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#searching-your-chat-history)

Searching your chat history
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Open Glean and go to **Glean Chat**.
2.  In the **chat history** panel, locate the search bar.
3.  Type a keyword from the thread title, for example, the topic or the agent name.
4.  Select the matching thread to open it.

**Tips:**

*   Use distinctive words from how the thread title appears in your history.
*   If you ran an agent, try including the agent name in your search term.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#filtering-your-chat-history)

Filtering your chat history
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click filter icon at the top right to view only chats with the Assistant or runs from a specific agent.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#troubleshooting-steps)

Troubleshooting steps
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   I do not see the search bar in chat history
    *   Refresh your browser tab and try again.
    *   Confirm that your organization has **Chat History** enabled.
    *   Check if your thread is outside retention window of your organization.
*   I cannot find a conversation I remember
    *   Try alternate title keywords, for example, the project name or a unique phrase you would expect in the title.
    *   Verify the conversation occurred under your account and not a shared screen or a different login.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/glean-chat/search-past-chats#limitations)

Limitations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   This feature only supports searching across past chat titles. It does not support searching across chat responses.

Configure browser notifications for Glean Chat

[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
