[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#overview)

Overview
---------------------------------------------------------------------

Sales teams use Glean’s MCP server to gather comprehensive account intelligence, prepare for meetings, and stay on top of deal progress without switching between multiple tools.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#prerequisites)

Prerequisites
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Recommended connectors:**

*   Salesforce or HubSpot (for CRM)
*   Slack
*   Gong or Zoom (for call recordings)
*   Gmail or Outlook
*   Confluence or Notion (for internal wikis)
*   Zendesk (for customer health)
*   Google Drive (for proposals and presentations)

**Supported MCP hosts:**

*   Claude Desktop
*   ChatGPT
*   Any MCP-compatible interface

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#use-cases)

Use Cases
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#1-customer-360-/-account-snapshot)

1\. Customer 360 / Account Snapshot

Get a comprehensive view of accounts by pulling together opportunities, tickets, and recent discussions.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Generate an account snapshot for [Company Name] using Glean. 
    
    Include:
    1. All open opportunities and their stages from Salesforce
    2. Recent support tickets from Zendesk
    3. Active discussions in Slack channels (#sales, #customer-success)
    4. Recent emails and meeting notes
    5. Product usage or engagement metrics mentioned anywhere
    
    Highlight any red flags or opportunities.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Use Glean to create a 360-degree view of [account]. What's happening
    across sales, support, and product? What should I know before my call?
    

Copy

    Search Glean for all activity related to [company] in the last 30 days.
    Summarize deal status, health issues, and recent wins.

**What it does:**

*   Aggregates information across CRM, support, and communication tools
*   Identifies risks and opportunities
*   Surfaces recent customer interactions
*   Provides complete context before calls

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#2-research-prospects-and-draft-outreach)

2\. Research Prospects and Draft Outreach

Gather intelligence on prospects and create personalized outreach.

Example Prompt

Copy

    I'm reaching out to [prospect name] at [company].
    Use Glean to:
    1. Search for any past interactions or mentions in our systems
    2. Find similar customers in our portfolio (same industry/size)
    3. Look for relevant case studies or success stories
    4. Check if anyone on our team has connections there
    Draft a personalized intro email highlighting relevant customer wins.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Research [prospect company] using Glean. Have we talked to them before?
    Do we have customers in the same space? Draft an outreach email.
    

Copy

    Use Glean to find case studies and wins relevant to [prospect's industry].
    Create talking points for my first call.

**What it does:**

*   Checks for prior engagement history
*   Finds relevant case studies and references
*   Identifies internal connections
*   Drafts personalized outreach

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#3-deal-preparation-and-call-briefing)

3\. Deal Preparation and Call Briefing

Prepare for customer calls with comprehensive background and talking points.

Example Prompt

Copy

    I have a call with [customer name] tomorrow about [topic].
    
    Use Glean to:
    1. Pull the latest Salesforce opportunity details
    2. Find recent emails and meeting notes with this customer
    3. Check for any open support issues or escalations
    4. Search for internal discussions about this deal in Slack
    5. Locate relevant product documentation or demo assets Create a call prep doc with key context and potential questions.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Prepare me for my customer call. Search Glean for everything about
    [account]: recent activity, deal status, pain points, and decision makers.
    

Copy

    Use Glean to create a briefing for my QBR with [customer]. What's
    their usage, issues, wins, and expansion opportunities?

**What it does:**

*   Aggregates deal context from Salesforce
*   Surfaces recent customer communications
*   Flags open issues or concerns
*   Prepares relevant materials and talking points

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#4-competitive-intelligence)

4\. Competitive Intelligence

Access competitive information from battlecards, calls, and win/loss analysis.

Example Prompt

Copy

    I'm in a competitive deal against [competitor].
    
    Use Glean to:
    1. Find our competitive battlecard for [competitor]
    2. Search Gong calls where we've competed against them
    3. Look for win/loss analysis in Slack or Confluence
    4. Check what features or pricing objections come up Summarize their
    positioning and our best counter-strategies.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for recent deals where we competed against [competitor].
    What made us win? What makes us lose?
    

Copy

    Use Glean to prepare me for a
    competitive situation. Find everything about [competitor]: their strengths,
    weaknesses, and our differentiation.

**What it does:**

*   Retrieves competitive battlecards and analysis
*   Analyzes win/loss patterns
*   Surfaces recent competitive insights from calls
*   Provides proven counter-strategies

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#5-weekly-account-progress-reports)

5\. Weekly Account Progress Reports

Track progress across your book of business and identify what needs attention.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Generate a weekly report on my top accounts using Glean.
    
    For each account:
    1. Summarize deal progress from Salesforce
    2. Note any support escalations or health issues
    3. Highlight completed activities and next steps
    4. Flag accounts that haven't had activity in 7+ days
    Call out red flags and recommend priorities for this week.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Use Glean to create a status report for all my enterprise deals. What
    moved forward? What's stuck? What needs my attention?
    

Copy

    Search Glean for activity across my territory in the last week. Summarize wins, losses,
    and deals needing action.

**What it does:**

*   Aggregates status across multiple accounts
*   Identifies stalled deals
*   Flags at-risk accounts
*   Recommends prioritization

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#6-closed-lost-deal-analysis)

6\. Closed-Lost Deal Analysis

Understand why deals are lost to improve future outcomes.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Opportunity [SFDC ID] just moved to closed-lost.
    
    Use Glean to:
    1. Search Gong call transcripts for objections and concerns
    2. Find Slack discussions about why we lost
    3. Check for competitive mentions
    4. Look for pricing or feature gaps discussed
    Extract insights: Why did we lose? What could we have done differently?
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Analyze this lost deal using Glean. What were the objections? Was it
    price, features, or something else? Find evidence in calls and emails.
    

Copy

    Search Glean for all closed-lost deals to [competitor] this quarter.
    What's the pattern? What are we missing?

**What it does:**

*   Analyzes call transcripts for loss reasons
*   Identifies common objection patterns
*   Surfaces competitive factors
*   Informs strategy improvements

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#7-customer-success-handoff)

7\. Customer Success Handoff

Create comprehensive handoff documentation when deals close.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Deal [opportunity name] just closed. Use Glean to create a handoff doc
    for customer success:
    1. Summarize the sales process and key stakeholders 
    2. List promised features or customizations from calls 
    3. Note pain points and use cases discussed 
    4. Include relevant emails, meeting notes, and demos 
    5. Highlight expectations set during the sales process 
    
    Suggest onboarding priorities and potential expansion areas.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for everything about [new customer] during the sales cycle.
    Create a transition document for the CSM team.
    

Copy

    Use Glean to compile all commitments and expectations for [customer] from sales calls, proposals,
    and contracts.

**What it does:**

*   Synthesizes the entire sales journey
*   Documents commitments and expectations
*   Identifies key stakeholders and pain points
*   Ensures smooth customer success transition

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#8-territory-planning-and-prioritization)

8\. Territory Planning and Prioritization

Analyze your territory to identify the best opportunities.

Example Prompt

Copy

    Help me plan my quarter. Use Glean to analyze my territory:
    
    1. List all accounts with open opportunities in Salesforce
    2. Identify accounts with high engagement but no active deal
    3. Find customers mentioned in expansion discussions
    4. Check for at-risk accounts with recent support escalations
    
    Recommend where I should focus my time for maximum pipeline impact.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for signals indicating expansion opportunities: increased usage,
    new stakeholder engagement, feature requests, budget discussions.
    

Copy

    Use Glean to identify my most engaged accounts that aren't in an active sales
    cycle. Who should I reach out to?

**What it does:**

*   Analyzes account health and engagement
*   Identifies expansion signals
*   Flags at-risk customers
*   Recommends prioritization strategy

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#9-proposal-and-rfp-support)

9\. Proposal and RFP Support

Gather information to respond to proposals and RFPs quickly.

Example Prompt

Copy

    I need to respond to an RFP asking about [specific requirements]. Use Glean to:
    1. Find similar past proposals we've submitted
    2. Locate relevant product documentation and specs
    3. Search for customer references in the same industry
    4. Check if we have compliance certifications they're asking for
    Draft responses to the key technical requirements sections.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean for our standard responses to RFP questions about security,
    compliance, and integration capabilities.
    

Copy

    Use Glean to find case studies, ROI data, and testimonials relevant to
    [prospect's industry] for this proposal.

**What it does:**

*   Locates past proposal content for reuse
*   Finds relevant documentation and specs
*   Identifies appropriate case studies
*   Accelerates response creation

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#10-lead-qualification-and-enrichment)

10\. Lead Qualification and Enrichment

Quickly qualify inbound leads with context from multiple sources.

Example Prompt

Copy

    New lead: [name] from [company]. Use Glean to qualify:
    1. Check if this company is already in Salesforce
    2. Search for any past interactions or demo requests
    3. Look for similar customers in our base (industry/size)
    4. Find mentions of this company in Slack or emails
    Assess fit and recommend whether to prioritize this lead.
Alternative Prompts

Copy

    Search Glean to see if anyone's talked to [company] before. Are they
    already a customer? Have we engaged with them?
    

Copy

    Use Glean to research [inbound lead]. Find relevant talking points, similar
    customers, and our best positioning for their industry.

**What it does:**

*   Checks for existing relationships
*   Identifies similar customers for reference
*   Assesses lead quality and fit
*   Provides context for initial outreach

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#best-practices)

Best Practices
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#keep-crm-data-current)

Keep CRM Data Current

MCP works best when your Salesforce data is up to date:

*   Ensure opportunities have current stages and close dates
*   Tag deals with relevant industries, competitors, use cases
*   Keep contact roles and stakeholder information current

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#use-specific-account-identifiers)

Use Specific Account Identifiers

Copy

    ✅ "Account: Acme Corp (SFDC ID 001...)"
    ✅ "Opportunity: Q4 Enterprise Deal (Opp-12345)"
    ❌ "That big deal I'm working on" (too vague)
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#combine-real-time-with-historical)

Combine Real-Time with Historical

Copy

    What's the current deal status? And what were the key concerns raised in
    our last three calls with this customer?
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#request-actionable-outputs)

Request Actionable Outputs

Copy

    Not just "summarize this account" but "identify the top 3 risks and suggest
    specific actions I should take this week"
    

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#verify-before-sending-externally)

Verify Before Sending Externally

Copy

    Always review Glean's output before sending to customers. Ensure accuracy
    and appropriateness of any competitive or internal information.
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#troubleshooting)

Troubleshooting
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Missing Salesforce data?**

*   Verify Salesforce connector is properly configured
*   Check that necessary fields and objects are being indexed
*   Ensure your user permissions allow access to the data

**Incomplete call intelligence?**

*   Confirm Gong or similar tool is connected
*   Check that call recordings are being transcribed
*   Verify date ranges match when calls occurred

**Can’t find competitive intel?**

*   Check that Confluence or document repositories are indexed
*   Search for specific competitor names or product names
*   Look in both formal battlecards and informal Slack discussions

**Generic account summaries?**

*   Be more specific about what you need (deal status, health, risks)
*   Reference specific time frames (“last 30 days”)
*   Ask for specific types of information (support issues, expansion signals)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/sales#related-resources)

Related Resources
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [MCP Setup Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/mcp/usage)
     - Initial configuration

     - Overview of sales use cases
*   [Chat Best Practices](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/assistant/best-practices)
     - Prompting tips

# Shortcuts - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Home Page

Shortcuts

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)

[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [What Are Shortcuts in Profile Settings?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#what-are-shortcuts-in-profile-settings)
    
*   [How to Enable Shortcuts in Your Profile Settings](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#how-to-enable-shortcuts-in-your-profile-settings)
    
*   [Managing Your Shortcuts](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#managing-your-shortcuts)
    
*   [Adding ‘My Shortcuts’](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#adding-%E2%80%98my-shortcuts%E2%80%99)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#what-are-shortcuts-in-profile-settings)

What Are Shortcuts in Profile Settings?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Shortcuts in profile settings are quick links to websites or pages you visit frequently. They provide easy and fast access to these sites directly from your home page, saving you time and helping you stay organized.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#how-to-enable-shortcuts-in-your-profile-settings)

How to Enable Shortcuts in Your Profile Settings
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Follow these simple steps to enable shortcuts and start managing them in your profile settings:

1.  Access ‘Your Settings’
    *   Navigate to your profile settings by clicking on your profile icon.
2.  Enable Shortcuts
    *   Under **Preferences**, go to the **General** section. Find the option labeled **Show Shortcuts** and toggle it On. This will enable the shortcut feature. ![images](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/pF_0vASXWAL79iHb/images/shortcuts1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=pF_0vASXWAL79iHb&q=85&s=cd8e6bb9face27de0056562fe75cb482)                            

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#managing-your-shortcuts)

Managing Your Shortcuts
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the feature is enabled, managing your shortcuts is easy. Here’s how:

1.  Navigate to the Glean Home Page
    *   Go to the Glean home page, where you’ll now notice a small menu with three dots (…). This is the **Manage Shortcuts** option. ![images](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/pF_0vASXWAL79iHb/images/shortcuts2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=pF_0vASXWAL79iHb&q=85&s=dfadb058ee7d33d392c8fbbf00af8951)                          
2.  Click on these three dots i.e ‘Manage Shortcuts’
    *   When you click on **Manage Shortcuts**, you’ll be presented with two options:
    *   **Top Sites**: This section automatically displays sites you visit most frequently. If enabled, these sites will appear on your home page for quick access.
    *   **My Shortcuts**: Clicking this option will reveal the ‘Add Shortcut’ sub-tab, which will now be highlighted (previously dimmed). This is where you can manually add and customize the sites you want to appear as your preferred shortcuts. ![images](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/pF_0vASXWAL79iHb/images/shortcuts3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=pF_0vASXWAL79iHb&q=85&s=098b1cfcb7ade08e26e2e6f4132716fd)                               

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/shortcuts#adding-%E2%80%98my-shortcuts%E2%80%99)

Adding ‘My Shortcuts’
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To add a new shortcut, follow these simple steps:

1.  Click on the ‘Add Shortcut’ option in the My Shortcuts tab.
2.  A small dialog box will appear asking for the name and URL of the site you want to add. Enter this information and click Add.
3.  Once the shortcut is created, you will receive a confirmation message on the screen. The icon for the site will also appear in your home page’s Shortcuts section.

While both Shortcuts and Go Links serve the purpose of providing quick access, they are distinct features. Shortcuts are designed for personal use, offering quick links to frequently visited sites. In contrast, Go Links are a specialized feature within Glean, intended for creating easy-to-remember URLs for company resources. This makes it easier to navigate internal tools and resources.

# How to use Team Pages - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Teams

How to use Team Pages

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Anatomy of a team page](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#anatomy-of-a-team-page)
    
*   [Team directory:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#team-directory)
    
*   [Teams in search:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#teams-in-search)
    

Glean’s team pages provide you and other employees at your company with a shared space to coordinate your efforts. Each page allows you to share what your team is working on, who’s on that team, and what the best way is to contact you. This way, it is easier for you to work within your team and share information across functions at your organization. At a minimum, Glean recommends creating team pages for all of your company’s departments, but the possibilities for what team pages you have are limitless! Customers use team pages to create workspaces for individual project pods, employee resource groups and social groups for particular geographic areas. Right now, only Glean admins and team moderators can create new team pages, so contact them for information on how to get new teams set up. This guide provides additional information on how to use team pages for teams you are a part of:

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#anatomy-of-a-team-page)

Anatomy of a team page
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Team profile pages contain detailed information about a team. Each page has a set of components, including:

*   Team name
*   What department the team is a part of
*   Team membersWho is on the teamWho leads the team
*   Team profile image
*   Banner image
*   “About” section for describing what the team does (this can include multiple paragraphs of rich text, with hyperlinks, images and more)
*   Contact infoEmail addresses to contact the team (can be a team alias, or any other address)Team Slack channelsURLs (can link to any page, including intranet and external content)

Glean recommends ensuring that your team pages have informative and up-to-date descriptions, and links to relevant contact information. Some ideas for information to put in your team’s description:

*   A description of what your team is responsible for
*   Information about who the correct contacts on your team are for cross-functional work
*   Links to project plans and quarterly goals
*   Information about upcoming events or key milestones

To edit these fields, click on the “edit” button in the upper-right-hand corner of the team page. Your organization may have particular style guides or best practices in place for teams pages — for information on that, contact your Glean admin.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#team-directory)

Team directory:
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For easy discoverability, teams appear in the directory alongside people. Teams can be sorted by relevance, team size, and alphabetically by name. Teams can also be filtered by name. The screenshot below is what part of the teams directory looks like when sorted by relevance for an engineer on the Verticals team that works closely with Product. Clicking on a team links to the team profile page.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages#teams-in-search)

Teams in search:
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When your search indicates you are looking for a team, they will appear prominently in the results. These teams appear as expandable cards and can be expanded to show a subset of up to 6 members. Clicking on a card will take you to the team profile page. Teams will also show up as autocomplete results as you are typing a query. If you click on the team name, you will be taken to the team’s profile page.

YesNo

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#content-area)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Home Page

Mentions

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)

[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Overview](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#overview)
    
*   [Mentions card details](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#mentions-card-details)
    
*   [Supported sources and triggers](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#supported-sources-and-triggers)
    
*   [Behavior and limits](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#behavior-and-limits)
    
*   [Using Mentions](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#using-mentions)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#overview)

Overview
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Keep up with important activity where teammates tag you across your work apps from your **Home** page. The **Mentions** card surfaces recent activity where you were mentioned or assigned, helping you jump back into relevant conversations and documents quickly. Mentions display chronologically and include quick actions to review, copy links, or dismiss items you no longer need. This feature aims to streamline the process of staying updated on relevant discussions and tasks, ensuring that you do not miss out on important mentions across your work platforms.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#mentions-card-details)

_Mentions_ card details
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **A dedicated Mentions tab/card** on Home that is shown by default when you have new mentions. If there are no new mentions, Glean shows your **Suggested** content instead.
*   **A chronological list** with the app icon, title, who mentioned you, and when. Multiple mentions in the same item is consolidated to reduce noise.
*   **Quick actions** to mark all as read, dismiss, and copy a link. If Assistant is enabled in your org, you can also summarize a mention’s content.

![Mentions](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/JHakyUsy_0iTzr-4/images/mentions1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=JHakyUsy_0iTzr-4&q=85&s=c294a77d5b912ceb7b0e120a2c9b586b)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#supported-sources-and-triggers)

Supported sources and triggers
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below are common events that create a mention in Glean. Only content you have permission to access will appear.

*   Jira integration
    *   You will receive notifications in your Mentions Feed when another user tags you in a comment or the body of a Jira issue, even if the issue is not assigned to you.
    *   Notifications are also sent when someone CC’s you in an issue, assigns an issue to you, or comments on an issue that is assigned to you.
    *   The feature covers instances when someone changes or updates an issue assigned to you. However, it does not include follow-up comments on a Jira comment you are tagged in unless you are tagged again.
*   Google Drive integration
    *   In Google Drive, you are notified when someone assigns a comment to you, @mentions you in a comment, or shares a document with you. Similar to Jira, if you are @mentioned in the body of a document, it is not currently within the scope of what triggers a mention notification.
*   Other supported apps
    *   Mentions can also surface from select connected apps. Current coverage includes Confluence, Google Drive, GitHub, and Jira.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#behavior-and-limits)

Behavior and limits
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Time window: Mentions remain visible for up to 10 days (240 hours).
*   List size: Up to 50 mentions are retrieved. The card shows up to 5 by default. You can select **More** to view the rest.
*   Unread vs. dismiss: There is no manual _unread_ state. You can use dismiss to clear items you no longer need. Dismissed mentions are removed from your Mentions queue.
*   Consolidation: Glean can consolidate multiple mentions in the same item and across users to reduce noise.
*   Enabling/disabling: Glean can set these feature functionality to be enabled or disabled by default or it can be self served.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/home-page/mentions#using-mentions)

Using Mentions
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The **Mentions** field on the home page gives you a quick glance of the tagged/mentioned conversations.
*   You can also sort the mentions application-wise by selecting the drop-down field.
*   You can review Mentions and mark them as read as needed in bulk or dismiss the suggestion.

![Using Mentions](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/JHakyUsy_0iTzr-4/images/mentions2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=JHakyUsy_0iTzr-4&q=85&s=81c0bbd548249e51383a2a584009668e)

Search...

Navigation

Document Verification

What Should I Do With Documents That Are Completely Out Of Date?

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)

[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Unsharing a document](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#unsharing-a-document)
    
*   [Deprecating a document](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#deprecating-a-document)
    
*   [How to deprecate a document](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#how-to-deprecate-a-document)
    
*   [What happens after I’ve deprecated a document?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#what-happens-after-i%E2%80%99ve-deprecated-a-document)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#unsharing-a-document)

Unsharing a document
==========================================================================================================================================================================

We get it. Sometimes, documents are just completely out of date and it’s best to remove them entirely. If you have such a document that is still being viewed by your teammates, then there are a few actions to take. If the document is not valuable at all anymore, it’s best to **unshare it or delete it** so that no one else will stumble across it. You will need to navigate to the document itself to **manually unshare or delete the document.** Glean does not have write permissions so you won’t be able to unshare or delete the document from Glean itself.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#deprecating-a-document)

Deprecating a document
==============================================================================================================================================================================

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#how-to-deprecate-a-document)

How to deprecate a document
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If your document is no longer valid but may still have some value, you can mark it as **Deprecated** to act as a warning to your teammates. This is similar to modifying the title of your document to include the words “deprecated”. For example, there might be documentation for an old product design that’s no longer in production, but can serve as a helpful reference for discussions or ideation in the future. To deprecate a document, go to the **Verification > Your content** and click on the **Deprecate** button for a document. You’ll see a confirmation in a modal, and also get an option to unshare the document itself. ![Deprecation Modal](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=10ae890501d16b0e90619f77fd4c45d6)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date#what-happens-after-i%E2%80%99ve-deprecated-a-document)

What happens after I’ve deprecated a document?
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deprecated documents may still appear on Glean search results. However, a deprecated symbol will appear next to the document on the search results page. This status will also be seen from the document itself. ![Deprecated Document in Search Results](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-should-i-do-with-documents-that-are-completely-out-of-date-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=c3a1fd5dbcb1b46e126c0ca357e8b361)

# Setting up Team Pages - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/setting-up-team-pages#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Teams

Setting up Team Pages

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Setup process:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/setting-up-team-pages#setup-process)
    

It can be hard to coordinate efforts between groups of people at your company, and Glean’s teams pages are here to help. These pages provide a single hub for people at your company to learn about who’s working together, and what they’re working on. This help article will guide you through the process of setting up teams; for more information on how to use team pages, see [this article](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/how-to-use-team-pages)
. _Note:_ _Only admins and users with team moderator permissions can set up teams._ Users can access teams pages through search, by clicking on teams in user profiles, and by visiting the team directory. All members of a team will be able to edit that page, including its name, description, profile photo, banner photo, contact information and which members are listed as leads on the team page. Users can be in multiple teams at once. That way, one person can be in a team for their department, the DevOps pod they’re working in and an employee resource group they’re a part of. Glean recommends – at a minimum – setting up teams pages for every department. After that, we see additional value from creating teams that match your company’s internal structure Once you have teams set up in your organization, Glean recommends enlisting individual team leads to update their teams’ contents, including photos, descriptions, and preferred contact methods. (Those leads are welcome to delegate those tasks as necessary, but they have the best context for what to create.) From there, users should begin seeing the teams pages as they use Glean normally.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/people/teams/setting-up-team-pages#setup-process)

Setup process:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To create a new team, go to the team dashboard in Glean’s Directory tab. Click on the “new team” button in the upper right-hand corner, and then follow the prompts to go about creating teams from there. We currently support creating teams from two sources:

*   **Slack**: All public slack channels connected to the Glean instance can be used to create teams where all channel members will be part of the team.
    *   Note: Slackentgrid is not supported as a team source.
*   **People data in Glean**: You can also create teams from eligible fields (e.g. department, role, location, and/or team) in your company’s people data. This data is typically populated from your single sign-on system, such as Google Workspace, Okta or Azure Active Directory.You can use multiple people data fields to create teams, so your company can have pages based on departments, locations and other facets.

While not recommended, Glean also supports manually creating teams through our Push API. For more information on that, contact your Account Manager or Delivery Excellence Manager. Once a team has been created in the teams dashboard, it may take up to an hour to appear in search results for all users. In the meantime, admins can view all teams (including those queued for creation) on the teams dashboard. The teams dashboard supports the following functions:

*   Searching for a team by name
*   Filtering for a team based on department or business unit
*   Ordering teams based on name, data source (e.g Slack, people data in Glean), created from (for Slack this is the channel name, for people fields this is the field name), and last updated (the last time a team page has been edited)Note that all teams being created or deleted will show up first in the dashboard while Glean is processing them.
*   Bulk deletion of teams

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-does-verification-mean#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Document Verification

What Does Verification Mean?

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)
[Security & Architecture](https://docs.glean.com/security/home)
[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [For example](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-does-verification-mean#for-example)
    

**Verification** is your stamp of approval on the accuracy of a document you own. By letting your teammates know your document is verified to be accurate, they can use the information in their work with confidence.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/what-does-verification-mean#for-example)

For example
====================================================================================================================

An engineer can **verify a tech plan**, so other engineers from different teams can input on the latest decisions with any dependencies or considerations. A product manager can **verify the release notes of a feature**, so Sales and Support teams can use that information in conversations with prospective and current customers. A designer can **verify a design proposal**, so engineers know it contains the most up-to-date requirements and designs for a feature they’re building. And many more! Get in touch via the Messenger with any questions or share use cases of your own.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[How Verification Works](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/how-verification-works)
[Which Documents Should I Verify?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify)

⌘I

# Which Documents Should I Verify? - Glean Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify#content-area)

[Glean Help Center home page![light logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/light.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=828d510ca943686b81ff369fb06b7ed0)![dark logo](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/logo/dark.svg?fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=a1901d91185c53325fe9ba60d993b592)](https://docs.glean.com/)

Search...

⌘K

Search...

Navigation

Document Verification

Which Documents Should I Verify?

[Home](https://docs.glean.com/home)
[User Guide](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/about/what-is-glean)
[Agents](https://docs.glean.com/agents/home)
[Get Started for Admins](https://docs.glean.com/get-started/welcome)
[Connectors](https://docs.glean.com/connectors/home)
[Admin Guide](https://docs.glean.com/administration/about)

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.glean.com/troubleshooting/home)
[Release Notes](https://docs.glean.com/release-notes/home)
[Archive](https://docs.glean.com/archive/help-glean/answers/what-are-answers-and-how-do-they-work)

On this page

*   [Which documents should I verify?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify#which-documents-should-i-verify)
    
*   [Respond to verification requests](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify#respond-to-verification-requests)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify#which-documents-should-i-verify)

Which documents should I verify?
=================================================================================================================================================================

A few good questions to ask yourself on any document is:

*   Will someone from another department look at this document for information they need?
*   Will this information be used by Sales or Marketing to create customer material?
*   Will someone on my team waste time if they use this information and it’s no longer accurate? Requirements docs, design docs, FAQs, wiki pages, and debugging instructions are good examples.

If you answer **Yes** to any of these questions, it’s probably a good document to **Verify** and focus on keeping up to date. _Note: this is not a comprehensive guide and you’ll have to use your own intuition on how valuable a document is._ **For example…** An engineer can **verify a tech plan,** so other engineers from different teams can input on the latest decisions with any dependencies or considerations. A product manager can **verify the release notes of a feature**, so Sales and Support teams can use that information in conversations with prospective and current customers. A designer can **verify a design proposal,** so engineers know it contains the most up-to-date requirements and designs for a feature they’re building.

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify#respond-to-verification-requests)

Respond to verification requests
==================================================================================================================================================================

When other teammates **request verification** on a document you own, or if it’s time to re-verify documents based on your own reminders, those documents appear on **Verification > Your tasks.** You can verify or deprecate documents from the list. ![Verification Tasks](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp/images/user-guide/knowledge/verification/which-documents-should-i-verify-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=oQFXWQsCPyCQbpvp&q=85&s=6128bf08865dfdb9e2f655eb5b9de09f)

* * *

Questions? Let us know in the Messenger.

Navigation

Announcements

How Announcements Work

On this page

*   [What are Announcements?](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#what-are-announcements)
    
*   [Create an Announcement](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#create-an-announcement)
    
*   [Customize the banner area](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#customize-the-banner-area)
    
*   [Write your announcement](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#write-your-announcement)
    
*   [Include images](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#include-images)
    
*   [Include a video URL](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#include-a-video-url)
    
*   [Show or hide the announcement author](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#show-or-hide-the-announcement-author)
    
*   [Choose your audience](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#choose-your-audience)

*   [Preview the announcement](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#preview-the-announcement)
    
*   [Publish the announcement](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#publish-the-announcement)
    
*   [How Announcements appear](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#how-announcements-appear)
    
*   [For Admins:](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#for-admins)
    

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#what-are-announcements)

What are Announcements?
=======================================================================================================================================

Announcements are a great way to promote updates on Glean’s home page for your teammates to see, especially if many teammates are using [Glean as their new tab page](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/scio-search/cfpdompphcacgpjfbonkdokgjhgabpij)
. They can be targeted to a limited set of teammates for whatever duration you choose. ![Announcements Overview](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-1.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=70493271bae9e1227060ebb82d9710dd)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#create-an-announcement)

Create an Announcement
======================================================================================================================================

If you have permission to create Announcements for your team, navigate to [Knowledge > Announcements](https://app.askscio.com/knowledge/announcements)
 and click the **“New announcement”** button in the upper right corner. ![Navigate to Announcements](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-2.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=ea93e6cd99090291b40abd328e68bab4) You will see the announcement composer to create your announcement. ![Announcement Composer](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-3.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=2003baa654bfb8d84c26d110448975b9)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#customize-the-banner-area)

Customize the banner area
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Choose a background color, icon, or image. ![Banner Customization 1](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-4.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=e938956253218774827eca70f8a91dca) ![Banner Customization 2](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-5.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=c5c89feb8f05168578012808cc80402d) ![Banner Customization 3](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-6.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=ae95dfaad3a6b58615818ea47bc77e19) ![Banner Customization 4](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-7.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=489bd8b8c70d5b0dfe621b8f1d871479)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#write-your-announcement)

Write your announcement
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Give your announcement a title and use our WYSIWYG editor to easily compose your message. ![Writing Announcement](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-8.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=7e64b6fa8b196e2c387a78789473b99e)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#include-images)

Include images
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can include images to enhance your announcement. Please make sure that your image is less than 1MB in size. After placing your image, you can resize it by clicking it and then dragging the corner handle. ![Including Images](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-9.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=e950ece39cd4f8d253154b29f23b5331)

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#include-a-video-url)

Include a video URL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We currently support Youtube, Vimeo, and Google Drive links. ![Video URL](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/n_UlLNleo8r03xaq/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-10.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=n_UlLNleo8r03xaq&q=85&s=ab4fd33625b126474295c0ccb3919574) ![Video Integration](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-11.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=a4bdbe54b587ea824cb74c78f6252997)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#show-or-hide-the-announcement-author)

Show or hide the announcement author

If you prefer to hide the author name, turn off the _Show author_ toggle. ![Author Settings](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-12.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=44c839fe97852ffacb06a0eccfdbe41f) ![Author Visibility](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-13.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=0f4cd9873a967cd15867412a0a8cf41f)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#choose-your-audience)

Choose your audience

By default, your announcement will be shown to everyone in your organization. If you want to display the announcement to a select group of teammates, choose their _Department_ and _Location_. ![Audience Selection](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-14.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=1c347fbab5739bfbda8af7732b580123)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#schedule-when-it-should-appear-and-disappear-from-your-teammates%E2%80%99-home-pages)

Schedule when it should appear and disappear from your teammates’ home pages

By default, your announcement will be featured on your teammates’ home page for a month. You can change it by specifying the dates in _Scheduling_. ![Scheduling Announcements](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-15.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=5de4893aec8c49460e5d3f5856921693)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#preview-the-announcement)

Preview the announcement

Click on the _More_ button on the top right and select _Preview announcement_. ![Preview Option](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-16.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=3bfb3e8e6b5243b74bb41c74429a040e) ![Preview Announcement](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-17.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=9318975ea9c09c4fe66cd816a73deda8)

### 

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#publish-the-announcement)

Publish the announcement

Select the _Publish_ button, and your announcement will appear on your teammates’ home pages! ![Publish Announcement](https://mintcdn.com/glean-5786559b/uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU/images/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work-18.png?w=2500&fit=max&auto=format&n=uSHOVvwKjGVdBPeU&q=85&s=618351c4a3a521b1892b1d68b5a7d6f2)

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#how-announcements-appear)

How Announcements appear
==========================================================================================================================================

If there are announcements visible to you, you’ll see an Announcements section on the Glean home page (or new tab page). You can click on the announcement itself to read in more detail, or hide it if irrelevant.

* * *

[​](https://docs.glean.com/user-guide/knowledge/announcements/how-announcements-work#for-admins)

For Admins:
===============================================================================================================

Want to allow users to push announcements from Slack? Follow the steps [here](https://docs.glean.com/administration/knowledge/announcements/announcements-slack)
.
